2026-04-24T08:41:39.5792568Z     # Lamb Shank × Braise — Recipe Temperature Research
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5792895Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5793528Z     ## Tree position
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5793812Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5793978Z     | Field | Value |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5794167Z     |-------|-------|
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5794610Z     | Category | `lamb` |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5794880Z     | Cut type folder | `roasts/` |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5795154Z     | Cut slug | `lamb_shank` |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5795414Z     | Method slug | `braise` |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5795686Z     | File | `lamb_shank-braise.md` |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5796093Z     | Full path | `docs/recipe_research/lamb/roasts/lamb_shank-braise.md` |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5796491Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5796772Z     ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5797001Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5797551Z     The lamb shank is the lower portion of the leg — the section below the knee joint on either
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5798249Z     the fore (front) or hind (back) leg. It contains a central bone surrounded by a dense network
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5798935Z     of collagen-rich connective tissue, which transforms into gelatin during prolonged moist-heat
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5799798Z     cooking and gives braised lamb shank its characteristic sticky, unctuous sauce and
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5800440Z     fall-off-the-bone texture. The shank has relatively little intramuscular fat compared with
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5801075Z     the shoulder but abundant surface and inter-muscular fat that melts into the braising liquid.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5801689Z     Because of the high collagen content, the shank is uniquely unsuited to dry-heat methods
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5802290Z     — it must reach 85–95 °C internal temperature and be held there for 2–4 hours for collagen
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5802845Z     conversion to complete. Any reading below 80 °C will leave the meat tough regardless of cooking
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5803336Z     time. This makes the MEATER probe essential for monitoring approach to the braising window;
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5803822Z     the pull temperature is the **minimum** at which the meat has rendered sufficiently, not a
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5804558Z     precision target. The shank is universal across Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, Northern European,
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5805069Z     Central Asian, and Indian culinary traditions. Braising liquid varies from red or white wine
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5805574Z     (European), beer (Belgian/Scandinavian), cider (Norman/Swedish), saffron-tomato (Persian/Moroccan),
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5805998Z     to spiced yoghurt or lentil-thickened broth (Indian/Turkish).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5806268Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5806441Z     ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5806625Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5806902Z     ### 1. BBC Good Food — Braised Lamb Shanks (British tradition)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5807256Z     **Source**: BBC Good Food, *bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/braised-lamb-shanks*
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5807587Z     (fetched 2026-04-24, author: Good Food magazine)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5807962Z     **Serves**: 8 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Rest**: 5 min
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5808238Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5808405Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5808590Z     - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5808772Z     - 2 tbsp olive oil
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5808978Z     - 1 onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5809199Z     - 2 carrots, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5809422Z     - Few sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5809638Z     - 3 fresh bay leaves
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5809842Z     - 4 garlic cloves, left whole
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5810056Z     - 2 tbsp plain flour
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5810283Z     - 1 tbsp tomato purée
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5810480Z     - 350 ml white wine
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5810680Z     - 500 ml lamb or chicken stock
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5810885Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5811045Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5811391Z     1. Heat oven to 200 °C / fan 180 °C / gas 6. Pour the oil into a casserole dish or roasting
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5811996Z        tray large enough to fit all the shanks. Over high heat on the hob, spend a good 10 minutes
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5812359Z        browning the lamb all over.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5812696Z     2. Remove the lamb, add the onion and carrot and cook for 10 minutes until starting to brown.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5813142Z        Stir in the herbs and garlic and cook for a few minutes more. Stir in the flour and tomato
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5813577Z        purée, season well, then pour over the wine and stock.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5813892Z     3. Return the lamb shanks to the pan. Bring to a simmer.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5814450Z     4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part of the shank, keeping the tip
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5814806Z        away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5815219Z     5. Cover with a lid or tightly with foil and cook in the oven, undisturbed, for 1½–2 hrs until
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5815555Z        lamb is tender.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5815862Z     6. Remove the lamb from the sauce and set aside. Put the pan back on the hob and bubble the
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5816272Z        sauce for a few minutes until thickened to a gravy consistency.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5816820Z     7. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F)** — at this temperature the collagen has converted to
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5817305Z        gelatin and the meat separates easily from the bone. The window is wide; holding at 90 °C
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5817822Z        for 30 minutes produces more tender results than hitting 90 °C and immediately pulling.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5818332Z     8. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (braised — collagen fully rendered,
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5818676Z        meat falling from bone).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5818881Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5819039Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5819198Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5819508Z     ### 2. Nigella Lawson — Aromatic Lamb Shank Stew (British / Middle Eastern fusion)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5819980Z     **Source**: Nigella Lawson, *Nigella Bites* (Chatto & Windus / Hyperion, 2001);
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5820422Z     nigella.com/recipes/aromatic-lamb-shank-stew (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5820811Z     **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 1½–2 hrs
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5821048Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5821212Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5821416Z     - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5821590Z     - 2 onions
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5821766Z     - 4 cloves garlic
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5821958Z     - 1 sprinkling of salt
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5822158Z     - 1 tbsp turmeric
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5822345Z     - 1 tsp ground ginger
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5822578Z     - ¼ tsp dried chilli flakes
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5822795Z     - 2 tsp ground cinnamon
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5823031Z     - ¼ tsp freshly grated nutmeg
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5823246Z     - Black pepper
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5823426Z     - 3 tbsp honey
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5823598Z     - 1 tbsp soy sauce
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5823785Z     - 3 tbsp Marsala
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5824005Z     - 6 tbsp (⅓ cup) red lentils
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5824369Z     - 6 tbsp vegetable or groundnut oil
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5824655Z     - 3 tbsp chopped pistachios or blanched almonds, to serve
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5824910Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5825067Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5825379Z     1. Put 3 tablespoons of the oil into a very large, wide, heavy-bottomed pan and warm over medium
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5825976Z        heat. Brown the lamb shanks in batches and then remove to a roasting tin to rest temporarily.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5826513Z     2. Peel the onions and garlic and process in a food processor or chop finely by hand. Add the
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5826979Z        remaining oil to the pan, and fry the onion-garlic mush until soft, sprinkling salt over to
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5827571Z        stop it catching. Stir in the turmeric, ground ginger, chilli, cinnamon and nutmeg, and
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5828141Z        season with freshly ground pepper.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5828534Z     3. Stir again, adding the honey, soy sauce and Marsala.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5829290Z     4. Put the shanks back in the pan. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5829849Z        of the largest shank, tip clear of the bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5830216Z     5. Add cold water almost to cover. Bring to the boil, then put a lid on the pan, lower the heat
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5830720Z        and simmer very gently for 1–1½ hours or until the meat is tender.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5831125Z     6. Add the red lentils and cook for about 20 minutes longer without the lid, until the lentils
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5831563Z        have softened into the sauce and the juices have reduced and thickened slightly.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5832056Z     7. **Pull at 90–95 °C (194–203 °F).** The lentil-thickened sauce signals doneness as much as
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5832500Z        the temperature; at 90 °C the meat should pull freely from the bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5832879Z     8. Toast the nuts in a dry frying pan; sprinkle onto the lamb as you serve it.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5833362Z     9. Final serving temperature: **90–95 °C / 194–203 °F** (fully braised, fall-off-bone).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5833843Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5834006Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5834165Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5834725Z     ### 3. Suzy Karadsheh — Mediterranean Braised Lamb Shanks (Mediterranean / Egyptian tradition)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5835126Z     **Source**: Suzy Karadsheh, *The Mediterranean Dish*;
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5835469Z     themediterraneandish.com/braised-lamb-shanks/ (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5835912Z     **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2½ hrs · **Oven**: 175 °C (350 °F)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5836191Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5836363Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5836548Z     - 6 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5836763Z     - 2¼ tsp garlic powder
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5836983Z     - 1 tsp sweet Spanish paprika
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5837198Z     - 1 tsp kosher salt
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5837405Z     - 1 tsp freshly ground black pepper
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5837679Z     - ¾ tsp ground nutmeg
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5837898Z     - 2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5838139Z     - 1 medium yellow onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5838380Z     - 2 celery ribs, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5838638Z     - 3 large carrots, peeled and cut into large pieces
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5838900Z     - 450 g (1 lb) baby potatoes
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5839107Z     - 2 cups red wine
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5839297Z     - 3 cups beef broth
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5839498Z     - 1 can (800 g / 28 oz) peeled tomatoes
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5839726Z     - 2 cinnamon sticks
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5839915Z     - 4 sprigs fresh thyme
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5840119Z     - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5840315Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5840475Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5840838Z     1. Preheat oven to 175 °C (350 °F). Mix garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper and nutmeg. Pat
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5841234Z        the lamb shanks dry and season all over with the spice mix.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5841637Z     2. In a large Dutch oven over medium-high, heat 2 tbsp olive oil. Working in batches, brown
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5842088Z        the shanks until golden on all sides (about 8 minutes). Transfer to a large tray.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5842529Z     3. Pour off excess fat and return pot to medium-high heat. Add onions, celery, carrots and
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5842995Z        potatoes. Sauté until the vegetables gain some colour, 5–7 minutes.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5843402Z     4. Add red wine and scrape up the brown bits from the bottom. Cook briefly until wine reduces
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5843850Z        slightly, then add beef broth, peeled tomatoes, cinnamon sticks, thyme and rosemary.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5844468Z        Sprinkle salt and pepper. Return lamb shanks to the pot; press down to submerge. Boil
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5844827Z        10 minutes, then turn off the heat.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5845305Z     5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, keeping the tip away from the
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5845656Z        central bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5846038Z     6. Cover the pot and transfer to the hot oven for 2½ hours. Check periodically; add more liquid
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5846376Z        if needed.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5846727Z     7. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The cooking liquid will have reduced to about one-third
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5847156Z        of the original volume. The meat should pull away from the bone with light tong pressure.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5847587Z     8. Serve on Lebanese rice, polenta or orzo with the vegetables and braising sauce.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5848090Z     9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (Mediterranean braise, fall-off-bone).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5848412Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5848574Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5848731Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5849127Z     ### 4. Matprat.no — Hvitvinbraiserte lammeskanker / White-Wine Braised Lamb Shanks (Norwegian tradition)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5849751Z     **Source**: Matprat.no (Norwegian Meat and Poultry Research Centre / Opplysningskontoret for egg og kjøtt);
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5850356Z     matprat.no/oppskrifter/gjester/hvitvinsbraiserte-lammeskanker/ (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5850789Z     **Serves**: 4 · **Cook**: 3½–4 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5851026Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5851189Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5851373Z     - 4 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5851546Z     - 1 tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5851748Z     - ½ tsp pepper
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5851929Z     - 2 cloves garlic
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5852157Z     - 1 red or yellow capsicum (paprika), cut into sticks
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5852405Z     - 3 green chillies
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5852603Z     - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5852874Z     - 2 tbsp rosé-pepper / pink peppercorns
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5853110Z     - 750 ml (7.5 dl) white wine
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5853341Z     - Salt and pepper (additional, to taste)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5853596Z     - 8 green asparagus spears (to serve)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5853842Z     - 4 sprigs fresh basil (to garnish)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5854052Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5854368Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5854648Z     1. Rub the lamb shanks with salt and pepper. Place in an ovenproof form.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5855059Z     2. Peel the garlic and cut the capsicum into sticks. Add the garlic, capsicum, chilli and
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5855576Z        rosemary to the form alongside the shanks. Sprinkle with rosé pepper and pour over the
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5855903Z        white wine.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5856263Z     3. Cover tightly with aluminium foil and cook in the oven at 150 °C for 3½–4 hours, or until
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5856608Z        the meat loosens from the bones.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5856934Z     4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5857380Z     5. Optional: remove the foil for the last few minutes and raise oven to high grill (overvarme)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5857737Z        to add colour to the shanks.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5858093Z     6. Remove the capsicum and chilli pieces from the cooking juices. Strain the juices; return
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5858542Z        capsicum and chilli. Use a hand blender to create a full-bodied sauce. Season to taste.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5859110Z     7. Peel the asparagus; cook in lightly salted water for 3 minutes, until just tender.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5859610Z     8. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F).** Norwegian culinary tradition cooks lamb shanks until
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5860331Z        they fall from the bone; the low oven temperature (150 °C) extends the collagen-conversion
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5860905Z        window and produces an exceptionally silky sauce.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5861461Z     9. Pour sauce into deep plates; place one lamb shank per plate. Garnish with basil and the
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5861986Z        lightly cooked green asparagus.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5862760Z     10. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian style).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5863270Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5863489Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5863681Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5864068Z     ### 5. Kasimir (Chefkoch.de) — Geschmorte Lammhaxen / Red-Wine Braised Lamb Shanks (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5864753Z     **Source**: Kasimir, Chefkoch.de community recipe;
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5865305Z     chefkoch.de/rezepte/566811155129665/Geschmorte-Lammhaxen.html (fetched 2026-04-24;
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5865806Z     4.8/5 stars from 55 ratings)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5866167Z     **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 45 min (+overnight marination) · **Cook**: 2½ hrs
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5866447Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5866612Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5866805Z     - 4 lamb shanks (Lammhaxen)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5867047Z     - 2 carrots (Möhren)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5867286Z     - 1 large onion (Gemüsezwiebel)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5867513Z     - 1 garlic clove (Knoblauchzehe)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5867767Z     - Tomato paste (Tomatenmark), a little
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5868146Z     - 1 glass lamb stock (Lammfond)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5868382Z     - 200 g sieved tomatoes (passierte Tomaten)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5868617Z     - 250 ml red wine (Rotwein)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5868818Z     - Olive oil
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5869009Z     - A splash balsamic vinegar (Balsamico)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5869266Z     - 1 bunch flat-leaf parsley (glatte Petersilie)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5869513Z     - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5869706Z     - Thyme, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5869913Z     - Rosemary, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5870164Z     - Cayenne pepper or Pul Biber (Turkish chilli flakes)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5870432Z     - 1 knife-tip ground cinnamon (Zimt)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5870720Z     - 1 knife-tip ground cumin (Kreuzkümmel)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5870942Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5871100Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5871408Z     1. The day before: trim the shanks of excess fat and sinews. Rub all over with thyme, rosemary,
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5871943Z        olive oil, salt and pepper. Refrigerate overnight — the dry-rub penetrates the meat and
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5872312Z        improves both flavour and crust formation.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5872668Z     2. The next day: brown the shanks all over in olive oil in a heavy braising pot. Remove and
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5873166Z        set aside. Dice the onion, garlic and carrots; sauté in the same pot until beginning to
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5873540Z        colour. Stir in the tomato paste and cook briefly.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5873919Z     3. Deglaze with red wine and reduce slightly. Add the sieved tomatoes and half the lamb stock.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5874652Z        Season with a knife-tip each of cumin and cinnamon — these must be barely perceptible; if
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5875030Z        they taste of spice the sauce is over-spiced.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5875468Z     4. Return the shanks to the pot, add a lid and braise over low heat for approximately 2½ hours.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5875849Z        Add more lamb stock if needed during cooking.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5876220Z     5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5876684Z     6. After cooking, remove the shanks and rest on a plate. Taste the sauce; adjust with cayenne
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5877151Z        or Pul Biber for heat, and a splash of balsamic vinegar to round. No additional thickening
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5877597Z        is needed — the reduced braising liquid is naturally concentrated.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5877944Z     7. Stir in freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley before serving.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5878393Z     8. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The German tradition uses no additional binding — the
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5885783Z        collagen in the shanks provides sufficient body. The author notes that serving with couscous
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5886279Z        and salad is traditional in his household.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5886917Z     9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (fully braised, German style).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5887272Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5887443Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5887608Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5887972Z     ### 6. Tasteline.com — Ugnsstekt lammlägg / Oven-braised Lamb Shank (Swedish tradition)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5888495Z     **Source**: *Det moderna köket* (The Modern Kitchen), recipe published on Tasteline.com;
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5888949Z     tasteline.com/recept/ugnsstekt-lammlagg/ (fetched 2026-04-24; 3.3/5 stars, 300 votes)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5889519Z     **Serves**: 7 · **Prep**: 15 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5889929Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5890161Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5890489Z     - 7 lamb shanks (lammlägg)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5890896Z     - 750 ml (7.5 dl) meat stock (köttbuljong)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5891267Z     - 250 ml (2.5 dl) red wine (rött vin)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5891502Z     - 6 bay leaves (lagerblad)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5891775Z     - 4 garlic cloves (vitlöksklyftor)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5892292Z     - 8 small onions (smålökar)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5892607Z     - 2 sprigs rosemary (rosmarin)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5892935Z     - 3 sprigs marjoram (mejram)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5893233Z     - 1 tbsp peppercorns (pepparkorn)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5893454Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5893614Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5893922Z     1. Set a frying pan on high heat. Add the lamb shanks and sear for approximately 2 minutes per
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5894652Z        side, or until well browned. No oil is specified — the shanks render their own fat in a hot
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5894988Z        dry pan.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5895287Z     2. Transfer the browned shanks to a roasting tin. Add the stock, wine, bay leaves, garlic,
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5895696Z        small whole onions, rosemary, marjoram and peppercorns to the tin.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5896122Z     3. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the largest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5896654Z     4. Cover the tin tightly with foil and oven-braise at 150 °C for approximately 2 hours, or
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5897054Z        until the meat is very tender (mycket mört).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5897457Z     5. **Pull at 88–90 °C (190–194 °F).** Swedish home-cooking tradition aims for complete
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5897961Z        tenderness rather than a specific pull temperature; the 2-hour window at 150 °C is designed
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5898314Z        to achieve this reliably.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5898633Z     6. Serve the shanks in deep bowls with polenta or potato-and-garlic mash (potatis- och
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5898977Z        vitlöksmos).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5899323Z     7. Final serving temperature: **88–90 °C / 190–194 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian home
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5899706Z        cooking style, from *Det moderna köket*).
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5899935Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5900093Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5900252Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5900433Z     ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5900647Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5900829Z     | Source | Pull temperature | Method |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5901071Z     |--------|----------------|--------|
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5901429Z     | BBC Good Food (UK) | 88–92 °C | Oven braise, white wine, 1½–2 hrs at 200 °C |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5901837Z     | Nigella Lawson (UK) | 90–95 °C | Stovetop simmer, aromatic broth |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5902256Z     | The Mediterranean Dish (Egypt/US) | 88–93 °C | Dutch oven, 2½ hrs at 175 °C |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5902711Z     | Matprat.no (Norway) | 88–92 °C | Foil-covered form, white wine, 3½–4 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5903162Z     | Chefkoch.de / Kasimir (Germany) | 88–93 °C | Covered braise, red wine, 2½ hrs |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5903710Z     | Tasteline.com / Det moderna köket (Sweden) | 88–90 °C | Covered roasting tin, red wine + stock, 2 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5904081Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5904767Z     **Consensus pull**: **88–95 °C (190–203 °F)**. The braise window is wide — collagen begins
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5905278Z     converting to gelatin at around 72–74 °C but full texture transformation requires sustained
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5905771Z     time above 80 °C. All six traditions agree on a minimum of approximately 88 °C. Higher
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5906286Z     temperatures (up to 95 °C) are acceptable and produce softer, more yielding meat. Overshooting
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5906744Z     to 100 °C risks drying out the outer muscle fibres in longer braises.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5907019Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5907342Z     **Representative coding value**: **90 °C (194 °F)** as the nominal pull, with a practical
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5907687Z     range of 88–95 °C.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5907870Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5908039Z     ## Notes for cooking_data.py
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5908238Z     
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5908407Z     - `doneness`: `braised_tender`
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5908627Z     - `pull_temp_c`: 90
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5908849Z     - `pull_temp_range_c`: [88, 95]
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5909263Z     - `safety_level`: `safe` (well above all pathogen destruction thresholds)
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5909692Z     - Collagen conversion is time-dependent as well as temperature-dependent. The MEATER probe
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5910150Z       temperature reading marks entry into the braising window; actual tenderness depends on
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5910670Z       the duration at that temperature. Recommend a minimum hold time of 30–45 minutes after
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5911022Z       reaching 88 °C.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5911320Z     - All six traditions sear the shanks before braising for Maillard flavour development.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5911766Z     - All six use covered cooking vessels (lid, foil) to maintain steam and prevent evaporation.
2026-04-24T08:41:39.5912122Z     <exited with exit code 0>

Use a preheated grill to medium-high heat. \n- **Forming Patties:** Shape patties gently, avoid pressing down on them as they cook to keep them juicy. If desired, add a little fat (like butter or cheese) into the center of each patty for more juiciness.\n- **Seasoning:** Use salt, pepper, and any other seasonings you enjoy.\n- **Grilling Time:** Grill about 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, given the lean nature of bison.\n- **Internal Temperature:** The ideal internal temperature for a bison burger is **135°F (57°C) for medium-rare** and **up to 145°F (63°C) for medium**. Because bison is so lean, it’s best not to cook it past medium, or it may become dry【3:4†source】.\n\n**Recipe Summary:**\n\n1. **Ingredients:** Bison mince, salt, pepper, (optionally, onion, garlic, or herbs).\n2. **Shape:** Form gently into patties, about 2 cm thick.\n3. **Grill:** Preheat grill to medium-high.\n4. **Cook:** Grill 3-4 minutes per side, turning once.\n5. **Check temp:** Use a meat thermometer; aim for 135–145°F (57–63°C).\n6. **Rest:** Let rest a couple of minutes before serving for juicier burgers.\n\nFor more hints and tips around burger perfection (including shaping, seasoning, and getting a juicy finish), BBC Good Food’s \"10 tips for perfect burgers\" is a useful reference—even though it mostly discusses beef, the tips apply well to bison【3:4†source】.\n\n**Serving Suggestion:**\nLoad your bison burger with your choice of toppings (lettuce, tomato, cheese, pickles, etc.) and a bun of your preference.\n\n---\n*Note: RecipeTin Eats and BBC Good Food have many burger recipes, but none specifically listed a bison burger by name; however, grilling instructions and tips for lean meats like bison follow the same fundamental best practices described above.*","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":1001,"end_index":1013,"url_citation":{"title":"10 tips for perfect burgers - BBC Good Food","url":"https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/howto/guide/how-make-burgers"}},{"text":"","start_index":1674,"end_index":1686,"url_citation":{"title":"10 tips for perfect burgers - BBC Good Food","url":"https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/howto/guide/how-make-burgers"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"bison burger recipe grill internal temperature site:bbcgoodfood.com OR site:recipetineats.com OR site:chefkoch.de OR site:cookpad.com","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=bison+burger+recipe+grill+internal+temperature+site%3Abbcgoodfood.com+OR+site%3Arecipetineats.com+OR+site%3Achefkoch.de+OR+site%3Acookpad.com"}],"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:42:50.7574414Z   result: {"type":"output_text","text":{"value":"Auf Chefkoch.de gibt es mehrere Rezepte und Grillideen für Bisonsteak. Zwar handelt es sich nicht immer um klassische Rezepte direkt vom Grill, doch einige Methoden eignen sich auch für den Grill oder kombinieren das scharfe Anbraten und sanfte Nachgaren (z. B. bei 80 Grad im Ofen – das geht auch indirekt auf dem Grill mit Deckel).\n\nHier ein paar Beispiele und Tipps, die du sofort finden kannst:\n\n1. **Bisonsteak mit Salicorne (Meeresspargel), 80 Grad Methode**\n   - Steaks werden scharf angebraten (geht auch auf dem Grill), dann bei niedriger Temperatur fertig gegart.\n   - Mit Wildkräutern und Gemüse serviert.\n   - Rezept: [Bisonsteak mit Salicorne](https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2940141444058172/Bisonsteak-mit-Salicorne.html)【3:7†source】\n\n2. **Bisonsteak mit Büffelmozzarella auf Wildpilzgemüse**\n   - Steaks scharf anbraten und auf dem Grill oder im Ofen sanft fertig garen.\n   - Mit Mozzarella belegen, dazu Pilze in Wildfond.\n   - Rezept: [Bisonsteak mit Büffelmozzarella](https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2841461426272208/Bisonsteak-mit-Bueffelmozzarella-auf-Wildpilzgemuese.html)【3:1†source】\n\n3. **Bisonsteak auf Paprika (80 Grad Methode)**\n   - Steaks anbraten und dann auf Paprikagemüse auf 80 Grad nachziehen lassen (passend fürs indirekte Grillen).\n   - Rezept: [Bisonsteak auf Paprika](https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2955311447318875/Bisonsteak-auf-Paprika.html)【3:3†source】\n\n4. **Bisonsteak in Rosmarin (ebenfalls 80 Grad)**\n   - Klassische Entrecôte-Zubereitung, nach dem Anbraten in Rosmarinöl bei niedriger Temperatur garziehen.\n   - Rezept: [Bisonsteak in Rosmarin](https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2940491444048937/Bisonsteak-in-Rosmarin.html)【3:5†source】\n\nIn vielen dieser Rezepte wird das Steak zunächst bei hoher Hitze (am besten auf dem Grill) angebraten, dann sanft bei etwa 80 °C fertiggegart. Das kannst du auf dem Gas- oder Kugelgrill gut umsetzen. Innen bleibt das Steak damit besonders zart.\n\n**Tipp:** Marinaden und Beilagen zu Steaks findest du ebenfalls in großer Auswahl auf Chefkoch.de, viele davon sind speziell fürs Grillen konzipiert【3:2†source】.\n\nGuten Appetit und viel Erfolg beim Grillen!","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":736,"end_index":748,"url_citation":{"title":"Bisonsteak mit Salicorne von Klaumix - Chefkoch","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2812381433245208/Bisonsteak-mit-Salicorne.html"}},{"text":"","start_index":1094,"end_index":1106,"url_citation":{"title":"Bisonsteak mit Büffelmozzarella auf Wildpilzgemüse","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2619231411385428/Bisonsteak-mit-Bueffelmozzarella-auf-Wildpilzgemuese.html"}},{"text":"","start_index":1383,"end_index":1395,"url_citation":{"title":"Bisonsteak auf Paprika von Klaumix - Chefkoch","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2841491436191027/Bisonsteak-auf-Paprika.html"}},{"text":"","start_index":1669,"end_index":1681,"url_citation":{"title":"Bisonsteak in Rosmarin von Klaumix - Chefkoch","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2841471436190764/Bisonsteak-in-Rosmarin.html"}},{"text":"","start_index":2077,"end_index":2089,"url_citation":{"title":"Steaks Grill Rezepte | Chefkoch","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rs/s0/steaks+grill/Rezepte.html"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"Bisonsteak grill site:chefkoch.de","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=Bisonsteak+grill+site%3Achefkoch.de"}],"annotations":null}

Here’s a delicious **bison burger recipe** for the grill, including ingredients, method, and temperature guidance.\n\n---\n\n## **Bison Burger Recipe (Grill)**\n\n### **Ingredients**\n- 1 lb ground bison\n- 1 tsp kosher salt\n- ½ tsp black pepper\n- 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce (optional)\n- 1 tsp garlic powder (optional)\n- 4 burger buns\n- Cheese slices (like cheddar, optional)\n- Toppings: lettuce, tomato, onion, pickles, ketchup, mustard, mayonnaise (as desired)\n- Olive oil (for brushing the grill)\n\n---\n\n### **Method**\n\n1. **Preparation:**\n   - In a bowl, gently mix ground bison with salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, and garlic powder if using. Don’t over-mix.\n   - Form into 4 equal patties, about ¾ inch thick. Make a small indentation in the center of each patty with your thumb to help them cook evenly.\n\n2. **Preheat the Grill:**\n   - Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (about 450°F / 230°C). Clean and oil the grates.\n\n3. **Grill the Patties:**\n   - Brush patties lightly with olive oil.\n   - Place burgers on the grill and cook for about 4–5 minutes per side **(internal temperature should reach 140–145°F / 60–63°C for medium-rare to medium)**. Flip once.\n   - **Important:** Bison is lean. Do not overcook as it can become dry.\n\n4. **Add Cheese:**\n   - If using cheese, add a slice in the last minute of grilling and close the grill lid to melt.\n\n5. **Toast Buns:**\n   - Optional: Toast the burger buns, cut side down, for about 30 seconds or until golden.\n\n6. **Assemble:**\n   - Place a bison patty on each bun bottom. Add desired toppings. Put on the bun tops.\n\n---\n\n### **Grill Tips**\n- Bison is leaner than beef, so avoid pressing down on the patties.\n- Use a meat thermometer to ensure proper doneness.\n- For safety, the USDA recommends cooking ground bison to an internal temp of 160°F (71°C), but many enjoy it at medium (140–145°F / 60–63°C).\n\n---\n\n**Enjoy your juicy bison burgers!**","annotations":null},"bing_searches":null,"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:43:13.9683014Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9683173Z     
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9683325Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9683490Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9684147Z         Tip the beef mince, shallot, horseradish sauce, herbs and garlic into a bowl, and season well with salt and black pepper. Combine everything well using your hands, squeezing the mixture through your fingers repeatedly to help it bind together.
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9684972Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9685135Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9685306Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9686303Z         Divide the mixture into four portions and shape each into a 3cm-thick patty. Press a small 50p-sized piece of blue cheese into the middle of each patty, if using, and pack the meat around it to enclose the cheese – this will melt during cooking.
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9687112Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9687275Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9687445Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9688076Z         Put the burgers on a plate, cover and chill for at least 30 mins or overnight. Light the [barbecue](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/review/best-charcoal-barbecues) and wait for the coals to turn ashen. Or, heat a gas barbecue to medium.
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9688723Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9688878Z     *   ### step 4
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9689042Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9690053Z         Lightly oil the burger patties all over and cook for 5 mins until just starting to char. Carefully flip over and cook for a further 4-5 mins (for medium) or longer if you prefer well done. Remove the burgers to a board and leave to rest for a few minutes before serving. Alternatively, fry the burgers over a medium heat for 4-5 mins on each side until cooked through (a probe thermometer should read 70C).
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9691128Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9691287Z     *   ### step 5
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9691456Z         
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9691842Z         Serve the burgers in the buns with lettuce, tomato slices and relish (see recipe, below), or any other accompaniments of your choice.
2026-04-24T08:43:13.9692276Z

2026-04-24T08:43:18.5657142Z   result: {"type":"output_text","text":{"value":"Here’s a complete **grilled bison burger recipe**, including recommended **internal temperature**, ingredients, and step-by-step instructions:\n\n---\n\n### Ingredients\n- 1 lb ground bison\n- 1 tsp salt\n- ½ tsp black pepper\n- ½ tsp garlic powder (optional)\n- ½ tsp onion powder (optional)\n- 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce (optional)\n- 4 hamburger buns\n- Your choice of toppings: lettuce, tomato, red onion, pickles, cheese, ketchup, mustard, etc.\n\n---\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Preheat Grill:**  \n   Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (about 400°F/204°C). Clean and oil the grates.\n\n2. **Mix Patties:**  \n   In a mixing bowl, gently combine the ground bison with salt, pepper, and any optional seasonings. Do not overmix, as bison is lean and can dry out.\n\n3. **Form Patties:**  \n   Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions and gently form into patties about ¾-inch thick. Press a thumb indent in the center of each patty to prevent puffing.\n\n4. **Season:**  \n   Sprinkle a little extra salt and pepper on both sides of each patty.\n\n5. **Grill Burgers:**  \n   Place patties on the hot grill. Grill for about 4–5 minutes per side, turning only once.  \n   **Internal Temperature:** Cook bison burgers to **140–145°F (60–63°C)** for medium-rare, or up to 160°F (71°C) for medium. Insert a meat thermometer sideways into the center to check.\n\n6. **Add Cheese (Optional):**  \n   If using cheese, add slices during the last 1–2 minutes of grilling and close the lid to melt.\n\n7. **Rest Patties:**  \n   Remove burgers from the grill and **let rest for 3–5 minutes** to allow juices to redistribute.\n\n8. **Toast Buns (Optional):**  \n   Place buns cut side down on the grill for 1 minute until lightly toasted.\n\n9. **Assemble Burgers:**  \n   Add patties to buns and top with your favorite vegetables, sauces, and condiments.\n\n---\n\n### Tips\n- **Don’t overcook:** Bison is leaner than beef; medium-rare to medium yields the juiciest results.\n- **Do not press down on the patties while grilling**—it squeezes out juices.\n\n---\n\nEnjoy your delicious bison burger!","annotations":null},"bing_searches":null,"annotations":null}

Die Bisonpatties im Kühlschrank auftauen und ca. 1 Stunde vor der Zubereitung aus dem Kühlschrank nehmen.\n2. Die Brötchenhälften goldbraun toasten, die untere mit rote-Bete-Dip und Gurken bestreichen, die obere mit Currysauce.\n3. Bacon knusprig braten. Das Ei als Spiegelei (am besten mit flüssigem Eigelb) zubereiten.\n4. Die Portweinschalotten (oder die Zwiebeln) erwärmen.\n5. Das Patty auf dem Grill (oder in einer Gusseisenpfanne) auf einer Seite mit Meersalz bestreuen und ca. 3 Minuten grillen, wenden, mit Käse belegen und weitere 3 Minuten grillen. Danach 4 Minuten ruhen lassen.\n6. Burger schichten: Patty auf das Brötchen mit den Gurken, darauf die Zwiebeln, Bacon und Spiegelei. Mit der anderen Brötchenhälfte bedecken und bei Bedarf mit einem Holzspieß fixieren.\n\nWeitere Hinweise (vom Rezeptautor bei Chefkoch):  \nDie Rezepte für den Rote-Bete-Dip und die Portweinschalotten sind ebenfalls bei Chefkoch zu finden【3:0†source】.\n\n**Tipp für den Grill:**  \nPatties möglichst nur einmal wenden und nicht plattdrücken, damit sie saftig bleiben.  \nDen Grill sehr heiß aufheizen und idealerweise eine gusseiserne Pfanne verwenden, da Bisonfleisch sehr mager ist und sonst leicht zu trocken werden kann.  \n\nViel Erfolg und guten Appetit!","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":1471,"end_index":1483,"url_citation":{"title":"Der Bisonburger von kkellings - Chefkoch","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/3007371453624664/Der-Bisonburger.html"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"Bisonburger rezept grill site:chefkoch.de","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=Bisonburger+rezept+grill+site%3Achefkoch.de"}],"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:44:22.8870284Z     ## **Hamburger recipe**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8870483Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8871190Z     In my youth, I learned how to make hamburgers from a celebrity chef who insisted that you needed breadcrumbs, egg and seasonings for a truly great burger. And that’s how I made it for years, thinking that was the right way.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8871832Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8872823Z     After multiple trips to the States (sorry Australia, but no one does burgers like America!), too many burgers, and watching countless cooking shows, meeting chefs, caterers and restauranteurs, **I’ve been converted to the right way.**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8873493Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8873725Z     **A great beef hamburger patty requires nothing more than beef.**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8873997Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8874603Z     No flavourings other than salt and pepper. Just a nice fatty beef mince – even from the supermarket.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8874972Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8875351Z     ![Beef Hamburgers in a black skillet, fresh off the stove](https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2016/02/Beef-Hamburgers_6.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8875765Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8875933Z     ## **Best Hamburger Patties**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8876142Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8877094Z     The secret to a truly great homemade hamburger recipe is to use beef that’s nice and fatty. Ideally, a good quality ground beef / mince from a butcher, but nowadays, supermarkets sell very good quality beef too.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8877969Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8878860Z     When it comes to cooking with meat, **here’s a big takeaway from this hamburger recipe: _fat = flavour_.** If you mix pork fat into lean beef then cook it up, you’d swear you’re eating pork.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8879829Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8881070Z     Thus, the big secret is revealed. **The very best burgers you’ve had are made with nothing more than fatty beef, salt and pepper.** That’s the way it’s done at all your favourite burger joints and diners (McDonald’s aside, because who knows the secret spices in those burgers??).
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8882201Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8882639Z     ![How to make Hamburger patties](https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2016/02/Beef-Hamburgers_4.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8883160Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8883390Z     ## **Tip: Dent the Hamburger Patty**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8883654Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8884100Z     If you’ve ever had your burgers shrink into little domes, you’ll love this little tip.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8884647Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8885330Z     Dent the surface of the patty using your fingers. You’ll still get some shrinkage, but substantially less. Great tip I picked up from Bobby Flay!
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8886001Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8886214Z     ## **What to put on Hamburgers**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8886489Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8887627Z     As a food blogger, it’s sometimes hard to resist making food look crazy irresistible. I confess to being a sucker for this, and such is the case with the first hamburger recipe I published, pictured below, with that mouth watering runny yolk and golden crispy bacon.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8888599Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8898383Z     Truth be told, I never order burgers fully loaded. Because classic hamburgers done right are crazy delicious as they are, and also for practical reasons – **whose mouth is big enough to shove THIS in????**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8899309Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8900257Z     ![Fully loaded Hamburger with bacon and egg, with a side of chips and beer, ready to be eaten](https://www.recipetineats.com/tachyon/2016/02/Beef-Burger-fully-loaded-2.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8901050Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8902000Z     For me, if you’ve got a big, juicy hamburger patty topped with melted cheese, all you need is a big dollop of a condiment of choice (relish, ketchup, mustard, mayo), lettuce, tomato and pickles.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8902859Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8903085Z     Everything else is optional.
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8903364Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8903965Z     Though as an Aussie, excluding beetroot from the list of “must haves” may be considered a blasphemy!!! **_– Nagi x_**
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8904669Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:22.8905285Z     PS I’m a sucker for hamburgers on the BBQ, but ironically, I usually make them on the stove because it’s easier midweek.

2026-04-24T08:44:22.8928782Z     *   ▢ 3 onions, peeled and sliced into rings

2026-04-24T08:44:35.7003058Z     ### Vil du have de bedste opskrifter på mail?
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7003339Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7003651Z     Få Madens Verdens nyhedsbrev med opskrifter og inspiration til din madlavning.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7003971Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7004735Z     ![holger rørby madsen madens verden](https://madensverden.dk/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/cropped-holger-roerby-madsen-madens-verden-30x30.jpeg)Holger Rørby Madsen
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7005290Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7005453Z     ## Ingredienser
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7005628Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7005845Z     *   ▢ 800 g hakket oksekød
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7006073Z     *   ▢ 1,5 tsk salt
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7006476Z     *   ▢ 1 tsk løgpulver
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7006721Z     *   ▢ 1 tsk hvidløgspulver
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7006937Z     *   ▢ 1 tsk sennep
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7007151Z     *   ▢ 1 spsk tomatketchup
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7007366Z     *   ▢ 1 æg
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7007574Z     *   ▢ friskkværnet peber
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7007770Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7007955Z     ## Fremgangsmåde
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7008136Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7008461Z     1.  Smuldr det hakkede oksekød i en skål, og kom saltet ved. Ælt eller rør sammen.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7008777Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7009403Z     2.  Tilsæt så løgpulver, hvidløgspulver, sennep, tomatketchup, æg og friskkværnet peber. Du kan i øvrigt undlade æg, hvis du synes det, men jeg mener det giver en bedre bøf til burger.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7010112Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7010760Z     3.  Ælt det godt sammen med hænderne indtil farsen er samlet. Du kan selvfølgelig også røre farsen til dine burgerbøffer med en grydeske, men jeg synes farsen er bedst når den blot æltes sammen.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7011585Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7012283Z     4.  Tag farsen ud på køkkenbordet, og del den i otte ligestore stykker, så hver bøf vejer 100 gram stykket. Der er beregnet to burgerbøffer til hver person. Vej gerne farsen af, så burgerbøfferne bliver lige store.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7012879Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7013293Z     5.  Form bøfferne som anvist i videoen. Du kan også bruge en bøf-ring eller lignende, som du kan se på billedet her.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7013678Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7014355Z         ![ring til formning af burger bøffer](https://madensverden.dk/wp-content/uploads/2018/03/ring-ti-lformning-af-burger-boeffer-360x240.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7014850Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7015240Z     6.  Derefter hakkes burgerbøfferne på begge sider med bagsiden af en kniv. Så hænger kødet bedre sammen.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7015604Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7016062Z         ![hakke burger bøf med kniv](https://madensverden.dk/wp-content/uploads/2018/03/hakke-burger-boef-med-kniv-360x240.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7016481Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7016965Z     7.  Burgerbøffer grilles ved direkte varme eller på panden ved høj varme. Afhængig af tykkelsen skal de have cirka 2-3 minutter på hver side.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7017409Z         
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7017563Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7017754Z     ## Næringsindhold

2026-04-24T08:44:35.7021427Z     ## Opskrift på saftige burgerbøffer
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7021763Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7022211Z     Saftige burgerbøffer, der æltes i hånden og får den helt rigtige smag.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7022513Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7022852Z     Dette er en både simpel og let opskrift, hvor du samtidig i videoen kan se hvordan du gør.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7023188Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7023350Z     ## Fedtprocent
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7023527Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7023884Z     Jeg synes du får de bedste burgerbøffer, hvis du bruger kød med en fedtprocent på omkring 10.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7024344Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7024692Z     Typisk sælges det som hakket oksekød med en 8-12 procent fedt, og det er perfekt til bøfferne.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7025027Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7025609Z     Jeg plejer at lave bøfferne med en størrelse på ca. 100 gram stykket, men du kan jo lave dem større, hvis du synes det. Her forlænges stegetiden på grillen eller panden så også lidt.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7026116Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7026284Z     Opskriften er til fire personer.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7026500Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7026718Z     **Se også de populære opskrifter på:**
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7027165Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7027394Z     *   [Smashburger](https://madensverden.dk/smashburger/)
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7027765Z     *   [Klassisk burger](https://madensverden.dk/verdens-bedste-burger-hjemmelavet/)
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7028176Z     *   [Big Mac burger](https://madensverden.dk/hjemmelavet-big-mac-burger/)
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7028468Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7028626Z     ## Note
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7028788Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7029055Z     De færdigstegte eller grillede burgerbøffer serveres straks.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7029317Z     
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7029882Z     Bøfferne puttes i [burgerboller](https://madensverden.dk/burgerboller/) sammen med en sprød salat, syltede løg, tomat og bacon. Eller hvad du nu vil have i din burger.
2026-04-24T08:44:35.7030390Z

Häufig wird eine Marinade mit dunklem Bier, Limettensaft, etwas Senf, Knoblauch, Ingwer und Chipotle-Chili verwendet. Die Steaks werden nach dem Marinieren auf dem Holzkohlegrill unter mehrmaligem Wenden bei starker Hitze gegrillt. Eine Kerntemperatur von maximal 50 °C für medium-rare wird empfohlen. Nach dem Grillen kurz in Alufolie ruhen lassen und erst dann mit grobem Meersalz und Pfeffer würzen. Als Beilage passt Kräuterbutter sehr gut dazu【3:0†source】【3:1†source】.\n\n- **Wichtige Hinweise:**  \n  - Bisonfleisch ist deutlich magerer als Rind, daher muss besonders auf die Garzeit geachtet werden, da das Steak sonst schnell trocken wird und einen leberartigen Geschmack annehmen kann.\n  - Ideale Kerntemperaturen: Rare bei ca. 45°C, Medium-Rare bei 50°C.\n  - Marinieren verbessert die Zartheit und gibt zusätzlichen Geschmack, das Fleisch sollte aber nie zu lange bei zu niedrigen Temperaturen gegart werden.\n\n- **Rezeptideen und weitere Zubereitung:**  \n  - Marinade: Dunkelbier, Limettensaft, Zwiebel, Knoblauch, brauner Zucker, Worcestershiresauce, körniger Senf, Rapsöl, Ingwer, Chipotle Pepper Sauce, etwas Chili.\n  - Tipps: 3 Stunden vor dem Grillen aus dem Kühlschrank nehmen, nur kurz und heiß grillen, ruhen lassen.\n  - Das Steak harmoniert besonders gut mit gegrilltem Gemüse oder Backofenkartoffeln【3:1†source】.\n\nFür mehr Inspiration oder Variationen schau dir auch die umfangreichen Steak-Grill-Rezepte bei chefkoch.de an. To make the chimichurri, blitz the parsley, oregano, garlic, shallot and chilli flakes in a food processor (or chop finely by hand).\n2. Add 2 tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar and seasoning. Pulse to form a saucy consistency.\n3. Rub the remaining oil and some seasoning onto the steaks.  \n4. Heat your BBQ grill or a griddle pan. Cook steaks 2-3 minutes per side (adjust for your preference).\n5. Rest for a few minutes, then serve with chimichurri sauce, fries, and salad【3:3†source】.\n\nFor a full BBQ experience, you can also marinate your steak, then cook directly on the barbecue for a smoky flavour, as shown in other BBC Good Food recipes【3:2†source】. \n\nFor even more inspiration, check out their full collection of sirloin steak BBQ and grill recipes:  \n[Sirloin steak recipes from BBC Good Food](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/collection/sirloin-steak-recipes)","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":874,"end_index":886,"url_citation":{"title":"Steak with chimichurri sauce recipe | Good Food","url":"https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/steak-chimichurri-sauce"}},{"text":"","start_index":1045,"end_index":1057,"url_citation":{"title":"Sirloin steak recipes - BBC Good Food","url":"https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/collection/simple-sirloin-steak-recipes"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"site:bbcgoodfood.com sirloin steak bbq grill recipe","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Abbcgoodfood.com+sirloin+steak+bbq+grill+recipe"}],"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:45:15.0449311Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0449520Z     0
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0449734Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0449944Z     1. Schritt
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0450172Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0451237Z     Bisonfilet ggf. parieren (wenn möglich besser schon vom Metzger parieren lassen), abwaschen und trockentupfen. 4 Tranchen à 250 g schneiden.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0451721Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0451881Z     Anzeige
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0452042Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0452193Z     2. Schritt
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0452364Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0453969Z     Knoblauch und Zwiebel fein hacken, Ingwer reiben und Limetten auspressen. In einer großen Schüssel Bier, Zucker, Limettensaft, Zwiebeln, Knoblauch, Worcestershire-Sauce, Senf, Öl, Ingwer und Chipotle-Pepper-Sauce mischen, bis die Marinade eine sämige Konsistenz bekommt. Abschmecken und nach Geschmack noch Chili-Pulver zufügen, ggf. von den anderen Zutaten ebenfalls noch etwas zugeben.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0455459Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0455625Z     3. Schritt
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0455794Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0456893Z     Die Steaks mit der Flüssigkeit marinieren und über Nacht im Kühlschrank durchziehen lassen. Tipp: Das Marinieren klappt am einfachsten in einem Gefrierbeutel: Man lege das Fleisch hinein, fülle die Marinade ein und drücke vor dem Verschließen die gesamte Luft aus dem Beutel. Etwas durchkneten, damit von allen Seiten Marinade ans Fleisch kommt.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0457978Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0458189Z     Anzeige
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0458400Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0458599Z     4. Schritt
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0458811Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0460058Z     Am nächsten Tag das Bisonfleisch mind. 3 Stunden vor dem Grillen aus dem Kühlschrank nehmen. Das Fleisch sollte vor der Zubereitung Raumtemperatur erreicht haben, dann wird es richtig schön zart.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0460905Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0461104Z     5. Schritt
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0461307Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0465443Z     Am besten die Steaks auf dem Holzkohlegrill je nach Dicke von jeder Seite insgesamt 3-5 Minuten grillen. Zwischendurch 2 bis 3 mal wenden und immer mal wieder mit der Grillzange testen, wie weit des Fleisch ist; Bison sollte immer „rare“ bis höchstens „medium-rare“ verzehrt werden und nie bei zu niedriger Temperatur zu lange gegart werden, da es sonst einen leberartigen Geschmack annimmt. Anschließend vor dem Servieren noch ca. 5 Minuten in Alufolie gewickelt ruhen lassen, damit sich das Fleisch entspannt und seinen vollen Geschmack entfaltet. Bei der Zubereitung in der Pfanne oder im Ofen: Bitte ebenfalls darauf achten, dass nicht das Niedrigtemperaturgaren angewandt wird. Bei anderen Fleischsorten funktioniert es gut, bei Bison aber verdirbt es den Geschmack. Lieber scharf anbraten – ruhen lassen – und zum Schluß noch mal heiß nachbraten. Dies gilt übrigens auch für nicht mariniertes Bisonfleisch.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0469003Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0469199Z     Anzeige
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0469403Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0469598Z     6. Schritt
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0469811Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0470870Z     Das Fleisch schmeckt jetzt unglaublich zart und saftig, auch ohne weitere Saucen. Es passen aber auch Kräuterbutter oder süß-scharfe Saucen dazu. Als Beilage eignen sich alle Arten von Grillgemüsen oder Backofenkartoffeln.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0471827Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0472027Z     Unser Tipp
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0472237Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0473950Z     Kann man es auch anders zubereiten? Bison schmeckt auch "à la nature" mit etwas Kräuterbutter ganz hervorragend. Gibt es eine Geschichte zu Ihrem Rezept? Sogar meine Schwester, die unglaublich auf gesunde Ernährung achtet, war vom Geschmack begeistert und vor allem davon, dass das Bisonfleisch sogar noch magerer als Geflügel ist - und dafür aber "8 Millionen mal so gut schmeckt."
2026-04-24T08:45:15.0475618Z

2026-04-24T08:45:15.2419893Z         
2026-04-24T08:45:15.2420592Z         To make the chimichurri, blitz the parsley, oregano, garlic, shallot and chilli flakes in a food processor or chop very finely by hand. Add 2 tbsp of the olive oil, the lemon juice, vinegar and some seasoning, and pulse to combine everything to a saucy consistency.

2026-04-24T08:45:15.3524719Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3524927Z     1
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3525140Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3525962Z     Das Fleisch einige Stunden vor der Zubereitung aus dem Kühlschrank nehmen und Zimmertemperatur annehmen lassen. Vor dem Zubereiten unter fließendem Wasser abspülen und trockentupfen.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3526579Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3526758Z     2
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3526923Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3527987Z     Bratöl und Butterschmalz heiß werden lassen und das Fleisch darin von allen Seiten langsam 3 - 4 min pro Seite gut anbraten. Darauf achten, dass das Fleisch nicht zu dunkel wird. In der Zwischenzeit den Backofen auf 90 °C vorheizen, eine flache Form (z. B. den Deckel vom Bräter o.ä.) mit erwärmen. 1 Zwiebel grob in Ringe schneiden.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3528915Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3529072Z     3
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3529231Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3530396Z     Für die Senfmarinade 4 Knoblauchzehen kleinschneiden und mit einem halben TL Salz mit einer Gabel vermischen und mit den anderen Zutaten verrühren. Den Blauschimmelkäse mit der Gabel zerdrücken und unter die Marinade rühren. Die Paste sollte streichfähig aber nicht zu dünnflüssig sein. Sollte sie zu dünn sein etwas Semmelmehl zugeben oder bei Bedarf etwas Öl unterrühren.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3531380Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3531533Z     4
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3531709Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3531955Z     Den Mozzarella quer in dünne Scheiben schneiden.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3532196Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3532349Z     5
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3532500Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3534558Z     Das Fleisch aus dem Bräter nehmen, das Öl etwas abtupfen und mit Salz , Pfeffer und Paprika würzen. Die Zwiebelringe in den erwärmten Deckel der Bratform legen und das Fleisch daraufsetzen. Dann mit der Senfmarinade rundum bestreichen, sie sollte haften bleiben. Den Mozzarella oben auf die Marinade legen und ein Fleischthermometer in die Mitte des Fleisches stecken. Form in den vorgeheizten Ofen schieben und bei 90 °C ca. 90 – 120 min braten. Wenn die Kerntemperatur bei 60 °C liegt, den Ofen ausschalten und das Fleisch noch 10 min ruhen lassen, der Braten gart noch ca. 2 - 3 °C nach. Dann ist das Fleisch innen zart rosa (medium).
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3536140Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3536303Z     6
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3536457Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3538735Z     In der Zwischenzeit die Soße bereiten. Dazu den Bratfond wieder erhitzen und feingehackte Zwiebeln, die Knoblauchzehen und eine Handvoll fein gehacktes Suppengemüse darin anbraten. Wacholderbeeren, Lorbeerblätter und Pfefferkörner dazugeben und mitbraten. Sobald die Zwiebeln zu bräunen beginnen, mit dem Balsamico ablöschen, und verdampfen lassen. Dann den Sherry dazugeben und ebenfalls einkochen lassen. Dann mit der Brühe ablöschen, aufkochen und reduzieren, bis von der Brühe nichts mehr übrig ist. Den Sud erneut mit 200 ml Wasser auffüllen und auf kleiner Flamme ca. 20 min köcheln bzw. ziehen lassen, aber nicht einkochen. Dieser wiederholte Prozess erzeugt ein sehr kräftiges Aroma.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3540477Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3540631Z     7
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3540784Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3542087Z     Den Blauschimmelkäse zerdrücken und unter die köchelnde Soße rühren, bis er sich aufgelöst hat. Nach 20 min die Lorbeerblätter entfernen und anschließend die Soße mit allen anderen Zutaten in einem Mixer pürieren und durch ein Sieb zurück in den Topf geben. Die roten Beeren und den grünen Pfeffer dazugeben und auf ganz kleiner Hitze weitere 20 min ziehen lassen. Ggf. etwas Wasser nachfüllen, falls die Soße zu dick ist.
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3543284Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3543437Z     8
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3543588Z     
2026-04-24T08:45:15.3544133Z     20 min bevor das Fleisch gar ist (bei Kerntemperatur ca. 57°C) können die übrigen Beilagen zubereitet werden. Bei uns gab es Bratkartoffeln und Kaiserschoten dazu.

2026-04-24T08:47:08.0319808Z ### Source 1: BBC Good Food — Barbecue beef burger (British tradition)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0320431Z **URL**: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/barbecue-beef-burger  (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0320973Z **Recipe from Good Food magazine, July 2022**
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0321482Z **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 10 min + 30 min chilling
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0321735Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0321847Z **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0322123Z - 400g beef mince (substitute bison)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0322472Z - 1 banana shallot, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0322782Z - 1 tbsp strong horseradish sauce
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0323076Z - 1 tsp dried mixed herbs
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0323380Z - 1 large garlic clove, finely grated
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0323723Z - 40g blue cheese (optional)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0324026Z - 2 tsp vegetable oil
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0324493Z - 4 burger buns
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0324808Z - iceberg lettuce, tomato slices and relish to serve
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0325098Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0325219Z **Method** (as fetched):
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0325941Z 1. Tip the mince, shallot, horseradish sauce, herbs and garlic into a bowl, season well with salt and black pepper. Combine everything well using your hands.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0327283Z 2. Divide the mixture into four portions and shape each into a 3cm-thick patty. Press a small 50p-sized piece of blue cheese into the middle of each patty if using, and pack the meat around it.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0328647Z 3. Put the burgers on a plate, cover and chill for at least 30 mins or overnight. Light the barbecue and wait for the coals to turn ashen. Or, heat a gas barbecue to medium.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0330267Z 4. Lightly oil the burger patties all over and cook for 5 mins until just starting to char. Carefully flip over and cook for a further 4-5 mins (for medium) or longer if you prefer well done. Remove the burgers to a board and leave to rest for a few minutes before serving.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0332196Z 5. Alternatively, fry the burgers over a medium heat for 4-5 mins on each side until cooked through (a probe thermometer should read 70°C).
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0333019Z 6. Serve the burgers in the buns with lettuce, tomato slices and relish.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0333369Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0333855Z **Key detail**: "a probe thermometer should read 70°C" for cooked through (ground meat food safety).
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0334504Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0334833Z ### Source 2: Madensverden.dk — Saftige burgerbøffer (Danish tradition)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0335452Z **URL**: https://madensverden.dk/saftige-burgerboeffer/  (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0335999Z **Author**: Holger Rørby Madsen
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0336459Z **Serves**: 4 (8 patties of 100g each) · **Cook**: 6 min total
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0336761Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0336873Z **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0337265Z - 800 g hakket oksekød (ground beef — substitute bison)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0337689Z - 1½ tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0338036Z - 1 tsp løgpulver (onion powder)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0338624Z - 1 tsp hvidløgspulver (garlic powder)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0338977Z - 1 tsp sennep (mustard)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0339271Z - 1 tbsp tomatketchup
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0339539Z - 1 egg
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0339874Z - friskkværnet peber (freshly ground pepper)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0340133Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0340262Z **Method** (translated from Danish):
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0340699Z 1. Crumble the ground meat into a bowl, add the salt. Mix together.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0341485Z 2. Add onion powder, garlic powder, mustard, ketchup, egg and freshly ground pepper. Knead well with hands until the mince is combined.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0343116Z 3. Take the mince onto the work surface and divide into 8 equal pieces so each patty weighs 100 g. Shape the patties (can use a burger ring as shown in the video). Hack the patties on both sides with the back of a knife — this helps the meat hold together.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0344787Z 4. Grill over direct heat or in a pan over high heat. Depending on thickness, grill for approximately 2–3 minutes on each side.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0346051Z 5. Fat percentage: the author recommends around 10% fat for the best patties (8–12% is typical supermarket ground beef). Notes that bison is very lean — may need to add fat.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0346717Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0347115Z **Key detail**: no specific internal temperature given, grill 2–3 min per side at high heat.
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0347521Z 
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0347806Z ### Source 3: Chefkoch.de — Der Bisonburger (German tradition)

2026-04-24T08:47:08.0351589Z - 2 EL Dip: Rote Bete Dip mit Pecanüssen (beetroot dip with pecans)
2026-04-24T08:47:08.0352048Z - 2 Scheiben Sandwichgurken (sandwich pickles)
