2026-04-24T08:48:32.1181562Z       ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1181810Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1184438Z       Bison burger patties are made from ground bison meat, typically sourced from the shoulder, chuck, or round — the same primal cuts used for beef hamburgers. Bison is notably leaner than beef, often containing only 2–7% fat depending on the grind, compared to 15–20% in a typical beef burger blend. This leanness is both the appeal and the challenge: bison has a clean, slightly sweet, mineral-forward flavour but is prone to drying out if overcooked or if insufficient fat is present.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1186465Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1188866Z       Because ground bison is so lean, fat management is critical. Many cooks either blend pure ground bison with a fattier cut (e.g., bison back fat or beef suet) to reach roughly 10–15% total fat, or they compensate with binding ingredients such as egg, mustard, and ketchup that add moisture and help hold the patty together. Cooking over high direct heat — whether on charcoal, a gas grill, or a cast-iron pan — is the preferred method, as it quickly builds a crust that seals in juices before the lean meat has time to dry out.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1190443Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1192230Z       Food safety regulations in most countries classify ground meat as a high-risk product requiring thorough cooking: the USDA standard for ground meat is 71 °C / 160 °F internal temperature. BBC Good Food echoes this at 70 °C. However, gourmet and restaurant preparations for trusted-source ground bison sometimes target 63–65 °C for a medium result with a pink centre. The appropriate pull temperature depends on the intended doneness, the diners' preferences, and local food safety guidelines.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1193562Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1194351Z       A MEATER probe inserted sideways into a patty gives reliable internal readings even for thin cuts, provided it is placed horizontally through the thickest part of the patty, keeping the tip and ambient sensor both clear of the grill grates.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1195049Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1195215Z       ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1195405Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1195723Z       ### 1. BBC Good Food — Barbecue Beef Burger (British tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1195988Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1196404Z       **Source**: Good Food magazine, [Barbecue beef burger](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/barbecue-beef-burger) (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1197268Z       **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 10 min + 30 min chilling · **Cook**: 9–10 min · **Rest**: 2–3 min
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1197593Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1197761Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1198113Z       - 400 g beef mince (substitute ground bison; consider blending with ~15% fat to compensate for leanness)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1198513Z       - 1 banana shallot, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1198749Z       - 1 tbsp strong horseradish sauce
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1198978Z       - 1 tsp dried mixed herbs
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1199215Z       - 1 large garlic clove, finely grated
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1199474Z       - 40 g blue cheese (optional, for stuffing)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1199718Z       - 2 tsp vegetable oil
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1199917Z       - 4 burger buns
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1200140Z       - Iceberg lettuce, tomato slices and relish, to serve
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1200389Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1200550Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1201021Z       1. Tip the mince, shallot, horseradish sauce, herbs and garlic into a bowl, season well with salt and black pepper. Combine everything well using your hands.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1201982Z       2. Divide the mixture into four portions and shape each into a 3 cm-thick patty. Press a small 50p-sized piece of blue cheese into the middle of each patty if using, and pack the meat around it.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1202845Z       3. Put the burgers on a plate, cover and chill for at least 30 minutes or overnight. Light the barbecue and wait for the coals to turn ashen, or heat a gas barbecue to medium.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1203476Z       4. Insert the MEATER probe sideways into the thickest part of a patty, avoiding the edges.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1204450Z       5. Lightly oil the burger patties all over and cook for 5 minutes until just starting to char. Carefully flip over and cook for a further 4–5 minutes (for medium) or longer if you prefer well done.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1205710Z       6. **Pull at 65–70 °C (149–158 °F)** — BBC Good Food states a probe thermometer should read 70 °C for "cooked through" (ground meat food safety). For medium with a slight pink centre, pull at 65 °C; for fully cooked through, pull at 70 °C and the carry-over will reach ~71 °C.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1206586Z       7. Remove the burgers to a board and leave to rest for 2–3 minutes before serving.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1207058Z       8. Final serving temperature: **70–72 °C / 158–162 °F** (food-safe, cooked through).
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1207793Z       9. Serve the burgers in the buns with lettuce, tomato slices and relish. Alternatively, fry over a medium heat for 4–5 minutes per side until cooked through (probe reads 70 °C).
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1208313Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1208474Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1208635Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1208924Z       ### 2. Madensverden.dk — Saftige Burgerbøffer (Danish tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1209203Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1209774Z       **Source**: Holger Rørby Madsen, [Saftige burgerbøffer](https://madensverden.dk/saftige-burgerboeffer/) (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1210488Z       **Serves**: 4 (8 patties of 100 g each) · **Prep**: 10 min · **Cook**: 6 min total · **Rest**: 2 min
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1210815Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1210984Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1211503Z       - 800 g hakket oksekød / ground beef (substitute ground bison; author notes bison is very lean — consider adding fat to reach ~10% fat content)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1211993Z       - 1½ tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1212220Z       - 1 tsp løgpulver (onion powder)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1212496Z       - 1 tsp hvidløgspulver (garlic powder)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1212735Z       - 1 tsp sennep (mustard)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1212947Z       - 1 tbsp tomatketchup
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1213144Z       - 1 egg
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1213387Z       - Friskkværnet peber (freshly ground pepper)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1213623Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1213780Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1214160Z       1. Crumble the ground meat into a bowl, add the salt and mix together.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1214852Z       2. Add onion powder, garlic powder, mustard, ketchup, egg and freshly ground pepper. Knead well with hands until the mince is fully combined.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1216073Z       3. Transfer the mince to the work surface and divide into 8 equal pieces so each patty weighs approximately 100 g. Shape into patties (a burger ring can be used for uniform results). Hack the surface of each patty on both sides with the back of a knife — this helps the meat hold together during cooking.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1217005Z       4. Insert the MEATER probe sideways into the thickest part of a patty, avoiding the edges.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1217653Z       5. Grill over direct high heat, or cook in a pan over high heat. Depending on thickness, grill for approximately 2–3 minutes on each side.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1218782Z       6. **Pull at 65–70 °C (149–158 °F)** — the author does not specify an internal temperature but notes the goal is a juicy patty cooked through. Given the lean nature of bison, pulling at 68–70 °C ensures food safety while retaining as much moisture as possible from the lean grind.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1219686Z       7. Rest for 1–2 minutes before serving.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1220069Z       8. Final serving temperature: **70–71 °C / 158–160 °F** (food-safe, cooked through).
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1220822Z       9. Note: The author recommends approximately 10% fat for the best patties (8–12% is typical supermarket ground beef). Pure ground bison may require added fat to prevent dry patties.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1221359Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1221520Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1221678Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1221930Z       ### 3. Chefkoch.de — Der Bisonburger (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1222179Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1222575Z       **Source**: kkellings, [Der Bisonburger](https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/3007371453624664/Der-Bisonburger.html) (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1223135Z       **Serves**: 1 · **Prep**: 30 min · **Cook**: 10 min · **Rest**: 4 min
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1223406Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1223572Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1223926Z       - 200 g Burger Patties aus Bisonfleisch, tiefgekühlt (frozen bison burger patty)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1224469Z       - 1 Burgerbrötchen (burger bun)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1224814Z       - 2 EL Dip: Rote Bete Dip mit Pecanüssen (beetroot dip with pecans)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1225131Z       - 2 Scheiben Sandwichgurken (sandwich pickles)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1225457Z       - 4 Scheiben Bacon, hauchdünn (thin-sliced bacon)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1225704Z       - 1 medium egg
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1225914Z       - 2 EL Portweinschalotten (port wine shallots)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1226213Z       - 2 Scheiben Raclettekäse (Raclette cheese)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1226462Z       - 1 EL Currysauce (curry sauce)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1226735Z       - Butterschmalz zum Braten (clarified butter for cooking)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1227011Z       - 1 TL Meersalz (sea salt)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1227211Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1227390Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1227764Z       1. Allow the bison patty to thaw in the refrigerator and bring to room temperature approximately 1 hour before cooking.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1228767Z       2. Cut the burger bun and toast the inside golden-brown. Spread the bottom half with beetroot dip and lay on pickle slices. Spread the top half with curry sauce. Fry bacon until crispy and drain on kitchen paper. Fry an egg sunny-side up with runny yolk in clarified butter. Warm the port wine shallots.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1229808Z       3. Heat clarified butter in a cast-iron pan until very hot. Salt the top of the bison patty with sea salt and place salted-side down in the pan.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1230390Z       4. Insert the MEATER probe sideways into the thickest part of the patty, avoiding the edges.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1231141Z       5. Fry for 3 minutes with the lid closed. Turn the patty over, cover the cooked side with Raclette cheese and cook for a further 3 minutes with the lid on.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1232473Z       6. **Pull at 65–68 °C (149–154 °F)** — the recipe does not specify an internal temperature, but the 3+3 minute method in a very hot cast-iron pan with a lid is designed to cook the patty through while the cheese melts. Given the 200 g patty size and cast-iron retained heat, pulling at 65–68 °C and resting allows carry-over to reach ~70–71 °C.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1233451Z       7. Move the pan aside and let the patty rest for approximately 4 minutes before assembling.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1233933Z       8. Final serving temperature: **70–71 °C / 158–160 °F** (food-safe after resting).
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1234825Z       9. Assemble the burger: place the bison patty on the pickle slices, add the port wine shallots onto the melted cheese, lay on the crispy bacon, followed by the fried egg. Secure the top bun with a skewer.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1235409Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1235572Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1235734Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1236023Z       ### 4. RecipeTin Eats — Hamburger Recipe (Australian tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1236415Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1236744Z       **Source**: Nagi, [Hamburger recipe](https://www.recipetineats.com/hamburger-recipe/) (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1237237Z       **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 15 min · **Cook**: 8 min · **Rest**: 2–3 min
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1237500Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1237665Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1238234Z       - 800 g–1 kg ground beef (substitute ground bison; Nagi strongly recommends 20%+ fat — critical note for lean bison: blend with added fat or use a fattier grind)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1238744Z       - Salt and freshly ground black pepper
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1238982Z       - 3 onions, sliced into rings
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1239198Z       - 2 tbsp oil
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1239419Z       - 4–8 slices cheese (optional)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1239659Z       - 4 soft hamburger buns, lightly toasted
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1239998Z       - Lettuce, tomato slices, ketchup, mustard, relish and sliced pickles, to serve
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1240329Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1240490Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1241184Z       1. Separate the ground meat into 4 equal portions. Using hands, lightly form each into a patty the size of the bun (approximately 10 cm / 4 inches in diameter). Do not overwork the meat — handle only enough to shape.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1242069Z       2. Using fingers, make a shallow dent in the surface of each patty. This prevents the patty from doming upward as it cooks and ensures even contact with the grill.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1242740Z       3. Season generously with salt and pepper on both sides just before cooking (salting too early draws out moisture).
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1243255Z       4. Insert the MEATER probe sideways into the thickest part of a patty, avoiding the edges.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1243844Z       5. Cook over high direct heat (charcoal grill, gas grill or a very hot pan). Grill for approximately 3–4 minutes per side.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1245061Z       6. **Pull at 65–70 °C (149–158 °F)** — Nagi does not state an internal temperature in this recipe but the "fat = flavour" philosophy and high-heat method are aimed at a juicy, medium-cooked result. For bison (lean), pull at 68–70 °C to ensure food safety while the carry-over finishes the cook.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1245924Z       7. Add cheese slices in the final minute of cooking if using, allowing them to melt.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1246298Z       8. Rest for 2–3 minutes before assembling.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1246682Z       9. Final serving temperature: **70–72 °C / 158–162 °F** (food-safe, cooked through).
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1247410Z       10. Note: Nagi states that 20%+ fat is essential for flavour and texture. For bison adaptation, adding fat (e.g. mixing in beef back fat, or using a bison/beef blend) is strongly recommended to achieve a comparable result.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1248013Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1248176Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1248338Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1248624Z       ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1248842Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1249043Z       | Source | Pull temperature | Method | Doneness |
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1249315Z       |--------|-----------------|--------|---------|
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1249708Z       | BBC Good Food (UK) | 70 °C / 158 °F | Charcoal/gas grill, 5 + 4–5 min | Cooked through |
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1250264Z       | Madensverden.dk (DK) | Not stated; ~68–70 °C inferred | Direct high heat, 2–3 min per side | Cooked through / juicy |
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1250882Z       | Chefkoch.de (DE) | Not stated; ~65–68 °C pull, ~70 °C after rest | Cast-iron pan, 3+3 min + 4 min rest | Cooked through |
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1251506Z       | RecipeTin Eats (AU) | Not stated; ~68–70 °C inferred | High-heat grill/pan, 3–4 min per side | Medium–cooked through |
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1251894Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1253038Z       **Consensus pull**: **68–70 °C (154–158 °F)** for food-safe cooked-through bison burger. BBC Good Food explicitly states 70 °C. All other sources use time-based methods that align with this range given typical patty thickness (2–3 cm). For a medium result with a slight pink centre (gourmet/restaurant style), 63–65 °C is used, but this is below USDA ground meat safety standards.
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1254166Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1254463Z       **Representative coding value**:
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1254831Z       - `well_done` / food-safe: **71 °C / 160 °F** pull (USDA ground meat standard)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1255311Z       - `medium`: **65 °C / 149 °F** pull (gourmet compromise; below USDA standard — note in UI)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1255619Z

2026-04-24T08:48:32.1255999Z

2026-04-24T08:48:32.1256623Z       - `pull_temp_c`: `71` (USDA/BBC consensus for ground meat — cooked through)
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1256948Z       - `pull_temp_f`: `160`
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1257170Z       - `finish_temp_c`: `72`
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1257389Z       - `finish_temp_f`: `162`
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1257823Z       - Secondary doneness `"medium"`: pull `65 °C / 149 °F` — display a food safety advisory in the UI for ground meat
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1258205Z       - Bison-specific notes:
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1258549Z         - Very lean meat (2–7% fat); recommend blending to ~10–15% fat for best texture
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1258955Z         - Benefits from binding agents (egg, mustard, ketchup) to hold patty together
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1259324Z         - Do not overwork the mince; handle minimally when shaping
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1259840Z         - MEATER probe placement: insert sideways into the thickest part of the patty, keeping both probe tip and ambient sensor clear of grill grates
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1260537Z       - Method: direct high heat (charcoal grill, gas grill, or cast-iron pan); 2–5 min per side depending on thickness
2026-04-24T08:48:32.1260966Z       - Rest: 2–4 minutes after pulling

2026-04-24T08:48:38.4474581Z       **URL**: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/barbecue-beef-burger  (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4475106Z       **Recipe from Good Food magazine, July 2022**
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4475594Z       **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 10 min + 30 min chilling
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4475925Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4476160Z       **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4476457Z       - 400g beef mince (substitute bison)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4476789Z       - 1 banana shallot, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4477129Z       - 1 tbsp strong horseradish sauce
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4477449Z       - 1 tsp dried mixed herbs
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4477776Z       - 1 large garlic clove, finely grated
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4478126Z       - 40g blue cheese (optional)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4478439Z       - 2 tsp vegetable oil
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4478730Z       - 4 burger buns

2026-04-24T08:48:38.4481967Z       2. Divide the mixture into four portions and shape each into a 3cm-thick patty. Press a small 50p-sized piece of blue cheese into the middle of each patty if using, and pack the meat around it.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4483379Z       3. Put the burgers on a plate, cover and chill for at least 30 mins or overnight. Light the barbecue and wait for the coals to turn ashen. Or, heat a gas barbecue to medium.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4485181Z       4. Lightly oil the burger patties all over and cook for 5 mins until just starting to char. 2026-04-24T08:48:38.4488866Z       **Key detail**: "a probe thermometer should read 70°C" for cooked through (ground meat food safety).
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4489651Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4490109Z       ### Source 2: Madensverden.dk — Saftige burgerbøffer (Danish tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4490719Z       **URL**: https://madensverden.dk/saftige-burgerboeffer/  (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4491260Z       **Author**: Holger Rørby Madsen
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4491742Z       **Serves**: 4 (8 patties of 100g each) · **Cook**: 6 min total
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4492123Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4492352Z       **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4492776Z       - 800 g hakket oksekød (ground beef — substitute bison)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4493204Z       - 1½ tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4493523Z       - 1 tsp løgpulver (onion powder)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4493926Z       - 1 tsp hvidløgspulver (garlic powder)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4494435Z       - 1 tsp sennep (mustard)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4494741Z       - 1 tbsp tomatketchup
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4495020Z       - 1 egg
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4495401Z       - friskkværnet peber (freshly ground pepper)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4495760Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4496005Z       **Method** (translated from Danish):
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4496445Z       1. Crumble the ground meat into a bowl, add the salt. Mix together.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4497252Z       2. Add onion powder, garlic powder, mustard, ketchup, egg and freshly ground pepper. Knead well with hands until the mince is combined.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4499031Z       3. Take the mince onto the work surface and divide into 8 equal pieces so each patty weighs 100 g. Shape the patties (can use a burger ring as shown in the video). Hack the patties on both sides with the back of a knife — this helps the meat hold together.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4500596Z       4. Grill over direct heat or in a pan over high heat. Depending on thickness, grill for approximately 2–3 minutes on each side.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4501958Z       5. Fat percentage: the author recommends around 10% fat for the best patties (8–12% is typical supermarket ground beef). Notes that bison is very lean — may need to add fat.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4502788Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4503334Z       **Key detail**: no specific internal temperature given, grill 2–3 min per side at high heat.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4503828Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4504406Z       ### Source 3: Chefkoch.de — Der Bisonburger (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4505103Z       **URL**: https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/3007371453624664/Der-Bisonburger.html  (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4505813Z       **Author**: kkellings · **Rating**: 2.5/5 from 2 ratings
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4506311Z       **Serves**: 1 · **Prep**: 30 min · **Cook**: 10 min
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4506657Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4506885Z       **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4507432Z       - 200 g Burger Patties aus Bisonfleisch, tiefgekühlt (frozen bison burger patty)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4508010Z       - 1 Burgerbrötchen (burger bun)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4508726Z       - 2 EL Dip: Rote Bete Dip mit Pecanüssen (beetroot dip with pecans)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4509323Z       - 2 Scheiben Sandwichgurken (sandwich pickles)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4509790Z       - 4 Scheiben Bacon, hauchdünn (bacon, thin)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4510140Z       - 1 medium egg
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4510445Z       - 2 EL Portweinschalotten (port wine shallots)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4510899Z       - 2 Scheiben Raclettekäse (Raclette cheese)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4511240Z       - 1 EL Currysauce
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4511597Z       - Butterschmalz zum Braten (clarified butter for cooking)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4512007Z       - 1 TL Meersalz (sea salt)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4512309Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4512572Z       **Method** (translated from German):
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4513202Z       1. Allow the bison patty to thaw in the refrigerator and bring to room temperature about 1 hour before cooking.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4515000Z       2. Cut the burger bun and toast the inside golden-brown. Spread the bottom half with beetroot dip and lay on pickle slices. Spread the top half with curry sauce. Fry bacon until crispy and drain on kitchen paper. Fry an egg as a fried egg with runny yolk in butter. Warm the port wine shallots.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4517400Z       3. Heat clarified butter in a cast iron pan until very hot. Salt the top of the bison patty with sea salt on this side and fry for 3 minutes with closed lid. Turn over, cover the fried side with Raclette cheese and cook for another 3 minutes with the lid. Then move the pan aside and let the meat rest for about 4 minutes.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4519438Z       4. Assemble the burger: place the bison patty on the pickle slices, add the port wine shallots onto the melted cheese, lay on the crispy bacon, followed by the fried egg. Secure the top bun half with a skewer.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4520440Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4520846Z       **Key detail**: 3+3 minutes in a cast iron pan, rest 4 minutes. Winter burger recipe.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4521334Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4521810Z       ### Source 4: RecipeTin Eats — Hamburger recipe (Australian tradition)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4522442Z       **URL**: https://www.recipetineats.com/hamburger-recipe/  (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4522960Z       **Author**: Nagi (RecipeTin Eats)
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4523285Z       **Serves**: 4
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4523523Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4523754Z       **Key points from the recipe**:
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4524516Z       - "A great beef hamburger patty requires nothing more than beef. No flavourings other than salt and pepper."
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4525261Z       - Recommends 20%+ fat in the ground beef for best flavor. "Fat = flavour."
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4526049Z       - Separate beef into 4 equal portions, use hands to lightly form into patties the size of buns (about 10 cm / 4 inches).
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4526815Z       - Dent the surface of the patty using fingers to prevent doming while cooking.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4528221Z       - Ingredients for 4 servings: 800g-1kg ground beef, salt and pepper, 3 onions sliced into rings, 2 tbsp oil, 4-8 slices cheese (optional), 4 soft hamburger buns (lightly toasted), lettuce, tomato, ketchup, mustard, relish, sliced pickles.
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4529274Z       
2026-04-24T08:48:38.4529774Z       **Key detail**: Only beef/salt/pepper in the patty. 20%+ fat is essential. Bison adaptation requires adding fat.

2026-04-24T08:49:22.7862472Z

Kaninchenbeine:\n\n1. **Kaninchen geschmort - Fleischgeschmack pur:**  \n   Für 8 Portionen brauchst du z. B. 8 Kaninchenkeulen, 200 g Räucherspeck, Buttermilch, Butterschmalz und Salz. Die Keulen werden mindestens einen halben Tag in Buttermilch eingelegt, danach angebraten, mit Wasser abgelöscht und mit Speckscheiben im Backofen bei 150 °C ca. 90 Minuten geschmort. Dazu gibt es oft Salzkartoffeln, Rotkohl und viel Soße. Besonders wird hier auf das saftige und zarte Fleisch ohne viele Gewürze Wert gelegt【3:2†source】.\n\n2. **Geschmorte Kaninchenkeulen mit Spätzle:**  \n   Die Kaninchenkeulen werden in einer Marinade aus Rotwein, Essig, Gemüse und Gewürzen mindestens einen Tag eingelegt, danach kurz und heiß angebraten, mit Gemüse und Tomatenmark angeschmort und mit Brühe und der Marinade ca. eine Stunde lang geschmort. Die Sauce wird anschließend eingekocht und zu den Keulen gereicht. Als Beilage eignen sich Spätzle hervorragend zu diesem würzigen Gericht【3:8†source】.\n\n3. **Geschmorte Kaninchenkeulen (ganz simpel):**  \n   Hier werden die Kaninchenkeulen mit Senf, Salz und Pfeffer eingerieben, im Öl angebraten und mit grob geschnittenem Speck sowie Zwiebeln im eigenen Saft geschmort (zwischendurch etwas Wasser zugeben). Nach etwa 45 Minuten ist das Fleisch schön weich. Der Schmorfond kann püriert und evtl. angedickt werden. 2026-04-24T08:50:08.0818669Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0818894Z     
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0819097Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0819321Z         
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0821266Z         Soak the shallots in boiling water for 5 mins, drain, then peel – this makes it a lot easier to remove the skins. While they are soaking, slice the rest of the vegetables. Heat a large, wide flameproof casserole dish, then add the lardons. Fry for 5 mins or until the fat has run from the meat, then tip in the vegetables and cook for 10 mins until golden and starting to soften. Tip into a bowl.
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0822794Z         
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0823020Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0823252Z         
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0825015Z         Toss the rabbit meat with the flour, then tap off the excess. Heat half the butter and oil in the dish, then brown half the rabbit for 10 mins until golden all over. Add a good splash of water, scrape around the dish and tip the juices into a jug. Repeat with the second batch of rabbit, but use the wine to deglaze the pan this time, letting it reduce by about a third.
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0826460Z         
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0826686Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0826922Z         
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0829504Z         Add the rabbit and the vegetables to the wine, pour in the stock, stir in the herbs and bring to a simmer. Cover, leaving just a small gap for steam to escape, then simmer for 1½-2 hrs or until the meat falls away from the bones. If you like a thicker sauce to your stew, you can lift out the meat and vegetables and boil the sauce to thicken it a little. To finish, heat the knob of butter in a frying pan and cook the mushrooms with salt, pepper and a few thyme leaves over a high heat until browned. Spoon these over the stew, sprinkle with thyme leaves and bring to the table.
2026-04-24T08:50:08.0831750Z

2026-04-24T08:50:28.4423112Z     Eisenpfanne erhitzen und die Steaks ohne weiteres Öl bei mittlerer Hitze unter Wenden ca. 4 Min. insgesamt anbraten. Sie sollten schön gebräunt sein.
2026-04-24T08:50:28.4423583Z     
2026-04-24T08:50:28.4423733Z     3
2026-04-24T08:50:28.4423889Z     
2026-04-24T08:50:28.4425390Z     Während des Anbratens eine Auflaufform bei 140° im Backofen vorwärmen. Nach dem Anbraten werden die Steaks von der Pfanne in die leicht mit Olivenöl ausgestrichene Auflaufform platziert. Bei 140° 10 Min. garen, dann die Temperatur auf 100° reduzieren und weitere 15 Min. garen. Danach sollten die Steaks medium gegart sein. Wer sie lieber etwas weniger durch haben möchte, gart sie nur 7 Min. bei 140° und stellt dann schon um auf 100°. Salzen, pfeffern.

2026-04-24T08:50:33.9947204Z   result: {"type":"output_text","text":{"value":"Ein sehr beliebtes und gut bewertetes Rezept für im Ofen geschmorte Wildschweinschulter findest du auf Chefkoch.de. Die wichtigsten Schritte in Kurzform:\n\n**Wildschweinschulter ganz einfach – Geschmort ohne Schnickschnack**\n- 1,5 kg Wildschweinschulter mit Knochen\n- 0,25 l trockener Rotwein (z. B. Merlot)\n- 400 ml Wildfond\n- 1 Bund Suppengemüse, 1 EL Tomatenmark, 2 Wacholderbeeren, 2 Lorbeerblätter, 2 Zwiebeln, Salz, Pfeffer\n\n**Zubereitung:**\n1. Ofen auf 250 °C vorheizen.\n2. Fleisch mit Öl und Salz einreiben. Gemüse säubern, grob schneiden und mit Tomatenmark vermengen.\n3. Gemüse in einen Bräter geben, Schulter daraufsetzen und ca. 20 Min. rösten, bis alles etwas Farbe bekommt.\n4. Rotwein, Fond, Wacholder, Lorbeer und ggf. Zwiebeln hinzufügen, Deckel auflegen, Temperatur auf 120 °C senken, ca. 3 Stunden schmoren lassen.\n5. Zum Schluss Sauce abgießen, Gemüse passieren und nach Belieben einreduzieren oder andicken.\n\nAls Beilage passen Kartoffelpüree oder gebratener Kohl sehr gut dazu.\n\nDas Rezept ist beliebt für seinen guten Geschmack und die einfache Zubereitung. 2026-04-24T08:52:12.7172914Z ## FILE 1: `/home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/bison/bison_steak-grill.md`
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7173278Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7173707Z ### VERIFIED SOURCES (all personally fetched):
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7173884Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7174633Z **Source 1**: Daskochrezept.de — Gegrilltes Bison-Filet-Steak in süß-scharfer Bier-Chili-Marinade (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7175404Z **URL**: https://www.daskochrezept.de/rezepte/gegrilltes-bison-filet-steak-suess-scharfer-bier-chili-marinade (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7176213Z **Method** (from page content, translated from German):
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7176687Z 1. Parieren the bison fillet if necessary (better to have the butcher do it), wash and pat dry. Cut 4 slices of 250g each.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7177652Z 2. Finely chop garlic and onion, grate ginger, squeeze limes. In a large bowl, mix beer, sugar, lime juice, onions, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, oil, ginger and chipotle pepper sauce until the marinade has a creamy consistency. Adjust seasoning, add chili powder to taste.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7178934Z 3. Marinate the steaks in the liquid overnight in the refrigerator. Tip: Best in a freezer bag — put the meat in, add marinade, squeeze out the air before sealing. Knead lightly so marinade reaches all sides.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7180150Z 4. The next day: take the bison meat out of the refrigerator at least 3 hours before grilling. The meat should have reached room temperature before cooking — then it will be nicely tender.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7182703Z 5. Best method: grill the steaks over charcoal (Holzkohlegrill), depending on thickness, for a total of 3-5 minutes from each side. Turn 2-3 times during grilling, and occasionally test with tongs how done the meat is; bison should always be eaten "rare" to at most "medium-rare" and should never be cooked for too long at too low a temperature, as it will develop a liver-like taste. Then rest for about 5 minutes wrapped in aluminum foil before serving, so the meat relaxes and develops its full flavor. When preparing in a pan or oven: also note that slow-cooking/Niedrigtemperatur should NOT be used. While it works well for other meats, with bison it ruins the flavor. Better to sear sharply — rest — then briefly re-heat on high heat.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7185000Z 6. The meat will now be incredibly tender and juicy, even without extra sauces. Herb butter or sweet-spicy sauces pair well. Grilled vegetables or baked potatoes are good sides.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7185456Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7185895Z **Key temperature insight**: Bison should ALWAYS be rare to at most medium-rare. Never cook slowly at low temperatures (no Niedrigtemperatur). Liver-like off-flavors develop when overcooked.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7186523Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7186894Z **Source 2**: BBC Good Food — Steak with chimichurri sauce (British / Argentine tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7187444Z **URL**: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/steak-chimichurri-sauce (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7187809Z **Recipe from Good Food magazine, June 2012**
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7188136Z **Serves**: 2 · **Prep**: 5 min · **Cook**: 5-6 min
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7188389Z **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7188606Z - Small bunch parsley, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7188878Z - ½ tsp oregano (fresh or dried)
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7189091Z - 2 garlic cloves
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7189271Z - 1 shallot, chopped
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7189488Z - ½ tsp chilli flakes
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7189698Z - 2½ tbsp olive oil
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7189902Z - Juice of ½ lemon
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7190088Z - 2 tsp red wine vinegar
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7190298Z - 2 x 125g rib-eye or sirloin steaks
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7190532Z - Fries and salad to serve

2026-04-24T08:52:12.7190734Z **Method**:
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7191693Z 1. To make the chimichurri, blitz the parsley, oregano, garlic, shallot and chilli flakes in a food processor or chop very finely by hand. Add 2 tbsp of the olive oil, the lemon juice, vinegar and some seasoning, and pulse to combine everything to a saucy consistency.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7193078Z 2. Rub the remaining oil and a little seasoning into the steaks. Heat a griddle or frying pan and cook the steaks for 2-3 mins on each side or until done to your liking. Rest for a few mins, then spoon over the sauce and serve with fries and salad.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7194692Z **Note**: This recipe uses griddle/pan, 2-3 min per side for rib-eye or sirloin. Pull temp not explicitly stated — medium-rare at this thickness is approximately 57°C.

2026-04-24T08:52:12.7213263Z **Author**: acigrand · **Rating**: 4.5/5 from 2 ratings
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7213621Z **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Total**: 20 min (oven time ~45-60 min)
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7213934Z

2026-04-24T08:52:12.7214349Z - 1 kg bison roast
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7214568Z - Salt, pepper, thyme, rosemary
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7214776Z - 1 onion
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7214945Z - 2 garlic cloves

2026-04-24T08:52:12.7215979Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7216068Z **Method** (translated from German):
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7217487Z 1. Wash the meat and pat dry. Season with thyme, rosemary, salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven (Bräter) and sear the meat on all sides. Dice the onion and garlic, add to the meat and sauté briefly. Pour over with red wine and stock. Crush cardamom pods and add with the remaining spices to the broth. Put a lid on the Dutch oven and roast in the oven at 200°C for 45-60 minutes (depending on how through you want it), basting occasionally with some stock.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7219146Z 2. After the cooking time, keep the roast warm. Strain the cooking juices through a sieve. Bring to the boil, season with pepper and salt. Thicken with sauce binder if desired. This dish is also suitable for the Tupperware Mikro-Plus (microwave cooker).
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7219765Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7220329Z **Key note**: "45-60 min (je nach dem wie durch es sein soll)" — the time varies by preferred doneness. No explicit probe temperature stated. At 200°C, a 1kg roast reaches ~60°C internal in approximately 45 min.

2026-04-24T08:52:12.7229743Z 1. Take the meat out of the refrigerator a few hours before cooking and let it come to room temperature. Rinse under cold water and pat dry.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7230844Z 2. Heat oil and clarified butter very hot and sear the meat slowly from all sides 3-4 min per side — take care that the meat doesn't get too dark. Meanwhile preheat the oven to 90°C, warming a flat form (e.g. the lid of the Dutch oven) inside. Cut 1 onion into rough rings.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7232380Z 3. For the mustard marinade: cut 4 garlic cloves small and mix with ½ tsp salt using a fork, then stir in the other ingredients. Crush the blue cheese with a fork and stir into the marinade. The paste should be spreadable but not too runny. If too thin, add breadcrumbs; if needed, add some oil.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7233165Z 4. Cut the mozzarella into thin slices.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7235069Z 5. Remove the meat from the pan, pat off some of the oil and season with salt, pepper and paprika. Place onion rings in the warmed form and set the meat on them. Coat all around with the mustard marinade — it should stick. Place mozzarella slices on top of the marinade and insert a meat thermometer into the center of the meat. Place the form in the preheated oven at 90°C and roast for approximately 90-120 min. **When the core temperature is at 60°C, switch off the oven and rest the meat for another 10 min — the roast will continue to cook about 2-3°C. Then the meat will be tender pink inside (medium).**
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7238381Z 6. For the sauce: reheat the pan drippings and sauté finely chopped onions, garlic and soup vegetables. Add juniper berries, bay leaves and peppercorns. Once the onions start to brown, deglaze with balsamic cream and let evaporate. Then add sherry and reduce. Deglaze with stock, bring to boil and reduce until nothing remains. Refill with 200ml water and simmer on low heat for ~20 min (do not reduce further — this repeated process creates very intense flavor). Stir crushed blue cheese into the simmering sauce until dissolved. After 20 min remove bay leaves and blend all ingredients in a mixer, push through a sieve. Add red berries and green pepper, simmer on very low heat for another 20 min.
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7240369Z 7. About 20 min before the meat is done (at ~57°C core): prepare the side dishes (the author served Bratkartoffeln and mangetout).
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7240710Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7240993Z **Key temperature detail**: Pull at 60°C, rest 10 min, finish at ~62-63°C. "Tender pink inside (medium)."
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7241270Z 
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7241358Z ### TEMPERATURE REFERENCE
2026-04-24T08:52:12.7241622Z - Bison roast oven: pull temp 63°C, range 60-65°C

While it's traditionally made as a spicy, smoky, roasted goat dish, the method can easily be adapted for grilling goat chops instead of using a whole goat or larger cuts.\n\nHere’s a summary of how to make it based on the blog's instructions:\n\n### Asun (Spicy Roast or Grilled Goat)\n**Ingredients:**  \n- Goat meat (can use chopped up goat chops/rib chops)\n- Salt, black pepper, and bouillon powder\n- Onion, garlic, and ginger\n- Tomatoes\n- Paprika and white pepper\n- Green onions\n- Curry powder\n- Scotch bonnet or habanero pepper (adjust for your spice level)\n- Bell peppers (red, green, or both)\n\n**Method Overview:**\n1. **Parboil or Steam the Goat:**  \n   Boil or steam your goat meat/chops with salt, black pepper, bouillon, and onion until just tender. This helps with tougher goat cuts.\n\n2. **Grill or Broil:**  \n   Place the goat pieces on a grill (or in your oven broiler). Grill until browned and slightly charred, rotating as needed to get a smoky effect. Meat should be fully cooked and crisp around the edges.\n\n3. **Sauté Aromatics:**  \n   In a pan, sauté onions, garlic, ginger for about a minute. Add tomatoes, white pepper, paprika, green onions, curry powder, hot peppers, and bell peppers. Stir and cook for about 5-7 minutes.\n\n4. **Combine:**  \n   Add grilled goat to the sautéed veggies and toss well to coat and heat through. Cook for another 3 minutes. Taste and adjust seasonings if needed.\n\n5. **Serve:**  \n   Serve hot, often with sides like fried or baked plantains.\n\n**Tips from the blog:**  \n- If using fresh goat instead of smoked, steaming first is recommended for tenderness, especially for female goats which are usually tougher.\n- For deep flavor, use smoked paprika to mimic traditional smoked goat if you’re grilling over a regular BBQ.\n- Easily adjust the heat level by changing the amount of habanero/scotch bonnet used.\n\nFor the full detailed recipe and step-by-step photos, visit Immaculate Bites' Asun (Spicy Roast Goat) page:  \n[Asun (Spicy Roast Goat) Recipe on AfricanBites.com][3:5†source]\n\nWhile they do not list a dedicated \"grilled goat chop\" recipe, this spicy, grilled/roasted goat method is as close as you’ll get to an authentic African grilled goat chop experience on the site!","annotations":null},"bing_searches":[{"text":"goat chop recipe grill site:africanbites.com","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=goat+chop+recipe+grill+site%3Aafricanbites.com"}],"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:52:38.7836538Z     # Lamb Shank × Braise — Recipe Temperature Research
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7836924Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7837187Z     ## Tree position
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7837464Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7837696Z     | Field | Value |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7837957Z     |-------|-------|
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7838222Z     | Category | `lamb` |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7838520Z     | Cut type folder | `roasts/` |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7838854Z     | Cut slug | `lamb_shank` |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7839160Z     | Method slug | `braise` |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7839474Z     | File | `lamb_shank-braise.md` |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7839928Z     | Full path | `docs/recipe_research/lamb/roasts/lamb_shank-braise.md` |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7840367Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7840588Z     ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7840829Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7841368Z     The lamb shank is the lower portion of the leg — the section below the knee joint on either
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7842116Z     the fore (front) or hind (back) leg. It contains a central bone surrounded by a dense network
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7843309Z     of collagen-rich connective tissue, which transforms into gelatin during prolonged moist-heat
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7844072Z     cooking and gives braised lamb shank its characteristic sticky, unctuous sauce and
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7844955Z     fall-off-the-bone texture. The shank has relatively little intramuscular fat compared with
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7845736Z     the shoulder but abundant surface and inter-muscular fat that melts into the braising liquid.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7846494Z     Because of the high collagen content, the shank is uniquely unsuited to dry-heat methods
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7847360Z     — it must reach 85–95 °C internal temperature and be held there for 2–4 hours for collagen
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7848221Z     conversion to complete. Any reading below 80 °C will leave the meat tough regardless of cooking
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7849002Z     time. This makes the MEATER probe essential for monitoring approach to the braising window;
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7849769Z     the pull temperature is the **minimum** at which the meat has rendered sufficiently, not a
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7850733Z     precision target. The shank is universal across Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, Northern European,
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7851541Z     Central Asian, and Indian culinary traditions. Braising liquid varies from red or white wine
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7852350Z     (European), beer (Belgian/Scandinavian), cider (Norman/Swedish), saffron-tomato (Persian/Moroccan),
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7853035Z     to spiced yoghurt or lentil-thickened broth (Indian/Turkish).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7853432Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7853632Z     ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7853875Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7854397Z     ### 1. BBC Good Food — Braised Lamb Shanks (British tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7854885Z     **Source**: BBC Good Food, *bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/braised-lamb-shanks*
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7855387Z     (fetched 2026-04-24, author: Good Food magazine)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7855962Z     **Serves**: 8 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Rest**: 5 min
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7856379Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7856620Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7856880Z     - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7857134Z     - 2 tbsp olive oil
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7857414Z     - 1 onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7857727Z     - 2 carrots, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7858046Z     - Few sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7858357Z     - 3 fresh bay leaves
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7858649Z     - 4 garlic cloves, left whole
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7858960Z     - 2 tbsp plain flour
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7859290Z     - 1 tbsp tomato purée
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7859574Z     - 350 ml white wine
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7859858Z     - 500 ml lamb or chicken stock
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7860157Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7860372Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7860896Z     1. Heat oven to 200 °C / fan 180 °C / gas 6. Pour the oil into a casserole dish or roasting
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7861610Z        tray large enough to fit all the shanks. Over high heat on the hob, spend a good 10 minutes
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7862173Z        browning the lamb all over.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7862701Z     2. Remove the lamb, add the onion and carrot and cook for 10 minutes until starting to brown.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7863431Z        Stir in the herbs and garlic and cook for a few minutes more. Stir in the flour and tomato
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7864113Z        purée, season well, then pour over the wine and stock.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7864734Z     3. Return the lamb shanks to the pan. Bring to a simmer.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7865343Z     4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part of the shank, keeping the tip
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7865899Z        away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7866532Z     5. Cover with a lid or tightly with foil and cook in the oven, undisturbed, for 1½–2 hrs until
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7867070Z        lamb is tender.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7867534Z     6. Remove the lamb from the sauce and set aside. Put the pan back on the hob and bubble the
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7868380Z        sauce for a few minutes until thickened to a gravy consistency.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7869088Z     7. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F)** — at this temperature the collagen has converted to
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7869881Z        gelatin and the meat separates easily from the bone. The window is wide; holding at 90 °C
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7870717Z        for 30 minutes produces more tender results than hitting 90 °C and immediately pulling.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7871539Z     8. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (braised — collagen fully rendered,
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7872064Z        meat falling from bone).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7872354Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7872579Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7872800Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7873270Z     ### 2. Nigella Lawson — Aromatic Lamb Shank Stew (British / Middle Eastern fusion)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7873907Z     **Source**: Nigella Lawson, *Nigella Bites* (Chatto & Windus / Hyperion, 2001);
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7874651Z     nigella.com/recipes/aromatic-lamb-shank-stew (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7875360Z     **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 1½–2 hrs
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7875696Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7875924Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7876186Z     - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7876439Z     - 2 onions
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7876684Z     - 4 cloves garlic
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7876953Z     - 1 sprinkling of salt
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7877233Z     - 1 tbsp turmeric
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7877492Z     - 1 tsp ground ginger
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7877853Z     - ¼ tsp dried chilli flakes
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7878133Z     - 2 tsp ground cinnamon
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7878451Z     - ¼ tsp freshly grated nutmeg
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7878738Z     - Black pepper
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7878965Z     - 3 tbsp honey
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7879236Z     - 1 tbsp soy sauce
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7879485Z     - 3 tbsp Marsala
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7879796Z     - 6 tbsp (⅓ cup) red lentils
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7880100Z     - 6 tbsp vegetable or groundnut oil
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7880502Z     - 3 tbsp chopped pistachios or blanched almonds, to serve
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7880862Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7881070Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7881477Z     1. Put 3 tablespoons of the oil into a very large, wide, heavy-bottomed pan and warm over medium
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7882119Z        heat. Brown the lamb shanks in batches and then remove to a roasting tin to rest temporarily.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7882816Z     2. Peel the onions and garlic and process in a food processor or chop finely by hand. Add the
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7883509Z        remaining oil to the pan, and fry the onion-garlic mush until soft, sprinkling salt over to
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7884317Z        stop it catching. Stir in the turmeric, ground ginger, chilli, cinnamon and nutmeg, and
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7884822Z        season with freshly ground pepper.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7885203Z     3. Stir again, adding the honey, soy sauce and Marsala.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7885751Z     4. Put the shanks back in the pan. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7886270Z        of the largest shank, tip clear of the bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7886781Z     5. Add cold water almost to cover. Bring to the boil, then put a lid on the pan, lower the heat
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7887473Z        and simmer very gently for 1–1½ hours or until the meat is tender.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7888062Z     6. Add the red lentils and cook for about 20 minutes longer without the lid, until the lentils
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7888706Z        have softened into the sauce and the juices have reduced and thickened slightly.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7889444Z     7. **Pull at 90–95 °C (194–203 °F).** The lentil-thickened sauce signals doneness as much as
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7890146Z        the temperature; at 90 °C the meat should pull freely from the bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7890696Z     8. Toast the nuts in a dry frying pan; sprinkle onto the lamb as you serve it.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7891619Z     9. Final serving temperature: **90–95 °C / 194–203 °F** (fully braised, fall-off-bone).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7892091Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7892302Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7892517Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7892998Z     ### 3. Suzy Karadsheh — Mediterranean Braised Lamb Shanks (Mediterranean / Egyptian tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7893504Z     **Source**: Suzy Karadsheh, *The Mediterranean Dish*;
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7893951Z     themediterraneandish.com/braised-lamb-shanks/ (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7894892Z     **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2½ hrs · **Oven**: 175 °C (350 °F)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7895295Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7895517Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7895768Z     - 6 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7896073Z     - 2¼ tsp garlic powder
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7896366Z     - 1 tsp sweet Spanish paprika
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7896681Z     - 1 tsp kosher salt
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7896977Z     - 1 tsp freshly ground black pepper
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7897238Z     - ¾ tsp ground nutmeg
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7897564Z     - 2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7898106Z     - 1 medium yellow onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7898433Z     - 2 celery ribs, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7898827Z     - 3 large carrots, peeled and cut into large pieces
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7899198Z     - 450 g (1 lb) baby potatoes
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7899486Z     - 2 cups red wine
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7899746Z     - 3 cups beef broth
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7900027Z     - 1 can (800 g / 28 oz) peeled tomatoes
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7900342Z     - 2 cinnamon sticks
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7900604Z     - 4 sprigs fresh thyme
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7900893Z     - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7901167Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7901374Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7901927Z     1. Preheat oven to 175 °C (350 °F). Mix garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper and nutmeg. Pat
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7902518Z        the lamb shanks dry and season all over with the spice mix.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7903121Z     2. In a large Dutch oven over medium-high, heat 2 tbsp olive oil. Working in batches, brown
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7903792Z        the shanks until golden on all sides (about 8 minutes). Transfer to a large tray.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7904612Z     3. Pour off excess fat and return pot to medium-high heat. Add onions, celery, carrots and
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7905304Z        potatoes. Sauté until the vegetables gain some colour, 5–7 minutes.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7905932Z     4. Add red wine and scrape up the brown bits from the bottom. Cook briefly until wine reduces
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7906614Z        slightly, then add beef broth, peeled tomatoes, cinnamon sticks, thyme and rosemary.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7907286Z        Sprinkle salt and pepper. Return lamb shanks to the pot; press down to submerge. Boil
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7907808Z        10 minutes, then turn off the heat.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7908334Z     5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, keeping the tip away from the
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7908867Z        central bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7909482Z     6. Cover the pot and transfer to the hot oven for 2½ hours. Check periodically; add more liquid
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7909988Z        if needed.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7910497Z     7. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The cooking liquid will have reduced to about one-third
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7911170Z        of the original volume. The meat should pull away from the bone with light tong pressure.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7911839Z     8. Serve on Lebanese rice, polenta or orzo with the vegetables and braising sauce.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7912596Z     9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (Mediterranean braise, fall-off-bone).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7913080Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7913293Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7913506Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7914096Z     ### 4. Matprat.no — Hvitvinbraiserte lammeskanker / White-Wine Braised Lamb Shanks (Norwegian tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7915318Z     **Source**: Matprat.no (Norwegian Meat and Poultry Research Centre / Opplysningskontoret for egg og kjøtt);
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7916137Z     matprat.no/oppskrifter/gjester/hvitvinsbraiserte-lammeskanker/ (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7916797Z     **Serves**: 4 · **Cook**: 3½–4 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7917155Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7917385Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7917638Z     - 4 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7917888Z     - 1 tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7918246Z     - ½ tsp pepper
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7918499Z     - 2 cloves garlic
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7918839Z     - 1 red or yellow capsicum (paprika), cut into sticks
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7919214Z     - 3 green chillies
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7919485Z     - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7919882Z     - 2 tbsp rosé-pepper / pink peppercorns
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7920224Z     - 750 ml (7.5 dl) white wine
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7920555Z     - Salt and pepper (additional, to taste)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7920921Z     - 8 green asparagus spears (to serve)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7921288Z     - 4 sprigs fresh basil (to garnish)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7921826Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7922038Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7922416Z     1. Rub the lamb shanks with salt and pepper. Place in an ovenproof form.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7923067Z     2. Peel the garlic and cut the capsicum into sticks. Add the garlic, capsicum, chilli and
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7923875Z        rosemary to the form alongside the shanks. Sprinkle with rosé pepper and pour over the
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7924577Z        white wine.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7925134Z     3. Cover tightly with aluminium foil and cook in the oven at 150 °C for 3½–4 hours, or until
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7925666Z        the meat loosens from the bones.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7926156Z     4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7926843Z     5. Optional: remove the foil for the last few minutes and raise oven to high grill (overvarme)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7927403Z        to add colour to the shanks.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7927919Z     6. Remove the capsicum and chilli pieces from the cooking juices. Strain the juices; return
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7928635Z        capsicum and chilli. Use a hand blender to create a full-bodied sauce. Season to taste.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7929328Z     7. Peel the asparagus; cook in lightly salted water for 3 minutes, until just tender.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7930104Z     8. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F).** Norwegian culinary tradition cooks lamb shanks until
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7930870Z        they fall from the bone; the low oven temperature (150 °C) extends the collagen-conversion
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7931450Z        window and produces an exceptionally silky sauce.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7932007Z     9. Pour sauce into deep plates; place one lamb shank per plate. Garnish with basil and the
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7932534Z        lightly cooked green asparagus.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7933168Z     10. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian style).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7933671Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7933881Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7934100Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7934790Z     ### 5. Kasimir (Chefkoch.de) — Geschmorte Lammhaxen / Red-Wine Braised Lamb Shanks (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7935393Z     **Source**: Kasimir, Chefkoch.de community recipe;
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7935945Z     chefkoch.de/rezepte/566811155129665/Geschmorte-Lammhaxen.html (fetched 2026-04-24;
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7936462Z     4.8/5 stars from 55 ratings)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7936981Z     **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 45 min (+overnight marination) · **Cook**: 2½ hrs
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7937382Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7937606Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7937878Z     - 4 lamb shanks (Lammhaxen)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7938246Z     - 2 carrots (Möhren)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7938608Z     - 1 large onion (Gemüsezwiebel)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7938938Z     - 1 garlic clove (Knoblauchzehe)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7939475Z     - Tomato paste (Tomatenmark), a little
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7939835Z     - 1 glass lamb stock (Lammfond)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7940190Z     - 200 g sieved tomatoes (passierte Tomaten)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7940555Z     - 250 ml red wine (Rotwein)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7940847Z     - Olive oil
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7941129Z     - A splash balsamic vinegar (Balsamico)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7941516Z     - 1 bunch flat-leaf parsley (glatte Petersilie)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7941889Z     - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7942162Z     - Thyme, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7942462Z     - Rosemary, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7942835Z     - Cayenne pepper or Pul Biber (Turkish chilli flakes)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7943244Z     - 1 knife-tip ground cinnamon (Zimt)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7943666Z     - 1 knife-tip ground cumin (Kreuzkümmel)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7944001Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7944382Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7944833Z     1. The day before: trim the shanks of excess fat and sinews. Rub all over with thyme, rosemary,
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7945698Z        olive oil, salt and pepper. Refrigerate overnight — the dry-rub penetrates the meat and
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7946415Z        improves both flavour and crust formation.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7946965Z     2. The next day: brown the shanks all over in olive oil in a heavy braising pot. Remove and
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7947765Z        set aside. Dice the onion, garlic and carrots; sauté in the same pot until beginning to
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7948343Z        colour. Stir in the tomato paste and cook briefly.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7948921Z     3. Deglaze with red wine and reduce slightly. Add the sieved tomatoes and half the lamb stock.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7949753Z        Season with a knife-tip each of cumin and cinnamon — these must be barely perceptible; if
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7950331Z        they taste of spice the sauce is over-spiced.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7951010Z     4. Return the shanks to the pot, add a lid and braise over low heat for approximately 2½ hours.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7951593Z        Add more lamb stock if needed during cooking.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7952169Z     5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7952908Z     6. After cooking, remove the shanks and rest on a plate. Taste the sauce; adjust with cayenne
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7953613Z        or Pul Biber for heat, and a splash of balsamic vinegar to round. No additional thickening
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7954458Z        is needed — the reduced braising liquid is naturally concentrated.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7954984Z     7. Stir in freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley before serving.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7955653Z     8. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The German tradition uses no additional binding — the
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7956341Z        collagen in the shanks provides sufficient body. The author notes that serving with couscous
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7956956Z        and salad is traditional in his household.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7957605Z     9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (fully braised, German style).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7958100Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7958321Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7958539Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7959041Z     ### 6. Tasteline.com — Ugnsstekt lammlägg / Oven-braised Lamb Shank (Swedish tradition)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7959831Z     **Source**: *Det moderna köket* (The Modern Kitchen), recipe published on Tasteline.com;
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7968638Z     tasteline.com/recept/ugnsstekt-lammlagg/ (fetched 2026-04-24; 3.3/5 stars, 300 votes)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7969420Z     **Serves**: 7 · **Prep**: 15 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7969846Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7970081Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7970410Z     - 7 lamb shanks (lammlägg)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7970820Z     - 750 ml (7.5 dl) meat stock (köttbuljong)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7971251Z     - 250 ml (2.5 dl) red wine (rött vin)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7971591Z     - 6 bay leaves (lagerblad)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7972168Z     - 4 garlic cloves (vitlöksklyftor)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7972605Z     - 8 small onions (smålökar)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7972929Z     - 2 sprigs rosemary (rosmarin)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7973266Z     - 3 sprigs marjoram (mejram)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7973601Z     - 1 tbsp peppercorns (pepparkorn)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7973918Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7974139Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7974771Z     1. Set a frying pan on high heat. Add the lamb shanks and sear for approximately 2 minutes per
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7975635Z        side, or until well browned. No oil is specified — the shanks render their own fat in a hot
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7976151Z        dry pan.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7976601Z     2. Transfer the browned shanks to a roasting tin. Add the stock, wine, bay leaves, garlic,
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7977246Z        small whole onions, rosemary, marjoram and peppercorns to the tin.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7977909Z     3. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the largest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7978743Z     4. Cover the tin tightly with foil and oven-braise at 150 °C for approximately 2 hours, or
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7979574Z        until the meat is very tender (mycket mört).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7980216Z     5. **Pull at 88–90 °C (190–194 °F).** Swedish home-cooking tradition aims for complete
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7981028Z        tenderness rather than a specific pull temperature; the 2-hour window at 150 °C is designed
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7981595Z        to achieve this reliably.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7982083Z     6. Serve the shanks in deep bowls with polenta or potato-and-garlic mash (potatis- och
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7982633Z        vitlöksmos).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7983166Z     7. Final serving temperature: **88–90 °C / 190–194 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian home
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7983766Z        cooking style, from *Det moderna köket*).
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7984106Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7984452Z     ---
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7984668Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7984916Z     ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7985218Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7985469Z     | Source | Pull temperature | Method |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7985808Z     |--------|----------------|--------|
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7986357Z     | BBC Good Food (UK) | 88–92 °C | Oven braise, white wine, 1½–2 hrs at 200 °C |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7986971Z     | Nigella Lawson (UK) | 90–95 °C | Stovetop simmer, aromatic broth |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7987617Z     | The Mediterranean Dish (Egypt/US) | 88–93 °C | Dutch oven, 2½ hrs at 175 °C |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7988323Z     | Matprat.no (Norway) | 88–92 °C | Foil-covered form, white wine, 3½–4 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7989026Z     | Chefkoch.de / Kasimir (Germany) | 88–93 °C | Covered braise, red wine, 2½ hrs |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7989902Z     | Tasteline.com / Det moderna köket (Sweden) | 88–90 °C | Covered roasting tin, red wine + stock, 2 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7990499Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7991011Z     **Consensus pull**: **88–95 °C (190–203 °F)**. The braise window is wide — collagen begins
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7991808Z     converting to gelatin at around 72–74 °C but full texture transformation requires sustained
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7992608Z     time above 80 °C. All six traditions agree on a minimum of approximately 88 °C. Higher
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7993425Z     temperatures (up to 95 °C) are acceptable and produce softer, more yielding meat. Overshooting
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7994147Z     to 100 °C risks drying out the outer muscle fibres in longer braises.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7994735Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7995243Z     **Representative coding value**: **90 °C (194 °F)** as the nominal pull, with a practical
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7995784Z     range of 88–95 °C.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7996045Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7996285Z     ## Notes for cooking_data.py
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7996580Z     
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7996820Z     - `doneness`: `braised_tender`
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7997141Z     - `pull_temp_c`: 90
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7997622Z     - `pull_temp_range_c`: [88, 95]
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7998206Z     - `safety_level`: `safe` (well above all pathogen destruction thresholds)
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7998885Z     - Collagen conversion is time-dependent as well as temperature-dependent. The MEATER probe
2026-04-24T08:52:38.7999621Z       temperature reading marks entry into the braising window; actual tenderness depends on
2026-04-24T08:52:38.8000430Z       the duration at that temperature. Recommend a minimum hold time of 30–45 minutes after
2026-04-24T08:52:38.8000972Z       reaching 88 °C.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.8001434Z     - All six traditions sear the shanks before braising for Maillard flavour development.
2026-04-24T08:52:38.8002155Z     - All six use covered cooking vessels (lid, foil) to maintain steam and prevent evaporation.
