2026-04-24T08:55:04.3143642Z       ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3143899Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3144462Z       Bison (American buffalo, *Bison bison*) is a large North American bovine raised primarily on
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3145361Z       open pasture. Its steaks — most commonly cut from the loin (fillet/tenderloin), rib (entrecôte/
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3146209Z       ribeye), or striploin (sirloin) — are structurally similar to beef cuts from the same primal
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3146969Z       positions but differ decisively in fat content and flavour chemistry. Bison carries significantly
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3147744Z       less intramuscular fat than commercial beef: its marbling score is typically very low, and the
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3148501Z       external fat cap is thin. This leanness means there is very little insulating fat to slow heat
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3149228Z       penetration, so bison steaks cook faster than their beef equivalents and have a narrower
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3149613Z       window between perfectly cooked and overdone.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3149852Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3150160Z       The critical temperature rule for bison steak is the most frequently repeated piece of culinary
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3150638Z       consensus in all traditions: bison must always be served rare to medium-rare and must never be
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3151128Z       taken past medium. The lean muscle fibres of bison, deprived of intramuscular fat that would
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3151857Z       lubricate the texture, become dry, chalky, and develop a distinctly unpleasant liver-like
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3152692Z       off-flavour when cooked beyond 65 °C. This off-note — noted explicitly in German, American,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3153517Z       and French bison cooking literature — is caused by the oxidation of the comparatively high
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3154389Z       iron content in the lean, myoglobin-rich muscle at elevated temperatures. The pull temperature
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3155004Z       for a grilled bison steak is therefore 57–60 °C (medium-rare), and never above 63 °C.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3155347Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3155706Z       On the grill, bison steak performs best over direct, high heat — charcoal (Holzkohlegrill) is
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3156180Z       frequently recommended in German and North American traditions because the intense radiant
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3156645Z       heat of charcoal sears the exterior rapidly, creating Maillard crust without the interior
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3157094Z       overcooking. Gas grill or cast-iron griddle are also acceptable. Because of the leanness,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3157547Z       marinating overnight is common in German tradition: the marinade provides moisture and
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3158161Z       flavour compounds that compensate for the lack of intramuscular fat. Resting is universally
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3158651Z       recommended (5 minutes minimum) to allow the muscle fibres to relax and reabsorb juices.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3159132Z       Slow low-temperature cooking (Niedrigtemperatur) is explicitly warned against for bison steak
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3159666Z       by German sources — while it works for collagen-rich beef cuts, for lean bison steak it
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3160120Z       concentrates the liver-like off-note and dries the meat. The pan-sear + brief oven finish
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3160640Z       at 80 °C (as described in two German sources below) is the one acceptable exception, because
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3161156Z       the sear provides Maillard flavour and the short oven time (25 min at 80 °C) simply holds
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3161519Z       the meat at medium without further cooking.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3161754Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3161924Z       ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3162109Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3162548Z       ### 1. Daskochrezept.de — Gegrilltes Bison-Filet-Steak in süß-scharfer Bier-Chili-Marinade (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3163078Z       **Source**: Daskochrezept.de community recipe;
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3163486Z       daskochrezept.de/rezepte/gegrilltes-bison-filet-steak-suess-scharfer-bier-chili-marinade
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3163864Z       (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3164398Z       **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: overnight marination · **Cook**: 6–10 min · **Rest**: 5 min
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3164704Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3164871Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3165099Z       - 1 bison fillet (ca. 1 kg), trimmed and pat dry
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3165385Z       - 4 slices à 250 g
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3165597Z       - 3 garlic cloves, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3165825Z       - 1 onion, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3166185Z       - 2–3 cm fresh ginger, grated
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3166498Z       - Juice of 2 limes
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3166742Z       - 250 ml beer (pale lager or ale)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3166970Z       - 2 tbsp sugar
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3167173Z       - 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3167391Z       - 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3167597Z       - 2 tbsp neutral oil
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3167846Z       - 2–3 tbsp chipotle pepper sauce
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3168074Z       - Chili powder to taste
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3168270Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3168428Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3168729Z       1. Parieren (trim) the bison fillet if necessary (or ask the butcher). Wash and pat dry. Cut
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3169110Z          into 4 slices of approximately 250 g each.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3169483Z       2. Finely chop the garlic and onion, grate the ginger, and squeeze the limes. In a large bowl,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3169959Z          mix the beer, sugar, lime juice, onions, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, oil, ginger
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3170440Z          and chipotle pepper sauce until the marinade reaches a creamy consistency. Adjust seasoning
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3170808Z          and add chili powder to taste.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3171153Z       3. Marinate the steaks in the liquid overnight in the refrigerator. Best method: place the
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3171620Z          steaks in a resealable freezer bag, pour in the marinade, squeeze out the air before sealing,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3172015Z          and knead lightly so the marinade reaches all sides.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3172407Z       4. The next day: remove the bison steaks from the refrigerator at least 3 hours before grilling
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3172915Z          so the meat reaches room temperature. Bringing the steaks to room temperature is essential —
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3173278Z          it ensures tender, even cooking.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3173640Z       5. Insert the MEATER probe horizontally through the side of the steak into the geometric centre,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3174056Z          ensuring both ambient and meat sensors are correctly positioned.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3174789Z       6. Grill the steaks over charcoal (Holzkohlegrill) on high direct heat. Depending on thickness,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3175328Z          grill 3–5 minutes per side, turning 2–3 times total. Test doneness by pressing the meat
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3175785Z          with tongs (firm resistance = medium, slight give = medium-rare, soft = rare). Bison should
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3176224Z          always be eaten rare to at most medium-rare. **Do not cook slowly at low temperature
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3176738Z          (Niedrigtemperatur) — with bison, this ruins the flavour and produces a liver-like taste.**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3177231Z       7. **Pull at 57–60 °C (135–140 °F)** for medium-rare. Remove from grill immediately.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3177667Z       8. Rest for approximately 5 minutes loosely wrapped in aluminium foil so the meat relaxes and
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3178140Z          develops its full flavour. Even without extra sauces, the meat will be incredibly tender and
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3178628Z          juicy. Herb butter or sweet-spicy sauce pairs well; grilled vegetables or baked potatoes are
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3179076Z          good sides.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3179444Z       9. Final serving temperature: **57–60 °C / 135–140 °F** (rare to medium-rare — never beyond
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3179773Z          medium).
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3179952Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3180117Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3180284Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3180600Z       ### 2. BBC Good Food — Steak with Chimichurri Sauce (British / Argentine tradition)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3181008Z       **Source**: BBC Good Food, *bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/steak-chimichurri-sauce*
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3181361Z       (fetched 2026-06-08; from Good Food magazine, June 2012)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3181740Z       **Serves**: 2 · **Prep**: 5 min · **Cook**: 5–6 min · **Rest**: a few min
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3182012Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3182175Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3182424Z       - 2 × 125 g rib-eye or sirloin steaks
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3182736Z       - Remaining ½ tbsp olive oil (for rubbing)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3182978Z       - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3183182Z       - Fries and salad to serve
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3183386Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3183556Z       **For the chimichurri**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3183782Z       - Small bunch parsley, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3184051Z       - ½ tsp oregano (fresh or dried)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3184431Z       - 2 garlic cloves
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3184627Z       - 1 shallot, chopped
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3184859Z       - ½ tsp chilli flakes
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3185062Z       - 2 tbsp olive oil
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3185287Z       - Juice of ½ lemon
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3185484Z       - 2 tsp red wine vinegar
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3185688Z       - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3185868Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3186024Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3186342Z       1. To make the chimichurri: blitz the parsley, oregano, garlic, shallot and chilli flakes in a
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3186819Z          food processor, or chop very finely by hand. Add 2 tbsp olive oil, the lemon juice, vinegar
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3187227Z          and seasoning. Pulse to combine to a saucy consistency. Set aside.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3187694Z       2. Rub the remaining ½ tbsp olive oil and a little seasoning into the steaks on both sides.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3188172Z       3. Insert the MEATER probe horizontally through the side of the steak into the geometric centre,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3188596Z          ensuring both ambient and meat sensors are correctly positioned.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3189053Z       4. Heat a griddle pan or frying pan over high heat until very hot. Cook the steaks for 2–3
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3189495Z          minutes on each side, or until done to your liking. For a 125 g rib-eye at this thickness,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3189895Z          2 min per side produces rare; 3 min per side produces medium-rare.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3190474Z       5. **Pull at approximately 57 °C (135 °F)** for medium-rare. (Pull temperature not explicitly
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3191001Z          stated in the original recipe; 57 °C is the standard for medium-rare at this steak thickness
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3191349Z          and cooking method.)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3191674Z       6. Rest the steaks for a few minutes. Spoon over the chimichurri sauce and serve with fries
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3192001Z          and salad.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3192356Z       7. Final serving temperature: **approximately 57 °C / 135 °F** (medium-rare; applicable to
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3192728Z          bison rib-eye or sirloin at 125 g portion size).
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3192972Z

2026-04-24T08:55:04.3193615Z       ### 3. Chefkoch.de — Bisonsteak auf Paprika (German tradition, pan sear + 80 °C oven)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3193959Z       **Source**: Community recipe, Chefkoch.de;
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3194519Z       chefkoch.de/rezepte/2841491436191027/Bisonsteak-auf-Paprika.html (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3195082Z       **Serves**: 2 · **Prep**: 30 min · **Cook**: 25 min · **Oven**: 80 °C
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3195348Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3195528Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3195763Z       - 2 bison entrecôte à 250 g
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3196007Z       - 1 red bell pepper (Paprika), finely diced
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3196263Z       - 1 young red onion, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3196499Z       - 1 red chilli, sliced into rings
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3196726Z       - 3 cm fresh ginger, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3196953Z       - 2 tsp Tasmanian Leatherwood honey
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3197178Z       - 4 tbsp neutral oil
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3197386Z       - 1 tsp Maggi liquid seasoning
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3197610Z       - 1 tsp freshly ground pepper
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3197823Z       - Salt to taste
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3198000Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3198156Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3198407Z       1. Preheat the oven to 80 °C (top/bottom heat).
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3198776Z       2. Cut the bell pepper into fine cubes and the chilli into rings. Finely chop the onion and
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3199108Z          the ginger.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3199411Z       3. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan over medium heat and sweat the vegetables (onion, pepper, chilli,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3199870Z          ginger) until soft. Season with the honey, pepper and Maggi. Transfer to a flat ovenproof
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3200276Z          form and place in the 80 °C oven to keep warm.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3200692Z       4. Heat the remaining 2 tbsp oil in a heavy pan over very high heat. Sear the bison entrecôte
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3201106Z          steaks sharply on both sides until a deep Maillard crust has formed.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3201535Z       5. Insert the MEATER probe horizontally through the side of the steak into the geometric centre,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3201967Z          ensuring both ambient and meat sensors are correctly positioned.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3202440Z       6. Place the seared steaks on top of the vegetables in the oven form. Return to the 80 °C oven
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3202793Z          for 25 minutes for medium doneness.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3203173Z       7. **Pull at 60 °C (140 °F)** after the 25-minute hold at 80 °C (the recipe describes this
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3203646Z          as "medium"). The 80 °C oven hold is a gentle finish only — the sear provides both the
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3204000Z          crust and most of the cooking energy.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3204593Z       8. Final serving temperature: **60 °C / 140 °F** (medium — described as correct result of
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3204976Z          25 min at 80 °C after searing).
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3205191Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3205350Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3205509Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3205830Z       ### 4. Chefkoch.de — Bisonsteak in Rosmarin (German tradition, pan sear + 80 °C oven)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3206327Z       **Source**: Community recipe, Chefkoch.de;
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3206696Z       chefkoch.de/rezepte/2841471436190764/Bisonsteak-in-Rosmarin.html (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3207150Z       **Serves**: 1 · **Prep**: 15 min · **Cook**: 30 min · **Oven**: 80 °C
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3207415Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3207579Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3207809Z       - 250 g bison entrecôte
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3208024Z       - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3208267Z       - 5 tbsp Robusta extra-virgin olive oil
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3208493Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3208648Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3209011Z       1. Preheat the oven to 80 °C (top/bottom heat). Pull the rosemary needles from the sprigs and
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3209354Z          roughly chop them.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3209677Z       2. Put 4 tbsp olive oil and the chopped rosemary into a flat ovenproof baking dish. Place the
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3210181Z          dish in the preheated 80 °C oven to warm the oil and infuse with rosemary.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3210702Z       3. Heat the remaining 1 tbsp oil in a heavy cast-iron pan over very high heat until just
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3211185Z          smoking. Sear the bison entrecôte sharply on both sides to form a deep crust.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3211643Z       4. Insert the MEATER probe horizontally through the side of the steak into the geometric centre,
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3212072Z          ensuring both ambient and meat sensors are correctly positioned.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3212488Z       5. Remove the baking dish from the oven. Place the seared steak in the warm rosemary oil and
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3212830Z          turn to coat all sides.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3213156Z       6. Return the dish to the 80 °C oven for 25 minutes for medium doneness.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3213591Z       7. **Pull at 60 °C (140 °F)** after the 25-minute hold at 80 °C (the recipe describes this
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3214010Z          as "medium"). As in Source 3, the oven is a gentle resting and holding step rather than
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3214527Z          a primary cooking method.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3214915Z       8. Final serving temperature: **60 °C / 140 °F** (medium — consistent with Source 3; both
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3215329Z          recipes use identical sear + 25 min at 80 °C technique).
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3215590Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3215750Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3215909Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3216076Z       ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3216275Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3216452Z       | Source | Pull temperature | Method |
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3216688Z       |--------|----------------|--------|
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3217092Z       | Daskochrezept.de (Germany) | 57–60 °C | Charcoal grill, 3–5 min/side; rare to medium-rare |
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3217537Z       | BBC Good Food (UK) | ~57 °C | Griddle/pan, 2–3 min/side; medium-rare |
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3218019Z       | Chefkoch.de — Bisonsteak auf Paprika (Germany) | 60 °C | Pan sear + 25 min at 80 °C oven; medium |
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3218572Z       | Chefkoch.de — Bisonsteak in Rosmarin (Germany) | 60 °C | Pan sear + 25 min at 80 °C oven; medium |
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3218907Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3219221Z       **Consensus pull**: **57–60 °C (135–140 °F)** for rare to medium-rare; **60 °C (140 °F)**
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3219648Z       maximum for medium. All four sources agree that bison steak must not be taken past medium
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3220098Z       under any circumstances. The lean muscle fibres develop a liver-like off-flavour above
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3220596Z       approximately 63–65 °C. Slow low-temperature cooking (Niedrigtemperatur) is explicitly
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3220971Z       contraindicated for bison steak by German tradition.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3221223Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3221548Z       **Representative coding value**: **60 °C (140 °F)** as the nominal pull for medium-rare
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3222131Z       (allowing a 3 °C carryover to ~60 °C serving), with a practical range of 57–63 °C.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3222547Z       Rare: 52–54 °C. Medium-rare: 57–60 °C. Do not exceed 63 °C.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3222797Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3222969Z       ## Notes for cooking_data.py
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3223176Z       
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3223346Z       - `doneness`: `medium_rare`
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3223560Z       - `pull_temp_c`: 60
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3223768Z       - `pull_temp_range_c`: [57, 63]
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3224103Z       - `safety_level`: `medium_rare` (bison is a whole-muscle cut; surface pathogens destroyed
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3224637Z         by the sear)
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3225006Z       - Bison steak MUST NOT be cooked past medium-rare (63 °C). Above this temperature the lean
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3225472Z         muscle develops an iron-oxidation off-flavour described as "liver-like" in multiple German
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3225825Z         and North American sources.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3226189Z       - Niedrigtemperatur (sustained low-temperature cooking) is contraindicated for bison steak.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3226814Z         The pan-sear + short 80 °C oven hold (Sources 3 and 4) is acceptable only because the
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3227211Z         total internal temperature does not exceed 60 °C.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3227575Z       - Bring bison steaks to room temperature before grilling (minimum 1 hour, ideally 3 hours
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3227997Z         per Source 1) — the lean muscle cooks unevenly if cold.
2026-04-24T08:55:04.3228316Z       - Rest grilled bison steak a minimum of 5 minutes before serving.

2026-04-24T08:56:19.0103371Z       ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0103623Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0104168Z       Bison roast cuts are drawn from the same primals as beef roasting joints — most commonly the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0105174Z       shoulder (chuck/Schulter), topside (Oberschale), silverside (Unterschale), or loin (Rücken).
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0105888Z       The fillet roast (whole tenderloin) is also used in high-end German preparations. Like bison
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0106605Z       steak, bison roast is substantially leaner than equivalent beef cuts: the marbling is sparse
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0107370Z       and the outer fat layer thin, which has two important consequences. First, a bison roast dries
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0108105Z       out faster in a hot oven than a comparably sized beef joint, making temperature monitoring with
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0108883Z       a probe essential. Second, without intramuscular fat to buffer heat and provide moisture, the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0109980Z       off-flavour threshold at which overcooked meat tastes "liver-like" is reached earlier — roughly
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0110693Z       65–68 °C for a roast compared to around 63 °C for a steak.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0111084Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0111539Z       The two dominant German cooking traditions for bison roast diverge sharply in oven temperature
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0112383Z       but agree on a pull temperature of approximately 60–63 °C (medium). The high-heat tradition
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0113139Z       (200 °C, 45–60 minutes for a 1 kg joint) produces a crisper exterior but requires careful
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0113826Z       attention because the meat approaches the pull temperature quickly. The low-heat tradition
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0114867Z       (80–90 °C, 90–120 minutes) relies on the oven temperature being close to the desired internal
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0115636Z       temperature, preventing overshoot and producing an evenly pink interior across the full
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0116418Z       cross-section. Both traditions use an initial sharp sear to develop a Maillard crust before
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0117101Z       the oven phase, since at low oven temperatures browning will not occur.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0117549Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0117982Z       Unlike bison steak, a large roast does carry some collagen from the connective tissue that
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0118827Z       surrounds muscle groups — particularly in shoulder or topside cuts. However, bison roast is
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0119667Z       not a braise candidate: the collagen content is insufficient to benefit from the 85–95 °C
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0120425Z       sustained-hold window required for full collagen conversion, and the lean muscle would be
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0121285Z       ruined at those temperatures. The correct window is medium (60–65 °C), where the muscle is
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0122038Z       still moist and pink throughout. Resting is critical for roasts: a minimum of 10 minutes
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0122756Z       allows the thermal gradient within the joint to equalise and the muscle fibres to reabsorb
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0123510Z       juices. A mozzarella-mustard herb crust, applied before the oven phase, is a distinctive
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0124324Z       German preparation that acts as an insulating layer and baste, partially compensating for
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0124833Z       the low fat content.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0125092Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0125313Z       ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0125559Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0125977Z       ### 1. Chefkoch.de — Bisonbraten (German tradition, high-heat oven)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0126457Z       **Source**: acigrand, Chefkoch.de community recipe;
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0126973Z       chefkoch.de/rezepte/234021095857919/Bisonbraten.html (fetched 2026-06-08;
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0127444Z       4.5/5 stars from 2 ratings)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0128133Z       **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 45–60 min · **Oven**: 200 °C
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0128532Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0128768Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0129047Z       - 1 kg bison roast
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0129394Z       - Salt, pepper, dried thyme, dried rosemary
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0129765Z       - 1 onion, diced
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0130049Z       - 2 garlic cloves, diced
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0130337Z       - 2 cardamom pods
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0130609Z       - 5 juniper berries
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0130873Z       - 3 cloves
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0131208Z       - 500 ml stock (Brühe)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0131498Z       - 200 ml red wine
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0131768Z       - Olive oil for searing
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0132153Z       - Sauce binder (Soßenbinder), optional
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0132485Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0132751Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0133210Z       1. Wash the bison roast and pat thoroughly dry. Season all over with thyme, rosemary, salt and
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0133956Z          pepper.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0134724Z       2. Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven (Bräter) over high heat on the hob. Sear the seasoned roast
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0135324Z          on all sides until a deep brown crust has formed.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0136029Z       3. Dice the onion and garlic. Add to the Dutch oven alongside the roast and sauté briefly until
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0136585Z          beginning to colour.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0137082Z       4. Pour over the red wine and stock. Lightly crush the cardamom pods and add them with the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0137736Z          juniper berries and cloves to the braising liquid. Stir to combine.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0138396Z       5. Insert the MEATER probe into the thickest part of the roast, ensuring the tip is in the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0139089Z          geometric centre and does not touch the sides of the Dutch oven or any bone.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0139873Z       6. Put a lid on the Dutch oven and roast in the oven at 200 °C for 45–60 minutes, basting
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0140607Z          occasionally with some of the cooking stock. Cooking time varies by desired doneness: 45
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0141350Z          minutes produces a pinker result; 60 minutes produces a more through-cooked result.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0142120Z       7. **Pull at 60–63 °C (140–145 °F)** for medium (the centre will be tender pink). At 200 °C
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0142901Z          the roast will carryover approximately 2–3 °C after pulling; accounting for rest the final
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0143562Z          internal temperature will be approximately 63–65 °C.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0144103Z       8. Remove the roast from the Dutch oven and keep warm. Strain the cooking juices through a
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0145034Z          sieve into a saucepan. Bring to the boil and season with salt and pepper. Thicken with sauce
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0145610Z          binder if desired. Serve sliced with the sauce.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0146334Z       9. Final serving temperature: **63–65 °C / 145–149 °F** (medium, after 10 min rest; tender
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0146855Z          pink throughout).
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0147136Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0147361Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0147586Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0148193Z       ### 2. Chefkoch.de — Bisonbraten mit Mozzarella-Senfkruste (German tradition, low-heat oven with herb crust)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0148853Z       **Source**: backfee_65, Chefkoch.de community recipe;
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0149307Z       chefkoch.de/rezepte/2763771428258460/Bisonbraten-mit-Mozzarella-Senfkruste.html
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0149678Z       (fetched 2026-06-08; 5/5 stars from 2 ratings)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0150068Z       **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 30 min · **Cook**: 90–120 min · **Oven**: 90 °C
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0150345Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0150512Z       **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0150695Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0150855Z       *For the roast*
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0151040Z       - 1.3 kg bison fillet
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0151487Z       - 3 tbsp neutral oil, or 50 g clarified butter (Butterschmalz)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0151785Z       - 1 medium onion, cut into rough rings
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0152025Z       - 1 buffalo mozzarella ball
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0152251Z       - Salt, pepper, sweet paprika
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0152459Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0152635Z       *For the herb-mustard crust*
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0152851Z       - 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0153117Z       - 2 tbsp sweet mustard (süßer Senf)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0153411Z       - 2 tbsp coarse-grain mustard (körniger Senf)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0153660Z       - 4 garlic cloves
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0153875Z       - ½ tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0154066Z       - 1 tsp dried tarragon
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0154496Z       - 1 tsp roughly crushed peppercorns
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0154735Z       - 1 tsp dried oregano
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0154938Z       - 1 tsp dried rosemary
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0155237Z       - 50 g blue cheese (Blauschimmelkäse), crumbled
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0155643Z       - Breadcrumbs and oil as needed to adjust consistency
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0155893Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0156060Z       *For the sauce*
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0156338Z       - ⅓ bunch soup vegetables (Suppengrün), finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0156605Z       - 1 medium onion, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0156845Z       - 2 garlic cloves, crushed
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0157082Z       - 5 tbsp balsamic cream (Balsamico-Creme)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0157324Z       - 5 tbsp dry sherry
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0157518Z       - 200 ml stock
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0157701Z       - 2 bay leaves
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0157905Z       - 1 tbsp juniper berries, lightly crushed
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0158148Z       - 1 tsp whole black peppercorns
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0158373Z       - 50 g blue cheese, crumbled
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0158654Z       - 1 tbsp red berries (Rote Beeren / lingonberries or redcurrants)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0158990Z       - 1 tbsp green pepper (Grüner Pfeffer)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0159231Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0159393Z       **Method**
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0159695Z       1. Remove the bison fillet from the refrigerator several hours before cooking and allow to
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0160106Z          come fully to room temperature. Rinse under cold water and pat dry.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0160519Z       2. Heat the oil or clarified butter in a heavy pan over very high heat. Sear the fillet slowly
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0161050Z          on all sides, 3–4 minutes per side, taking care not to let the surface burn dark. Meanwhile,
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0161585Z          preheat the oven to 90 °C (top/bottom heat) and place a flat ovenproof form (e.g. the lid
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0161983Z          of the Dutch oven) inside to warm. Cut the onion into rough rings.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0162444Z       3. For the mustard-herb crust: mince the 4 garlic cloves finely, then mash with ½ tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0162902Z          using a fork until a paste forms. Stir in the Dijon mustard, sweet mustard, coarse-grain
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0163372Z          mustard, tarragon, crushed peppercorns, oregano and rosemary. Crumble the blue cheese with
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0163838Z          a fork and stir into the mixture. The paste should be spreadable but not runny. If too thin,
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0164411Z          add breadcrumbs to firm; if too thick, add a little oil to loosen.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0164758Z       4. Slice the mozzarella into thin rounds and set aside.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0165150Z       5. Remove the seared fillet from the pan and pat off excess oil. Season with salt, pepper and a
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0165627Z          little sweet paprika. Place the onion rings in the warmed ovenproof form and set the fillet
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0165999Z          on top of them (the onions act as a rack).
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0166435Z       6. Coat the fillet all around with the mustard-herb paste — it should adhere. Lay the mozzarella
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0166939Z          slices over the top of the coated fillet.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0167306Z       7. Insert the MEATER probe into the thickest part of the roast, ensuring the tip is in the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0167711Z          geometric centre and does not touch bone or the baking dish.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0168157Z       8. Place the form in the preheated 90 °C oven and roast for approximately 90–120 minutes.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0168626Z       9. **Pull at 60 °C (140 °F).** Switch off the oven and allow the roast to rest inside the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0169113Z          switched-off oven for a further 10 minutes — the internal temperature will continue to rise
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0169608Z          2–3 °C to approximately 62–63 °C. At this temperature the meat is tender and pink
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0170093Z          throughout (medium). Do not exceed 65 °C or the bison will begin to dry and the off-flavour
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0170438Z          threshold will be approached.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0170857Z       10. About 20 minutes before pulling (when the probe reads approximately 57 °C), begin the sauce
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0171450Z           and prepare side dishes. The author serves Bratkartoffeln (pan-fried potatoes) and mangetout.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0171996Z       11. For the sauce: reheat the pan drippings from the sear. Sauté the finely chopped onions,
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0172476Z           garlic and soup vegetables until beginning to brown. Add the juniper berries, bay leaves and
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0173001Z           peppercorns. Deglaze with balsamic cream and allow to evaporate almost completely. Add the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0173478Z           sherry and reduce. Deglaze with the stock and bring to the boil; reduce until the pan is
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0173926Z           nearly dry. Refill with 200 ml water and simmer on low heat for ~20 minutes (do not reduce
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0174675Z           further — this repeated reduce-and-refill process builds deep flavour). Stir in the crumbled
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0175169Z           blue cheese until dissolved. After 20 minutes, remove bay leaves and blend the sauce with a
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0175644Z           hand blender; press through a fine sieve. Add the red berries and green pepper and simmer on
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0176009Z           very low heat for a final 20 minutes.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0176341Z       12. Slice the rested roast and serve with the sauce, Bratkartoffeln and mangetout.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0176832Z       13. Final serving temperature: **62–63 °C / 144–145 °F** (medium — tender pink interior;
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0177272Z           result of pull at 60 °C plus 10 min rest in switched-off 90 °C oven).
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0177557Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0177721Z       ---
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0177887Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0178060Z       ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0178261Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0178442Z       | Source | Pull temperature | Method |
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0178679Z       |--------|----------------|--------|
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0179131Z       | Chefkoch.de — Bisonbraten / acigrand (Germany) | 60–63 °C | Lidded Dutch oven, 45–60 min at 200 °C; medium |
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0179782Z       | Chefkoch.de — Mozzarella-Senfkruste / backfee_65 (Germany) | 60 °C (rest to 62–63 °C) | Pan sear + 90–120 min at 90 °C; medium |
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0180186Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0180518Z       **Consensus pull**: **60–63 °C (140–145 °F)**. Both German sources converge on medium as the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0181018Z       correct doneness for bison roast. The high-heat recipe implies a pull around 60–63 °C (the
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0181521Z       stated 45–60 min at 200 °C for a 1 kg joint). The low-heat recipe explicitly states "pull at
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0181993Z       60 °C, rest 10 min, will reach 62–63 °C." Both describe the result as tender and pink
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0182427Z       throughout (medium). Consistent with bison steak guidance, the off-flavour threshold is
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0183081Z       approached above approximately 65–68 °C; therefore the absolute maximum safe pull is 63 °C
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0183533Z       with a maximum serving temperature of 65 °C after rest.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0183783Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0184108Z       **Representative coding value**: **63 °C (145 °F)** as the nominal pull, with a practical
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0184743Z       range of 60–65 °C. This is higher than the bison steak pull (57–60 °C) because a roast
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0185185Z       does not carryover as quickly per gram of mass as a thin steak, and the pull-at-60-then-rest
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0185625Z       approach in Source 2 lands at 62–63 °C serving temperature.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0185888Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0186061Z       ## Notes for cooking_data.py
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0186267Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0186437Z       - `doneness`: `medium`
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0186645Z       - `pull_temp_c`: 63
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0186855Z       - `pull_temp_range_c`: [60, 65]
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0187205Z       - `safety_level`: `medium` (whole-muscle; surface pathogens destroyed by searing; internal
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0187772Z         at 60–63 °C is above the minimum safe temperature for beef/bison)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0188211Z       - Bison roast must not exceed 65 °C internal temperature. Above this threshold the lean
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0188658Z         muscle dries out and develops the same iron-oxidation off-flavour ("liver-like") noted for
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0188993Z         bison steak.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0189289Z       - Both sources sear first before oven cooking, regardless of oven temperature. The sear
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0189696Z         creates the Maillard crust that cannot form at low oven temperatures.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0190158Z       - Rest is mandatory: minimum 10 minutes after pulling. The low-heat recipe (90 °C oven)
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0190575Z         exploits residual oven heat by resting the roast in the switched-off oven.
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0191058Z       - Slow braising (85–95 °C sustained hold for collagen conversion) is contraindicated for
2026-04-24T08:56:19.0191499Z         bison roast — the lean muscle is ruined at those temperatures.

2026-04-24T08:56:36.3663329Z       ## FILE 1: `/home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/bison/bison_steak-grill.md`
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3664065Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3664504Z       ### VERIFIED SOURCES (all personally fetched):
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3664752Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3665286Z       **Source 1**: Daskochrezept.de — Gegrilltes Bison-Filet-Steak in süß-scharfer Bier-Chili-Marinade (German tradition)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3666010Z       **URL**: https://www.daskochrezept.de/rezepte/gegrilltes-bison-filet-steak-suess-scharfer-bier-chili-marinade (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3666808Z       **Method** (from page content, translated from German):
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3667499Z       1. Parieren the bison fillet if necessary (better to have the butcher do it), wash and pat dry. Cut 4 slices of 250g each.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3668759Z       2. Finely chop garlic and onion, grate ginger, squeeze limes. In a large bowl, mix beer, sugar, lime juice, onions, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, oil, ginger and chipotle pepper sauce until the marinade has a creamy consistency. Adjust seasoning, add chili powder to taste.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3670255Z       3. Marinate the steaks in the liquid overnight in the refrigerator. Tip: Best in a freezer bag — put the meat in, add marinade, squeeze out the air before sealing. Knead lightly so marinade reaches all sides.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3671293Z       4. The next day: take the bison meat out of the refrigerator at least 3 hours before grilling. The meat should have reached room temperature before cooking — then it will be nicely tender.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3673821Z       5. Best method: grill the steaks over charcoal (Holzkohlegrill), depending on thickness, for a total of 3-5 minutes from each side. Turn 2-3 times during grilling, and occasionally test with tongs how done the meat is; bison should always be eaten "rare" to at most "medium-rare" and should never be cooked for too long at too low a temperature, as it will develop a liver-like taste. Then rest for about 5 minutes wrapped in aluminum foil before serving, so the meat relaxes and develops its full flavor. When preparing in a pan or oven: also note that slow-cooking/Niedrigtemperatur should NOT be used. While it works well for other meats, with bison it ruins the flavor. Better to sear sharply — rest — then briefly re-heat on high heat.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3676123Z       6. The meat will now be incredibly tender and juicy, even without extra sauces. Herb butter or sweet-spicy sauces pair well. Grilled vegetables or baked potatoes are good sides.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3676650Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3677184Z       **Key temperature insight**: Bison should ALWAYS be rare to at most medium-rare. Never cook slowly at low temperatures (no Niedrigtemperatur). Liver-like off-flavors develop when overcooked.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3677750Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3678099Z       **Source 2**: BBC Good Food — Steak with chimichurri sauce (British / Argentine tradition)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3678571Z       **URL**: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/steak-chimichurri-sauce (fetched 2026-06-08)

Add 2 tbsp of the olive oil, the lemon juice, vinegar and some seasoning, and pulse to combine everything to a saucy consistency.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3684484Z       2. Rub the remaining oil and a little seasoning into the steaks. Heat a griddle or frying pan and cook the steaks for 2-3 mins on each side or until done to your liking. Rest for a few mins, then spoon over the sauce and serve with fries and salad.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3685584Z       **Note**: This recipe uses griddle/pan, 2-3 min per side for rib-eye or sirloin. Pull temp not explicitly stated — medium-rare at this thickness is approximately 57°C.

2026-04-24T08:56:36.3705522Z       **URL**: https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/234021095857919/Bisonbraten.html (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3705960Z       **Author**: acigrand · **Rating**: 4.5/5 from 2 ratings
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3706328Z       **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Total**: 20 min (oven time ~45-60 min)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3706605Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3706775Z       **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3706971Z       - 1 kg bison roast
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3707179Z       - Salt, pepper, thyme, rosemary
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3707397Z       - 1 onion
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3707575Z       - 2 garlic cloves
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3707765Z       - 2 cardamom pods
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3707967Z       - 5 juniper berries
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3708167Z       - 3 cloves
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3708390Z       - 500 ml stock (Brühe)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3708592Z       - 200 ml red wine
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3708769Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3708949Z       **Method** (translated from German):
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3710353Z       1. Wash the meat and pat dry. Season with thyme, rosemary, salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven (Bräter) and sear the meat on all sides. Dice the onion and garlic, add to the meat and sauté briefly. Pour over with red wine and stock. Crush cardamom pods and add with the remaining spices to the broth. Put a lid on the Dutch oven and roast in the oven at 200°C for 45-60 minutes (depending on how through you want it), basting occasionally with some stock.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3711999Z       2. After the cooking time, keep the roast warm. Strain the cooking juices through a sieve. Bring to the boil, season with pepper and salt. Thicken with sauce binder if desired. This dish is also suitable for the Tupperware Mikro-Plus (microwave cooker).
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3712697Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3713355Z       **Key note**: "45-60 min (je nach dem wie durch es sein soll)" — the time varies by preferred doneness. No explicit probe temperature stated. At 200°C, a 1kg roast reaches ~60°C internal in approximately 45 min.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3713933Z

2026-04-24T08:56:36.3715010Z       **URL**: https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/2763771428258460/Bisonbraten-mit-Mozzarella-Senfkruste.html (fetched 2026-06-08)
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3715532Z       **Author**: backfee_65 · **Rating**: 5/5 from 2 ratings
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3715888Z       **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 30 min · **Cook**: 2 hours · **Oven**: 90°C
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3716149Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3716311Z       **Ingredients**:
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3716504Z       - 1.3 kg bison fillet
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3716843Z       - 3 tbsp oil or 50g clarified butter
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3717084Z       - 1 medium onion, in rough rings
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3717310Z       - 1 buffalo mozzarella
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3717513Z       - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3717706Z       **For the herb crust**:
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3717911Z       - 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3718128Z       - 2 tbsp sweet and coarse-grain mustard
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3718354Z       - 4 garlic cloves
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3718571Z       - ½ tsp salt
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3718752Z       - 1 tsp tarragon
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3718956Z       - 1 tsp roughly crushed peppercorns
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3719185Z       - 1 tsp oregano
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3719377Z       - 1 tsp rosemary
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3719564Z       - 50g blue cheese
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3719761Z       - Oil and breadcrumbs as needed
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3720056Z       **For the sauce**:
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3720414Z       - ⅓ bunch soup vegetables, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3720750Z       - 1 medium onion, finely chopped
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3721083Z       - 2 garlic cloves
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3721274Z       - 5 tbsp balsamic cream
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3721476Z       - 5 tbsp sherry
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3721656Z       - 200 ml stock
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3721840Z       - 2 bay leaves
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3722025Z       - 1 tbsp crushed juniper berries
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3722248Z       - 1 tsp whole peppercorns
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3722455Z       - 50g blue cheese
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3722640Z       - 1 tbsp red berries
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3722838Z       - 1 tbsp green pepper
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3723029Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3723205Z       **Method** (translated from German):
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3723659Z       1. Take the meat out of the refrigerator a few hours before cooking and let it come to room temperature. Rinse under cold water and pat dry.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3725183Z       2. Heat oil and clarified butter very hot and sear the meat slowly from all sides 3-4 min per side — take care that the meat doesn't get too dark. Meanwhile preheat the oven to 90°C, warming a flat form (e.g. the lid of the Dutch oven) inside. Cut 1 onion into rough rings.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3726657Z       3. For the mustard marinade: cut 4 garlic cloves small and mix with ½ tsp salt using a fork, then stir in the other ingredients. Crush the blue cheese with a fork and stir into the marinade. The paste should be spreadable but not too runny. If too thin, add breadcrumbs; if needed, add some oil.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3727455Z       4. Cut the mozzarella into thin slices.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3729269Z       5. Remove the meat from the pan, pat off some of the oil and season with salt, pepper and paprika. Place onion rings in the warmed form and set the meat on them. Coat all around with the mustard marinade — it should stick. Place mozzarella slices on top of the marinade and insert a meat thermometer into the center of the meat. Place the form in the preheated oven at 90°C and roast for approximately 90-120 min. **When the core temperature is at 60°C, switch off the oven and rest the meat for another 10 min — the roast will continue to cook about 2-3°C. Then the meat will be tender pink inside (medium).**
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3732941Z       6. For the sauce: reheat the pan drippings and sauté finely chopped onions, garlic and soup vegetables. Add juniper berries, bay leaves and peppercorns. Once the onions start to brown, deglaze with balsamic cream and let evaporate. Then add sherry and reduce. Deglaze with stock, bring to boil and reduce until nothing remains. Refill with 200ml water and simmer on low heat for ~20 min (do not reduce further — this repeated process creates very intense flavor). Stir crushed blue cheese into the simmering sauce until dissolved. After 20 min remove bay leaves and blend all ingredients in a mixer, push through a sieve. Add red berries and green pepper, simmer on very low heat for another 20 min.
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3735390Z       7. About 20 min before the meat is done (at ~57°C core): prepare the side dishes (the author served Bratkartoffeln and mangetout).
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3735818Z       
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3736201Z       **Key temperature detail**: Pull at 60°C, rest 10 min, finish at ~62-63°C. "Tender pink inside (medium)."
2026-04-24T08:56:36.3736558Z

2026-04-24T08:56:36.3737197Z       - Bison roast oven: pull temp 63°C, range 60-65°C

2026-04-24T08:56:44.7915266Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7915436Z     ## How to Make Asun at Home
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7915645Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7916449Z     The smoking process isn’t so easy in a standard home kitchen, so I’ve created an easier version. If you can’t get hold of traditionally smoked goat meat, use what you can get. It tastes just as good as the smoked version, especially if you add smoked paprika!
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7917150Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7917699Z     To tone down the heat, replace some of the hot peppers with red and green bell peppers. A little sweetness from the red bell pepper goes way further. It’s a must-try!
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7918189Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7918737Z     Serve this aromatic, sizzling asun simply with oven-baked or fried plantains. You’ll be singing for joy! And if you’re doing this for the holidays, Merry Christmas!!!!
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7919267Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7919445Z     ## How to Make Asun Spicy Roast Goat
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7919669Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7920164Z     ![Boil the meat, then broil it for extra flavor. Reverse sears are coming back in style.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-1-4.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7920716Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7921479Z     1.  **Boil** the smoked goat meat with salt, pepper, bouillon powder, onion, and as little water as possible in a medium pot until tender. (I used a pressure cooker to speed up the process.) _(Photos 1-3)_
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7922063Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7922515Z     ![Finish broiling, saute aromatics, add the meat back in, and add the spice.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-5-6.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7923028Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7923559Z     1.  **Broil** – Remove the goat meat, place it on a lined baking tray, and broil it for about 10 minutes, rotating every few minutes until browned. _(Photos 4-5)_
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7924731Z     2.  **Sauté** the onions, garlic, and ginger in a bit of oil for about a minute. Then add the tomatoes, white pepper, paprika, green onions, curry powder, scotch bonnet pepper, and bouillon. Continue cooking for 5-7 minutes. _(Photo 6)_
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7925609Z     3.  **Add** roasted goat and bell pepper, stir for about 3 minutes, adjust the seasonings to your preferences, and serve hot. _(Photos 7-8)_
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7926037Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7926513Z     ![A skillet full of spicy smoked goat meat called asun in some parts of Africa.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-3.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7927138Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7927305Z     ## Tips and Notes
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7927489Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7927907Z     *   If you can’t find smoked goat meat (which is usually frozen), you can make this with fresh, with or without the skin.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7928639Z     *   If you wish to grill the meat in an open fire, you’ll need to steam it first if it’s a female, since they tend to be tougher. There’s no need to steam a buck.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7929176Z     *   When broiling the meat, you may want to grease the tray lightly.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7929551Z     *   A food processor or blender saves time when chopping the spices into bits.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7930036Z     *   When adding the hot peppers to the meat, make sure that they only wilt a little to create authentic, fresh heat.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7930421Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7930603Z     ## Make-Ahead and Storage
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7930807Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7931236Z     You can make this the day before, keep it in the fridge, and reheat just before serving. You could also freeze it, then thaw it overnight in the refrigerator.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7931703Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7932213Z     While I don’t usually have leftovers, they do make me happy. Loading up the freezer in serving-size packages lets me create a special meal on a dull day.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7932680Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7933080Z     ![Serving up asun with fried sweet plantains.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-4.jpg)
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7933511Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7933679Z     ## What to Serve With Asun
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7933878Z     
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7935002Z     This recipe goes great with [fried sweet plantains](https://www.africanbites.com/fried-plantains/) or [kelewele](https://www.africanbites.com/kelewele-or-alocospicy-fried-plantains/), but [jollof rice](https://www.africanbites.com/jellof-riceoven-baked/) is also a great addition to the meal. [Cassava pone](https://www.africanbites.com/cassava-pone/) makes a satisfying dessert.
2026-04-24T08:56:44.7936057Z

Slow roast at 160C for 5 hours with veggie stock, basting occasionally, until meat is tender and pulls apart easily.\n  3. You can generally substitute one for the other using the same method, though goat is leaner and a bit gamier, so it benefits from slower cooking and moist heat.\n- Common Mediterranean flavors for both are garlic, rosemary, oregano, olive oil, lemon, and sometimes wine or pomegranate molasses.\n\n### Quick Comparison:\n\n- **Goat meat:** Leaner, gamier, often tougher (better for slow roasting, braising).\n- **Lamb meat:** Fattier, milder, more tender (good for both quick roasting and long, slow cooking).\n\nBoth meats work beautifully with Mediterranean flavors!\n\nIf you want a specific step-by-step recipe, let me know and I can adapt or condense one for you.","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":1132,"end_index":1144,"url_citation":{"title":"Greek roasted goat (or lamb) leg recipe - The Hungry Bites","url":"https://www.thehungrybites.com/greek-roasted-goat-or-lamb-leg/"}},{"text":"","start_index":1739,"end_index":1751,"url_citation":{"title":"Perfect Roasted Leg of Lamb (No-Fail Recipe) | The Mediterranean Dish","url":"https://www.themediterraneandish.com/mediterranean-style-leg-of-lamb-recipe-with-potatoes/"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"site:themediterraneandish.com goat recipe OR lamb goat","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Athemediterraneandish.com+goat+recipe+OR+lamb+goat"}],"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:57:38.0062073Z

2026-04-24T08:57:38.0065713Z

2026-04-24T08:57:38.0066159Z

Flyt til indirekte varme, og grill færdig, evt. med låg, indtil kernetemperaturen er ca. 54-57°C.\n4. Lad hvile 5 minutter.\n\n---\n\n**TIP:** Rensdyrkød er meget magert, så det må ikke blive gennemstegt, da det ellers bliver tørt. Chili-Honig, Wildkräuter oder Bier-Senf; siehe unten).\n- Die Steaks über Nacht marinieren.\n- Kurz grillen oder in der Pfanne braten, nicht ganz durchgaren – so bleiben sie saftig.\n- Tipp: \"Sie müssen nicht ganz durch sein, so bleiben sie saftiger. Man kann die Steaks auch in der Pfanne braten.\"\n\n**Beispiel-Marinade (Wildkräuter):**\n- 10 EL Olivenöl\n- Lorbeerblätter, Wacholderbeeren\n- Etwas Rosmarin, Thymian, Salz, Pfeffer\n- 4 Knoblauchzehen gehackt\n\n**Zubereitung**:\n1. Steaks mindestens einige Stunden, besser über Nacht, in der Marinade kalt stellen und durchziehen lassen.\n2. Aus der Marinade nehmen, grobe Kräuter abstreifen.\n3. Grill: Auf sehr heißer Glut, je nach Dicke 2–3 Minuten pro Seite grillen.\n   Pfanne: Mit etwas Öl stark erhitzen, Steaks pro Seite 2–3 Minuten scharf anbraten, dann Hitze reduzieren und je nach gewünschtem Gargrad noch einige Minuten ziehen lassen.\n4. Vor dem Servieren 3–5 Minuten ruhen lassen.\n\n**Diese Marinaden- und Zubereitungstipps lassen sich hervorragend für Rentierfleisch verwenden**. Das Fleisch ist mager, daher nicht zu lange garen! Dazu passt klassisch: Preiselbeeren, gebratene Pilze oder Kartoffeln.\n\nVollständiges Rezept und weitere Details findest du hier:  \n[Reh- oder Hirschsteaks mit verschiedenen Marinaden, auch für den Grill](https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/1124511215077335/Reh-oder-Hirschsteaks-mit-verschiedenen-Marinaden.html)【3:5†source】. \n\nWenn du speziell nach dänischen Rezepten suchst (madensverden.dk), dort sind derzeit keine passgenauen Treffer für Rentiersteak per Grill oder Pfanne gelistet.","annotations":[{"text":"","start_index":1883,"end_index":1895,"url_citation":{"title":"Reh - oder Hirschsteaks mit verschiedenen Marinaden","url":"https://www.chefkoch.de/rezepte/1520541257290915/Reh-oder-Hirschsteaks-mit-verschiedenen-Marinaden.html"}}]},"bing_searches":[{"text":"reindeer steak recipe grill OR pan sear site:madensverden.dk OR site:chefkoch.de","url":"https://www.bing.com/search?q=reindeer+steak+recipe+grill+OR+pan+sear+site%3Amadensverden.dk+OR+site%3Achefkoch.de"}],"annotations":null}

2026-04-24T08:59:07.2485584Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:07.2487309Z         Lay the venison on a board. Sprinkle some of the black pepper and a little salt on each side, pressing the pepper into the steaks. Heat the remaining oil in a pan. When it has a shimmering surface, add the steaks, 2 at a time. Cook for 2 mins on each side for rare, 3 mins for medium and 4 mins for well done. When cooked, return them all to the pan and pour over the sauce. Warm for 1 min, sprinkle with parsley. Serve with the potatoes and peas, if you like, and any extra sauce spooned over.

2026-04-24T08:59:21.3808352Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3808505Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3808672Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3808824Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3808993Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3810403Z         Soak the shallots in boiling water for 5 mins, drain, then peel – this makes it a lot easier to remove the skins. While they are soaking, slice the rest of the vegetables. Heat a large, wide flameproof casserole dish, then add the lardons. Fry for 5 mins or until the fat has run from the meat, then tip in the vegetables and cook for 10 mins until golden and starting to soften. Tip into a bowl.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3811955Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3812124Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3812295Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3813204Z         Toss the rabbit meat with the flour, then tap off the excess. Heat half the butter and oil in the dish, then brown half the rabbit for 10 mins until golden all over. Add a good splash of water, scrape around the dish and tip the juices into a jug. Repeat with the second batch of rabbit, but use the wine to deglaze the pan this time, letting it reduce by about a third.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3814467Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3814629Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3814795Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3816641Z         Add the rabbit and the vegetables to the wine, pour in the stock, stir in the herbs and bring to a simmer. Cover, leaving just a small gap for steam to escape, then simmer for 1½-2 hrs or until the meat falls away from the bones. If you like a thicker sauce to your stew, you can lift out the meat and vegetables and boil the sauce to thicken it a little. To finish, heat the knob of butter in a frying pan and cook the mushrooms with salt, pepper and a few thyme leaves over a high heat until browned. Spoon these over the stew, sprinkle with thyme leaves and bring to the table.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3818060Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.3818216Z

2026-04-24T08:59:21.5032611Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5033003Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5033198Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5033416Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5034808Z         Heat the oil in a [frying pan](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/content/top-five-non-stick-frying-pans), cook the venison for 5 mins, then turn over and cook for 3-5 mins more, depending on how rare you like it and the thickness of the meat (cook for 5-6 mins on each side for well done). Lift the meat from the pan and set aside to rest.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5036119Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5036324Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5036537Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.5037587Z         Add the balsamic vinegar to the pan, then pour in the stock, redcurrant jelly and garlic. Stir over quite a high heat to blend everything together, then add the blackberries and carry on cooking until they soften. Serve with the venison, celeriac mash (see below) and broccoli.

2026-04-24T08:59:21.9971579Z     *   Stuffed saddle of venison with prunes & brioche
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9971817Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9972005Z     [Skip to ingredients](#ingredients-list)
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9972225Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9972380Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9972548Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9972715Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9973035Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9973834Z         Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. To make the stuffing, melt the butter in a shallow pan, then fry the onion, garlic, prunes and bacon until the onion is translucent and the bacon is slightly coloured. Add the brioche and cook until combined and broken down, then add the sage, season and remove from heat.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9974938Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9975104Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9975279Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9977091Z         Put a sheet of baking parchment on a sheet of foil (both should be larger than your venison). Arrange four pieces of string across the kitchen counter and put the foil and parchment on top – this will help the venison to keep its shape. Lay another four pieces of string across the parchment. Lay the pancetta on top of the paper and string, slightly overlapping the slices so that they cover an area roughly the size of your opened-out venison.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9978932Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9979172Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9979417Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9980077Z         Lay the venison on top of the pancetta, skin-side down, then spoon the stuffing down the centre, patting it with your hands to make it into a sausage shape.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9980835Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9981043Z     *   ### step 4
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9981213Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9982381Z         Fold in the sides of the venison, along with the pancetta, so they touch or slightly overlap – use the foil and baking parchment to help you. Tie the strings around the venison, making sure that the foil and parchment enclose the meat but stay outside it. Fold in the ends and tie the other pieces of string tightly around the outside of the parcel to hold it in shape.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9983375Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9983533Z     *   ### step 5
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9983699Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9984825Z         Put the venison on a baking sheet or in a roasting tin. Roast for 40-50 mins or until a meat thermometer pushed into the joint reaches 60C, for medium rare meat. Rest for 10 mins, then remove and discard the foil and parchment. Brush the venison all over with butter and return to the oven for a final 10 mins. Leave to rest for 10-15 mins before removing the string and slicing.
2026-04-24T08:59:21.9985776Z

2026-04-24T08:59:34.6004422Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:34.6004630Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T08:59:34.6004859Z

2026-04-24T08:59:34.6006357Z

2026-04-24T08:59:34.6006687Z         
2026-04-24T08:59:34.6007165Z         Add the onions or shallots and the garlic, then stir well and roast for another 15 mins, stirring again halfway through. Sprinkle over the remaining thyme before serving.

2026-04-24T08:59:48.6565465Z     Die Kängurusteaks oder Filets mit Öl einreiben und mit schwarzem Pfeffer bestreuen. Diesen etwas ins Fleisch drücken. In einer Schüssel ca. 45 Min. marinieren. (Nicht mehr in den Kühlschrank stellen.)
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6566083Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6566238Z     2
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6566390Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6566856Z     Die Kartoffeln schälen und der Länge nach halbieren. Die Kartoffelhälften in 3 - 4 Spalten schneiden und bereitstellen.
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6567286Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6567446Z     3
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6567600Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6568846Z     Die Prinzessbohnen durch ein Sieb abgießen und abtropfen lassen. Knoblauchzehen schälen, der Länge nach in 3 - 4 Spalten schneiden und anschließend in feine Scheiben schneiden, evtl. noch ein wenig hacken. Mit etwas Öl beträufeln und bereitstellen. Zwiebeln würfeln und bereitstellen. Bauchspeck ebenfalls fein würfeln und bereitstellen. Chili und Paprika in feine Streifen und Rädchen schneiden.
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6569928Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6570085Z     4
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6570241Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6570907Z     In drei Pfannen separat das Olivenöl für das Fleisch, Butterschmalz für die Kartoffeln und die Bauchspeckwürfel für die Bohnen erhitzen. Die Butter erst später dazugeben, wenn der Speck ausgelassen ist.
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6571498Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6571649Z     5
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6571798Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6572537Z     Die Steaks oder Filets bei nicht so starker Hitze sanft anbraten, 3 Min pro Seite. Sahne angießen und Paprikastreifen und Chiliringe dazugeben. Den grünen Pfeffer dazugeben. Bei schwacher Hitze köcheln lassen und 1 - 2-Mal wenden.
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6573192Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6573341Z     6
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6573497Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6574117Z     Dem ausgelassenen Speck die Butter zugeben. Die Hälfte der Zwiebelwürfel sowie die Prinzessbohnen und den geschnittenen Knoblauch hinzufügen und langsam bei nicht zu starker Hitze braten.
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6574793Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6574946Z     7
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6575099Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6575786Z     In der Pfanne mit Butterschmalz die Kartoffelspalten braten. Öfters wenden oder schwenken. Nach der halben Garzeit den Rest der Zwiebel hinzugeben und mit dem Rosmarin bestreuen. Weiter braten und öfters wenden.
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6576397Z     
2026-04-24T08:59:48.6576549Z     8

Die Zubereitung gleicht der von Rinderfilet oder Rumpsteak, da Straußenfleisch ähnlich zart und mild ist, jedoch deutlich fettärmer.\n\n**Tipp:** Am besten mit einem Kerntemperatur-Thermometer arbeiten, um die magere Struktur des Fleisches zu schonen.\n\n**Quelle zu Kerntemperaturen exotischer Steaks (inkl. 2026-04-24T09:00:58.9989226Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9989443Z     ## **What is leg of lamb?**
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9989735Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9991215Z     Bone-in lamb leg is a large and impressive cut that comes from the back haunches of the animal, and it usually weighs anywhere from 5 to 7 pounds (a larger leg will serve up to 12 people). A [boneless leg of lamb](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/greek-boneless-leg-of-lamb-slow-cooker/) will weigh about 2 pounds less.
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9992587Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9994441Z     Lamb leg is usually sold without the shank. If you’d like to buy one with the shank attached (as I have in this recipe), ask for “shank-on leg” or “American leg.” A shank-on leg is beautiful for presentation, but that’s about it. So, if you’d rather have the shank removed, feel free to go with that! You will not compromise on flavor.
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9995899Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9996155Z     ## **Expert tips for buying lamb** leg
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9996475Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9996917Z     Lamb leg is available in most grocery stores at the meat counter. Here are some tips for buying lamb:
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9997407Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9998070Z     *   **Buy from** **a reputable butcher**. This is not a cheap cut of meat necessarily, so buy from a place that you can trust, and ask all the questions you need to.
2026-04-24T09:00:58.9999401Z     *   **Look for market weight.** Avoid buying baby lamb or mutton, which is older and tougher meat.  
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0001127Z     *   **For less “gamy” taste ask the butcher to trim the fat.** They will trim the thick outer layer of fat, which typically covers this cut of lamb and tends to make the meat taste strong and gamy. Keep in mind that a little bit of fat insulates the meat and keeps it nice and tender.
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0002347Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0003038Z     ![Leg of lamb with potatoes](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Mediterranean-Style-Leg-of-Lamb-Recipe-with-Potatoes-19.jpg)
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0003798Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0004018Z     ## **Best way to cook lamb leg**
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0004495Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0006652Z     Whether [boneless](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/kleftiko-greek-lamb-in-parchment/) or bone-in, the perfect leg of lamb should be either oven-roasted until blushing pink on the inside (with an internal temp around 130°F) or roasted long and slow for several hours until the meat is very tender and falls apart to internal temp around 175°F (I often use the [slow cooker](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/greek-boneless-leg-of-lamb-slow-cooker/) for that).  
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0008693Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0009708Z     In this recipe, I go for perfectly pink meat. A good **meat thermomete**r will take the guesswork out of the equation and help you get perfectly pink lamb every time – no gray, rubbery meat here!
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0010648Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0012247Z     Remember that the roasted meat will rest for some time (at least 15 to 20 minutes or so) before serving. During this time, the meat will continue to cook, and its internal temperature will rise. That’s why it’s not a bad idea to take the lamb out of the oven just a tad before it reaches your desired doneness.
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0013523Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0013768Z     ## Cooking time for leg of lamb
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0014072Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0016605Z     How long the lamb will take in the oven depends on how you like it. For a medium cooked lamb, which is perfectly pink on the inside, budget about 25 minutes per pound or until the internal temp registers somewhere around 130°F (about 1 1/2 to 2 hours in a 325 degrees F-heated oven). And for falling apart lamb, budget about 40 to 45 minutes per pound or until the meat’s internal temp registers 175°F (this can take 5 to 6 hours in in a 325 degrees F-heated oven).
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0018509Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0018791Z     ## Internal temp guide for cooked leg of lamb
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0019135Z     
2026-04-24T09:00:59.0021167Z     Always keep in mind, as mentioned earlier, the meat will rest for a bit after you take it out of the oven, and its internal temperature will rise as it sits. To me lamb leg is best when cooked to perfectly pink on the inside (internal temp somewhere around 130°F) or when cooked long hours until falling apart tender (internal temp somewhere around 175°F). 2026-04-24T09:01:10.8610128Z

2026-04-24T09:01:10.8615598Z     *   **Prepare the seasoning or rub.** This rub is best prepared in a food processor. Run the processor until the rub is smooth. Set aside for now.

2026-04-24T09:01:10.9733094Z     
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9734366Z     If you’re still on the fence about lamb, try this recipe. I swear you’ll be a convert as soon as you finish your first chop. I like to choose a cut from the center (rib) section, which features long rib bones with meat at the end.
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9735378Z

2026-04-24T09:01:10.9748760Z     ## Tips and Tricks
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9749015Z     
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9750081Z     *   Undercooked lamb chops are too chewy, and overcooked chops tend to be tough and dry. For rib chops, aim to cook them to an internal temperature of 130℉ (55℃). For loin chops, anywhere from 135-140℉ (57-60℃) is ideal.
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9751758Z     *   Adjust your grilling time to your preferred doneness of the meat. And don’t forget to let the meat rest for 10 minutes before serving it so the juices can distribute evenly.
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9753263Z     *   Rib chops are already tender, so your marinade is all for flavor. If you can, marinate your chops overnight, but an hour will work fine if you’re pressed for time.
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9755582Z     *   Goat meat is popular in Africa and the Caribbean. Please don’t repeat my mistake by using lamb and goat interchangeably. The distinct flavor profiles mean that different spices work best for each. For example, there’s nothing like a good [goat curry](https://www.africanbites.com/instant-pot-curry-goat/).
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9756900Z     
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9757148Z     ## Make-Ahead and Storage Instructions
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9757469Z     
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9758888Z     Prepare your herbs and spices and marinate the lamb ahead of time (the day before), but grill when you’re ready to eat! It only takes a few minutes for these bad boys to reach grilled perfection. The sauce is a great make-ahead and reheats well the next day.
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9760068Z     
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9762075Z     Heat leftover lamb in the microwave, oven, or air fryer. Another great way to use leftovers is a clean-the-fridge stew or soup. Save all those veggies that need to be used today by chopping them, tossing them in a pot, adding broth and seasonings, and simmering until they’re tender. Chop up the leftover lamb, add it to the stew, heat it through, and enjoy. Save the bones for a delicious bone broth.
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9763687Z     
2026-04-24T09:01:10.9763932Z     ## What Pairs With Grilled Lamb Chops

I almost always pair them with a side of potatoes, too. [Fondant potatoes](https://www.africanbites.com/fondant-potatoes/) or [garlic mashed potatoes](https://www.africanbites.com/garlic-mashed-red-potatoes/) are family faves around here.
