2026-04-24T09:02:42.3419305Z     14. ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3419491Z     15. 
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3419867Z     16. The lamb shank is the lower portion of the leg — the section below the knee joint on either
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3420342Z     17. the fore (front) or hind (back) leg. It contains a central bone surrounded by a dense network
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3420828Z     18. of collagen-rich connective tissue, which transforms into gelatin during prolonged moist-heat
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3421289Z     19. cooking and gives braised lamb shank its characteristic sticky, unctuous sauce and
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3421752Z     20. fall-off-the-bone texture. The shank has relatively little intramuscular fat compared with
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3422245Z     21. the shoulder but abundant surface and inter-muscular fat that melts into the braising liquid.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3422724Z     22. Because of the high collagen content, the shank is uniquely unsuited to dry-heat methods
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3423393Z     23. — it must reach 85–95 °C internal temperature and be held there for 2–4 hours for collagen
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3424041Z     24. conversion to complete. Any reading below 80 °C will leave the meat tough regardless of cooking
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3424740Z     25. time. This makes the MEATER probe essential for monitoring approach to the braising window;
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3425225Z     26. the pull temperature is the **minimum** at which the meat has rendered sufficiently, not a
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3425714Z     27. precision target. The shank is universal across Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, Northern European,
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3426209Z     28. Central Asian, and Indian culinary traditions. Braising liquid varies from red or white wine
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3426795Z     29. (European), beer (Belgian/Scandinavian), cider (Norman/Swedish), saffron-tomato (Persian/Moroccan),
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3427495Z     30. to spiced yoghurt or lentil-thickened broth (Indian/Turkish).
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3427900Z     31. 
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3428105Z     32. ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3428297Z     33. 
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3428592Z     34. ### 1. BBC Good Food — Braised Lamb Shanks (British tradition)
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3428946Z     35. **Source**: BBC Good Food, *bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/braised-lamb-shanks*
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3429289Z     36. (fetched 2026-04-24, author: Good Food magazine)
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3429662Z     37. **Serves**: 8 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Rest**: 5 min
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3429925Z     38. 
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3430090Z     39. **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3430292Z     40. - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3430489Z     41. - 2 tbsp olive oil
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3430696Z     42. - 1 onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3431064Z     43. - 2 carrots, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3431301Z     44. - Few sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3431523Z     45. - 3 fresh bay leaves
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3431738Z     46. - 4 garlic cloves, left whole
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3431960Z     47. - 2 tbsp plain flour
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3432212Z     48. - 1 tbsp tomato purée
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3432418Z     49. - 350 ml white wine
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3432624Z     50. - 500 ml lamb or chicken stock
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3432838Z     51. 
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3433000Z     52. **Method**
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3433361Z     53. 1. Heat oven to 200 °C / fan 180 °C / gas 6. Pour the oil into a casserole dish or roasting
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3433827Z     54.    tray large enough to fit all the shanks. Over high heat on the hob, spend a good 10 minutes
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3434317Z     55.    browning the lamb all over.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3434672Z     56. 2. Remove the lamb, add the onion and carrot and cook for 10 minutes until starting to brown.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3435130Z     57.    Stir in the herbs and garlic and cook for a few minutes more. Stir in the flour and tomato
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3435675Z     58.    purée, season well, then pour over the wine and stock.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3435993Z     59. 3. Return the lamb shanks to the pan. Bring to a simmer.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3436376Z     60. 4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part of the shank, keeping the tip
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3436722Z     61.    away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3437119Z     62. 5. Cover with a lid or tightly with foil and cook in the oven, undisturbed, for 1½–2 hrs until
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3437455Z     63.    lamb is tender.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3437764Z     64. 6. Remove the lamb from the sauce and set aside. Put the pan back on the hob and bubble the
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3438163Z     65.    sauce for a few minutes until thickened to a gravy consistency.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3438615Z     66. 7. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F)** — at this temperature the collagen has converted to
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3439121Z     67.    gelatin and the meat separates easily from the bone. The window is wide; holding at 90 °C
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3439631Z     68.    for 30 minutes produces more tender results than hitting 90 °C and immediately pulling.
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3440134Z     69. 8. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (braised — collagen fully rendered,
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3440471Z     70.    meat falling from bone).
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3440678Z     71. 
2026-04-24T09:02:42.3440842Z     72. ---

2026-04-24T09:02:46.7181870Z     220. - Olive oil
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7182164Z     221. - A splash balsamic vinegar (Balsamico)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7182569Z     222. - 1 bunch flat-leaf parsley (glatte Petersilie)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7182939Z     223. - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7183228Z     224. - Thyme, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7183542Z     225. - Rosemary, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7183933Z     226. - Cayenne pepper or Pul Biber (Turkish chilli flakes)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7184488Z     227. - 1 knife-tip ground cinnamon (Zimt)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7184945Z     228. - 1 knife-tip ground cumin (Kreuzkümmel)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7185281Z     229. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7185493Z     230. **Method**
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7185811Z     231. 1. The day before: trim the shanks of excess fat and sinews. Rub all over with thyme, rosemary,
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7186371Z     232.    olive oil, salt and pepper. Refrigerate overnight — the dry-rub penetrates the meat and
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7186761Z     233.    improves both flavour and crust formation.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7187132Z     234. 2. The next day: brown the shanks all over in olive oil in a heavy braising pot. Remove and
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7187664Z     235.    set aside. Dice the onion, garlic and carrots; sauté in the same pot until beginning to
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7188255Z     236.    colour. Stir in the tomato paste and cook briefly.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7188663Z     237. 3. Deglaze with red wine and reduce slightly. Add the sieved tomatoes and half the lamb stock.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7189228Z     238.    Season with a knife-tip each of cumin and cinnamon — these must be barely perceptible; if
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7189824Z     239.    they taste of spice the sauce is over-spiced.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7190517Z     240. 4. Return the shanks to the pot, add a lid and braise over low heat for approximately 2½ hours.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7191133Z     241.    Add more lamb stock if needed during cooking.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7191637Z     242. 5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7192200Z     243. 6. After cooking, remove the shanks and rest on a plate. Taste the sauce; adjust with cayenne
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7192882Z     244.    or Pul Biber for heat, and a splash of balsamic vinegar to round. No additional thickening
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7193456Z     245.    is needed — the reduced braising liquid is naturally concentrated.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7193961Z     246. 7. Stir in freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley before serving.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7194579Z     247. 8. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The German tradition uses no additional binding — the
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7195051Z     248.    collagen in the shanks provides sufficient body. The author notes that serving with couscous
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7195440Z     249.    and salad is traditional in his household.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7195856Z     250. 9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (fully braised, German style).
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7196178Z     251. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7196343Z     252. ---
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7196508Z     253. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7196862Z     254. ### 6. Tasteline.com — Ugnsstekt lammlägg / Oven-braised Lamb Shank (Swedish tradition)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7197373Z     255. **Source**: *Det moderna köket* (The Modern Kitchen), recipe published on Tasteline.com;
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7197840Z     256. tasteline.com/recept/ugnsstekt-lammlagg/ (fetched 2026-04-24; 3.3/5 stars, 300 votes)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7198306Z     257. **Serves**: 7 · **Prep**: 15 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7198578Z     258. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7198745Z     259. **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7198980Z     260. - 7 lamb shanks (lammlägg)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7199261Z     261. - 750 ml (7.5 dl) meat stock (köttbuljong)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7199552Z     262. - 250 ml (2.5 dl) red wine (rött vin)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7199790Z     263. - 6 bay leaves (lagerblad)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7200046Z     264. - 4 garlic cloves (vitlöksklyftor)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7200310Z     265. - 8 small onions (smålökar)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7200537Z     266. - 2 sprigs rosemary (rosmarin)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7200811Z     267. - 3 sprigs marjoram (mejram)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7201045Z     268. - 1 tbsp peppercorns (pepparkorn)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7201265Z     269. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7201437Z     270. **Method**
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7201760Z     271. 1. Set a frying pan on high heat. Add the lamb shanks and sear for approximately 2 minutes per
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7202289Z     272.    side, or until well browned. No oil is specified — the shanks render their own fat in a hot
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7202626Z     273.    dry pan.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7202937Z     274. 2. Transfer the browned shanks to a roasting tin. Add the stock, wine, bay leaves, garlic,
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7203356Z     275.    small whole onions, rosemary, marjoram and peppercorns to the tin.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7203786Z     276. 3. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the largest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7204502Z     277. 4. Cover the tin tightly with foil and oven-braise at 150 °C for approximately 2 hours, or
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7204932Z     278.    until the meat is very tender (mycket mört).
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7205459Z     279. 5. **Pull at 88–90 °C (190–194 °F).** Swedish home-cooking tradition aims for complete
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7206020Z     280.    tenderness rather than a specific pull temperature; the 2-hour window at 150 °C is designed
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7206397Z     281.    to achieve this reliably.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7206740Z     282. 6. Serve the shanks in deep bowls with polenta or potato-and-garlic mash (potatis- och
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7207103Z     283.    vitlöksmos).
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7207476Z     284. 7. Final serving temperature: **88–90 °C / 190–194 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian home
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7207874Z     285.    cooking style, from *Det moderna köket*).
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7208108Z     286. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7208273Z     287. ---
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7208440Z     288. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7208611Z     289. ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7208817Z     290. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7209011Z     291. | Source | Pull temperature | Method |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7209257Z     292. |--------|----------------|--------|
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7209627Z     293. | BBC Good Food (UK) | 88–92 °C | Oven braise, white wine, 1½–2 hrs at 200 °C |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7210156Z     294. | Nigella Lawson (UK) | 90–95 °C | Stovetop simmer, aromatic broth |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7210591Z     295. | The Mediterranean Dish (Egypt/US) | 88–93 °C | Dutch oven, 2½ hrs at 175 °C |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7211061Z     296. | Matprat.no (Norway) | 88–92 °C | Foil-covered form, white wine, 3½–4 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7211526Z     297. | Chefkoch.de / Kasimir (Germany) | 88–93 °C | Covered braise, red wine, 2½ hrs |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7212080Z     298. | Tasteline.com / Det moderna köket (Sweden) | 88–90 °C | Covered roasting tin, red wine + stock, 2 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7212461Z     299. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7212787Z     300. **Consensus pull**: **88–95 °C (190–203 °F)**. The braise window is wide — collagen begins
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7213293Z     301. converting to gelatin at around 72–74 °C but full texture transformation requires sustained
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7213812Z     302. time above 80 °C. All six traditions agree on a minimum of approximately 88 °C. Higher
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7214511Z     303. temperatures (up to 95 °C) are acceptable and produce softer, more yielding meat. Overshooting
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7214997Z     304. to 100 °C risks drying out the outer muscle fibres in longer braises.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7215283Z     305. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7215622Z     306. **Representative coding value**: **90 °C (194 °F)** as the nominal pull, with a practical
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7215989Z     307. range of 88–95 °C.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7216182Z     308. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7216354Z     309. ## Notes for cooking_data.py
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7216565Z     310. 
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7216738Z     311. - `doneness`: `braised_tender`
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7217017Z     312. - `pull_temp_c`: 90
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7217339Z     313. - `pull_temp_range_c`: [88, 95]
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7217792Z     314. - `safety_level`: `safe` (well above all pathogen destruction thresholds)
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7218423Z     315. - Collagen conversion is time-dependent as well as temperature-dependent. The MEATER probe
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7219200Z     316.   temperature reading marks entry into the braising window; actual tenderness depends on
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7219746Z     317.   the duration at that temperature. Recommend a minimum hold time of 30–45 minutes after
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7220116Z     318.   reaching 88 °C.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7220440Z     319. - All six traditions sear the shanks before braising for Maillard flavour development.
2026-04-24T09:02:46.7220909Z     320. - All six use covered cooking vessels (lid, foil) to maintain steam and prevent evaporation.

2026-04-24T09:06:00.9317604Z     # Lamb Shank × Braise — Recipe Temperature Research
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9317981Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9318226Z     ## Tree position
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9318487Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9318708Z     | Field | Value |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9319005Z     |-------|-------|
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9319643Z     | Category | `lamb` |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9319938Z     | Cut type folder | `roasts/` |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9320260Z     | Cut slug | `lamb_shank` |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9320561Z     | Method slug | `braise` |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9320928Z     | File | `lamb_shank-braise.md` |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9321316Z     | Full path | `docs/recipe_research/lamb/roasts/lamb_shank-braise.md` |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9321613Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9321780Z     ## Cut profile
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9321958Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9322329Z     The lamb shank is the lower portion of the leg — the section below the knee joint on either
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9322807Z     the fore (front) or hind (back) leg. It contains a central bone surrounded by a dense network
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9323275Z     of collagen-rich connective tissue, which transforms into gelatin during prolonged moist-heat
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9323746Z     cooking and gives braised lamb shank its characteristic sticky, unctuous sauce and
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9324361Z     fall-off-the-bone texture. The shank has relatively little intramuscular fat compared with
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9324832Z     the shoulder but abundant surface and inter-muscular fat that melts into the braising liquid.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9325299Z     Because of the high collagen content, the shank is uniquely unsuited to dry-heat methods
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9325854Z     — it must reach 85–95 °C internal temperature and be held there for 2–4 hours for collagen
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9326391Z     conversion to complete. Any reading below 80 °C will leave the meat tough regardless of cooking
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9326871Z     time. This makes the MEATER probe essential for monitoring approach to the braising window;
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9327353Z     the pull temperature is the **minimum** at which the meat has rendered sufficiently, not a
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9327833Z     precision target. The shank is universal across Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, Northern European,
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9328332Z     Central Asian, and Indian culinary traditions. Braising liquid varies from red or white wine
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9328825Z     (European), beer (Belgian/Scandinavian), cider (Norman/Swedish), saffron-tomato (Persian/Moroccan),
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9329250Z     to spiced yoghurt or lentil-thickened broth (Indian/Turkish).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9329516Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9329683Z     ## Source recipes
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9329867Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9330131Z     ### 1. BBC Good Food — Braised Lamb Shanks (British tradition)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9330471Z     **Source**: BBC Good Food, *bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/braised-lamb-shanks*
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9330794Z     (fetched 2026-04-24, author: Good Food magazine)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9331161Z     **Serves**: 8 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Rest**: 5 min
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9331414Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9331572Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9331755Z     - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9332096Z     - 2 tbsp olive oil
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9332309Z     - 1 onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9332527Z     - 2 carrots, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9332746Z     - Few sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9332954Z     - 3 fresh bay leaves
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9333157Z     - 4 garlic cloves, left whole
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9333367Z     - 2 tbsp plain flour
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9333593Z     - 1 tbsp tomato purée
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9333790Z     - 350 ml white wine
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9333986Z     - 500 ml lamb or chicken stock
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9334327Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9334494Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9334843Z     1. Heat oven to 200 °C / fan 180 °C / gas 6. Pour the oil into a casserole dish or roasting
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9335289Z        tray large enough to fit all the shanks. Over high heat on the hob, spend a good 10 minutes
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9335640Z        browning the lamb all over.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9335985Z     2. Remove the lamb, add the onion and carrot and cook for 10 minutes until starting to brown.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9336552Z        Stir in the herbs and garlic and cook for a few minutes more. Stir in the flour and tomato
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9336982Z        purée, season well, then pour over the wine and stock.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9337290Z     3. Return the lamb shanks to the pan. Bring to a simmer.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9337672Z     4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part of the shank, keeping the tip
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9338020Z        away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9338415Z     5. Cover with a lid or tightly with foil and cook in the oven, undisturbed, for 1½–2 hrs until
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9338752Z        lamb is tender.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9339051Z     6. Remove the lamb from the sauce and set aside. Put the pan back on the hob and bubble the
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9339443Z        sauce for a few minutes until thickened to a gravy consistency.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9339886Z     7. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F)** — at this temperature the collagen has converted to
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9340395Z        gelatin and the meat separates easily from the bone. The window is wide; holding at 90 °C
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9340900Z        for 30 minutes produces more tender results than hitting 90 °C and immediately pulling.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9341394Z     8. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (braised — collagen fully rendered,
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9341722Z        meat falling from bone).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9341927Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9342087Z     ---
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9342246Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9342553Z     ### 2. Nigella Lawson — Aromatic Lamb Shank Stew (British / Middle Eastern fusion)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9342960Z     **Source**: Nigella Lawson, *Nigella Bites* (Chatto & Windus / Hyperion, 2001);
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9343346Z     nigella.com/recipes/aromatic-lamb-shank-stew (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9343713Z     **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 1½–2 hrs
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9343956Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9344123Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9344443Z     - 8 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9344617Z     - 2 onions
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9344794Z     - 4 cloves garlic
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9344989Z     - 1 sprinkling of salt
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9345187Z     - 1 tbsp turmeric
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9345369Z     - 1 tsp ground ginger
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9345605Z     - ¼ tsp dried chilli flakes
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9345814Z     - 2 tsp ground cinnamon
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9346049Z     - ¼ tsp freshly grated nutmeg
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9346261Z     - Black pepper
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9346438Z     - 3 tbsp honey
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9346612Z     - 1 tbsp soy sauce
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9346795Z     - 3 tbsp Marsala
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9347021Z     - 6 tbsp (⅓ cup) red lentils
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9347241Z     - 6 tbsp vegetable or groundnut oil
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9347520Z     - 3 tbsp chopped pistachios or blanched almonds, to serve
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9347776Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9348047Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9348364Z     1. Put 3 tablespoons of the oil into a very large, wide, heavy-bottomed pan and warm over medium
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9348829Z        heat. Brown the lamb shanks in batches and then remove to a roasting tin to rest temporarily.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9349289Z     2. Peel the onions and garlic and process in a food processor or chop finely by hand. Add the
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9349748Z        remaining oil to the pan, and fry the onion-garlic mush until soft, sprinkling salt over to
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9350206Z        stop it catching. Stir in the turmeric, ground ginger, chilli, cinnamon and nutmeg, and
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9350658Z        season with freshly ground pepper.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9351052Z     3. Stir again, adding the honey, soy sauce and Marsala.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9351604Z     4. Put the shanks back in the pan. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest part
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9352135Z        of the largest shank, tip clear of the bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9352889Z     5. Add cold water almost to cover. Bring to the boil, then put a lid on the pan, lower the heat
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9353666Z        and simmer very gently for 1–1½ hours or until the meat is tender.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9354383Z     6. Add the red lentils and cook for about 20 minutes longer without the lid, until the lentils
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9355131Z        have softened into the sauce and the juices have reduced and thickened slightly.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9355678Z     7. **Pull at 90–95 °C (194–203 °F).** The lentil-thickened sauce signals doneness as much as
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9356124Z        the temperature; at 90 °C the meat should pull freely from the bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9356498Z     8. Toast the nuts in a dry frying pan; sprinkle onto the lamb as you serve it.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9356956Z     9. Final serving temperature: **90–95 °C / 194–203 °F** (fully braised, fall-off-bone).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9357269Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9357431Z     ---
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9357600Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9357958Z     ### 3. Suzy Karadsheh — Mediterranean Braised Lamb Shanks (Mediterranean / Egyptian tradition)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9358361Z     **Source**: Suzy Karadsheh, *The Mediterranean Dish*;
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9358699Z     themediterraneandish.com/braised-lamb-shanks/ (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9359136Z     **Serves**: 6 · **Prep**: 20 min · **Cook**: 2½ hrs · **Oven**: 175 °C (350 °F)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9359414Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9359580Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9359765Z     - 6 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9359977Z     - 2¼ tsp garlic powder
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9360192Z     - 1 tsp sweet Spanish paprika
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9360407Z     - 1 tsp kosher salt
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9360616Z     - 1 tsp freshly ground black pepper
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9360876Z     - ¾ tsp ground nutmeg
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9361081Z     - 2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9361322Z     - 1 medium yellow onion, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9361573Z     - 2 celery ribs, roughly chopped
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9361844Z     - 3 large carrots, peeled and cut into large pieces
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9362102Z     - 450 g (1 lb) baby potatoes
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9362310Z     - 2 cups red wine
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9362499Z     - 3 cups beef broth
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9362701Z     - 1 can (800 g / 28 oz) peeled tomatoes
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9362924Z     - 2 cinnamon sticks
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9363115Z     - 4 sprigs fresh thyme
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9363327Z     - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9363525Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9363682Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9364034Z     1. Preheat oven to 175 °C (350 °F). Mix garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper and nutmeg. Pat
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9364590Z        the lamb shanks dry and season all over with the spice mix.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9364984Z     2. In a large Dutch oven over medium-high, heat 2 tbsp olive oil. Working in batches, brown
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9365555Z        the shanks until golden on all sides (about 8 minutes). Transfer to a large tray.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9366019Z     3. Pour off excess fat and return pot to medium-high heat. Add onions, celery, carrots and
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9366487Z        potatoes. Sauté until the vegetables gain some colour, 5–7 minutes.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9366899Z     4. Add red wine and scrape up the brown bits from the bottom. Cook briefly until wine reduces
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9367341Z        slightly, then add beef broth, peeled tomatoes, cinnamon sticks, thyme and rosemary.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9367777Z        Sprinkle salt and pepper. Return lamb shanks to the pot; press down to submerge. Boil
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9368121Z        10 minutes, then turn off the heat.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9368467Z     5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, keeping the tip away from the
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9368808Z        central bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9369190Z     6. Cover the pot and transfer to the hot oven for 2½ hours. Check periodically; add more liquid
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9369624Z        if needed.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9369967Z     7. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The cooking liquid will have reduced to about one-third
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9370394Z        of the original volume. The meat should pull away from the bone with light tong pressure.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9370822Z     8. Serve on Lebanese rice, polenta or orzo with the vegetables and braising sauce.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9371310Z     9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (Mediterranean braise, fall-off-bone).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9371635Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9371795Z     ---
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9371953Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9372340Z     ### 4. Matprat.no — Hvitvinbraiserte lammeskanker / White-Wine Braised Lamb Shanks (Norwegian tradition)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9372947Z     **Source**: Matprat.no (Norwegian Meat and Poultry Research Centre / Opplysningskontoret for egg og kjøtt);
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9373462Z     matprat.no/oppskrifter/gjester/hvitvinsbraiserte-lammeskanker/ (fetched 2026-04-24)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9373894Z     **Serves**: 4 · **Cook**: 3½–4 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9374130Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9374486Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9374679Z     - 4 lamb shanks
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9374851Z     - 1 tsp salt
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9375059Z     - ½ tsp pepper
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9375247Z     - 2 cloves garlic
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9375477Z     - 1 red or yellow capsicum (paprika), cut into sticks
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9375730Z     - 3 green chillies
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9375933Z     - 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9376203Z     - 2 tbsp rosé-pepper / pink peppercorns
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9376447Z     - 750 ml (7.5 dl) white wine
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9376677Z     - Salt and pepper (additional, to taste)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9376923Z     - 8 green asparagus spears (to serve)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9377155Z     - 4 sprigs fresh basil (to garnish)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9377369Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9377538Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9377797Z     1. Rub the lamb shanks with salt and pepper. Place in an ovenproof form.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9378207Z     2. Peel the garlic and cut the capsicum into sticks. Add the garlic, capsicum, chilli and
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9378705Z        rosemary to the form alongside the shanks. Sprinkle with rosé pepper and pour over the
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9379033Z        white wine.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9379383Z     3. Cover tightly with aluminium foil and cook in the oven at 150 °C for 3½–4 hours, or until
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9379729Z        the meat loosens from the bones.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9380062Z     4. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9380500Z     5. Optional: remove the foil for the last few minutes and raise oven to high grill (overvarme)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9380847Z        to add colour to the shanks.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9381299Z     6. Remove the capsicum and chilli pieces from the cooking juices. Strain the juices; return
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9381766Z        capsicum and chilli. Use a hand blender to create a full-bodied sauce. Season to taste.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9382274Z     7. Peel the asparagus; cook in lightly salted water for 3 minutes, until just tender.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9383005Z     8. **Pull at 88–92 °C (190–198 °F).** Norwegian culinary tradition cooks lamb shanks until
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9383743Z        they fall from the bone; the low oven temperature (150 °C) extends the collagen-conversion
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9384447Z        window and produces an exceptionally silky sauce.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9385004Z     9. Pour sauce into deep plates; place one lamb shank per plate. Garnish with basil and the
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9385541Z        lightly cooked green asparagus.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9385974Z     10. Final serving temperature: **88–92 °C / 190–198 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian style).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9386302Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9386462Z     ---
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9386621Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9387014Z     ### 5. Kasimir (Chefkoch.de) — Geschmorte Lammhaxen / Red-Wine Braised Lamb Shanks (German tradition)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9387778Z     **Source**: Kasimir, Chefkoch.de community recipe;
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9388229Z     chefkoch.de/rezepte/566811155129665/Geschmorte-Lammhaxen.html (fetched 2026-04-24;
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9388574Z     4.8/5 stars from 55 ratings)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9388925Z     **Serves**: 4 · **Prep**: 45 min (+overnight marination) · **Cook**: 2½ hrs
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9389201Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9389364Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9389560Z     - 4 lamb shanks (Lammhaxen)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9389799Z     - 2 carrots (Möhren)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9390034Z     - 1 large onion (Gemüsezwiebel)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9390264Z     - 1 garlic clove (Knoblauchzehe)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9390500Z     - Tomato paste (Tomatenmark), a little
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9390733Z     - 1 glass lamb stock (Lammfond)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9390977Z     - 200 g sieved tomatoes (passierte Tomaten)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9391219Z     - 250 ml red wine (Rotwein)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9391418Z     - Olive oil
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9391608Z     - A splash balsamic vinegar (Balsamico)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9391862Z     - 1 bunch flat-leaf parsley (glatte Petersilie)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9392116Z     - Salt and pepper
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9392312Z     - Thyme, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9392528Z     - Rosemary, dried or fresh
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9392779Z     - Cayenne pepper or Pul Biber (Turkish chilli flakes)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9393050Z     - 1 knife-tip ground cinnamon (Zimt)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9393329Z     - 1 knife-tip ground cumin (Kreuzkümmel)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9393548Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9393706Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9394004Z     1. The day before: trim the shanks of excess fat and sinews. Rub all over with thyme, rosemary,
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9394770Z        olive oil, salt and pepper. Refrigerate overnight — the dry-rub penetrates the meat and
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9395155Z        improves both flavour and crust formation.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9395517Z     2. The next day: brown the shanks all over in olive oil in a heavy braising pot. Remove and
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9396018Z        set aside. Dice the onion, garlic and carrots; sauté in the same pot until beginning to
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9396389Z        colour. Stir in the tomato paste and cook briefly.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9396775Z     3. Deglaze with red wine and reduce slightly. Add the sieved tomatoes and half the lamb stock.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9397289Z        Season with a knife-tip each of cumin and cinnamon — these must be barely perceptible; if
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9397660Z        they taste of spice the sauce is over-spiced.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9398079Z     4. Return the shanks to the pot, add a lid and braise over low heat for approximately 2½ hours.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9398449Z        Add more lamb stock if needed during cooking.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9398961Z     5. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the thickest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9399440Z     6. After cooking, remove the shanks and rest on a plate. Taste the sauce; adjust with cayenne
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9399889Z        or Pul Biber for heat, and a splash of balsamic vinegar to round. No additional thickening
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9400329Z        is needed — the reduced braising liquid is naturally concentrated.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9400672Z     7. Stir in freshly chopped flat-leaf parsley before serving.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9401092Z     8. **Pull at 88–93 °C (190–200 °F).** The German tradition uses no additional binding — the
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9401535Z        collagen in the shanks provides sufficient body. The author notes that serving with couscous
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9401910Z        and salad is traditional in his household.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9402303Z     9. Final serving temperature: **88–93 °C / 190–200 °F** (fully braised, German style).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9402613Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9402786Z     ---
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9403044Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9403372Z     ### 6. Tasteline.com — Ugnsstekt lammlägg / Oven-braised Lamb Shank (Swedish tradition)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9403862Z     **Source**: *Det moderna köket* (The Modern Kitchen), recipe published on Tasteline.com;
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9404544Z     tasteline.com/recept/ugnsstekt-lammlagg/ (fetched 2026-04-24; 3.3/5 stars, 300 votes)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9404996Z     **Serves**: 7 · **Prep**: 15 min · **Cook**: 2 hrs · **Oven**: 150 °C
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9405258Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9405419Z     **Ingredients**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9405638Z     - 7 lamb shanks (lammlägg)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9405906Z     - 750 ml (7.5 dl) meat stock (köttbuljong)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9406178Z     - 250 ml (2.5 dl) red wine (rött vin)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9406399Z     - 6 bay leaves (lagerblad)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9406642Z     - 4 garlic cloves (vitlöksklyftor)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9406894Z     - 8 small onions (smålökar)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9407129Z     - 2 sprigs rosemary (rosmarin)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9407362Z     - 3 sprigs marjoram (mejram)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9407586Z     - 1 tbsp peppercorns (pepparkorn)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9407798Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9407955Z     **Method**
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9408249Z     1. Set a frying pan on high heat. Add the lamb shanks and sear for approximately 2 minutes per
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9408761Z        side, or until well browned. No oil is specified — the shanks render their own fat in a hot
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9409086Z        dry pan.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9409379Z     2. Transfer the browned shanks to a roasting tin. Add the stock, wine, bay leaves, garlic,
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9409783Z        small whole onions, rosemary, marjoram and peppercorns to the tin.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9410195Z     3. Insert the MEATER probe lengthways into the largest shank, tip away from the central bone.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9410711Z     4. Cover the tin tightly with foil and oven-braise at 150 °C for approximately 2 hours, or
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9411111Z        until the meat is very tender (mycket mört).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9411664Z     5. **Pull at 88–90 °C (190–194 °F).** Swedish home-cooking tradition aims for complete
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9412428Z        tenderness rather than a specific pull temperature; the 2-hour window at 150 °C is designed
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9412963Z        to achieve this reliably.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9413436Z     6. Serve the shanks in deep bowls with polenta or potato-and-garlic mash (potatis- och
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9413980Z        vitlöksmos).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9414700Z     7. Final serving temperature: **88–90 °C / 190–194 °F** (fully braised, Scandinavian home
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9415299Z        cooking style, from *Det moderna köket*).
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9415599Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9415760Z     ---
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9415974Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9416198Z     ## Temperature consensus
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9416479Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9416724Z     | Source | Pull temperature | Method |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9417212Z     |--------|----------------|--------|
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9417584Z     | BBC Good Food (UK) | 88–92 °C | Oven braise, white wine, 1½–2 hrs at 200 °C |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9417988Z     | Nigella Lawson (UK) | 90–95 °C | Stovetop simmer, aromatic broth |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9418408Z     | The Mediterranean Dish (Egypt/US) | 88–93 °C | Dutch oven, 2½ hrs at 175 °C |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9418867Z     | Matprat.no (Norway) | 88–92 °C | Foil-covered form, white wine, 3½–4 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9419327Z     | Chefkoch.de / Kasimir (Germany) | 88–93 °C | Covered braise, red wine, 2½ hrs |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9419864Z     | Tasteline.com / Det moderna köket (Sweden) | 88–90 °C | Covered roasting tin, red wine + stock, 2 hrs at 150 °C |
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9420241Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9420557Z     **Consensus pull**: **88–95 °C (190–203 °F)**. The braise window is wide — collagen begins
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9421067Z     converting to gelatin at around 72–74 °C but full texture transformation requires sustained
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9421665Z     time above 80 °C. All six traditions agree on a minimum of approximately 88 °C. Higher
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9422179Z     temperatures (up to 95 °C) are acceptable and produce softer, more yielding meat. Overshooting
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9422636Z     to 100 °C risks drying out the outer muscle fibres in longer braises.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9422911Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9423238Z     **Representative coding value**: **90 °C (194 °F)** as the nominal pull, with a practical
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9423582Z     range of 88–95 °C.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9423767Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9423937Z     ## Notes for cooking_data.py
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9424142Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9424464Z     - `doneness`: `braised_tender`
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9424685Z     - `pull_temp_c`: 90
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9424890Z     - `pull_temp_range_c`: [88, 95]
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9425195Z     - `safety_level`: `safe` (well above all pathogen destruction thresholds)
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9425637Z     - Collagen conversion is time-dependent as well as temperature-dependent. The MEATER probe
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9426111Z       temperature reading marks entry into the braising window; actual tenderness depends on
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9426615Z       the duration at that temperature. Recommend a minimum hold time of 30–45 minutes after
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9426962Z       reaching 88 °C.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9427263Z     - All six traditions sear the shanks before braising for Maillard flavour development.
2026-04-24T09:06:00.9427707Z     - All six use covered cooking vessels (lid, foil) to maintain steam and prevent evaporation.

2026-04-24T09:06:22.1769587Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1769786Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1770008Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1770525Z         Make the gravy by simmering all the ingredients together until the redcurrant jelly has completely melted. Keep warm.
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1771094Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1771289Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1771505Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1771959Z         Steam or simmer the potatoes until just tender, about 8 mins, then drain well and add a few drops of oil.
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1772473Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1772681Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1772899Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.1774886Z         Lay the venison on a board. Sprinkle some of the black pepper and a little salt on each side, pressing the pepper into the steaks. Heat the remaining oil in a pan. When it has a shimmering surface, add the steaks, 2 at a time. Cook for 2 mins on each side for rare, 3 mins for medium and 4 mins for well done. When cooked, return them all to the pan and pour over the sauce. Warm for 1 min, sprinkle with parsley. Serve with the potatoes and peas, if you like, and any extra sauce spooned over.

2026-04-24T09:06:22.3851317Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3851543Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3851762Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3852004Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3853351Z         Heat the oil in a [frying pan](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/content/top-five-non-stick-frying-pans), cook the venison for 5 mins, then turn over and cook for 3-5 mins more, depending on how rare you like it and the thickness of the meat (cook for 5-6 mins on each side for well done). Lift the meat from the pan and set aside to rest.
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3855026Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3855247Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3855481Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3856632Z         Add the balsamic vinegar to the pan, then pour in the stock, redcurrant jelly and garlic. Stir over quite a high heat to blend everything together, then add the blackberries and carry on cooking until they soften. Serve with the venison, celeriac mash (see below) and broccoli.

2026-04-24T09:06:22.3859093Z

2026-04-24T09:06:22.3860431Z     ###### HEALTHY BENEFITS
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3860695Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:22.3862260Z     Venison, an excellent source of protein, is low in calories. It also supplies an easily absorbed form of iron, so is ideal for pregnant and menstruating women. Venison contains good levels of the energising B vitamins – a standard portion provides 60 per cent of our daily B12 requirement.

2026-04-24T09:06:32.7263856Z     *   Stuffed saddle of venison with prunes & brioche
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7264562Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7264830Z     [Skip to ingredients](#ingredients-list)
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7265146Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7265362Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7265599Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7265823Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7266058Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7267316Z         Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. To make the stuffing, melt the butter in a shallow pan, then fry the onion, garlic, prunes and bacon until the onion is translucent and the bacon is slightly coloured. Add the brioche and cook until combined and broken down, then add the sage, season and remove from heat.
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7268672Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7268898Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7269144Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7271677Z         Put a sheet of baking parchment on a sheet of foil (both should be larger than your venison). Arrange four pieces of string across the kitchen counter and put the foil and parchment on top – this will help the venison to keep its shape. Lay another four pieces of string across the parchment. Lay the pancetta on top of the paper and string, slightly overlapping the slices so that they cover an area roughly the size of your opened-out venison.
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7273655Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7273891Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7274139Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7275006Z         Lay the venison on top of the pancetta, skin-side down, then spoon the stuffing down the centre, patting it with your hands to make it into a sausage shape.
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7275795Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7276019Z     *   ### step 4
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7276260Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7278214Z         Fold in the sides of the venison, along with the pancetta, so they touch or slightly overlap – use the foil and baking parchment to help you. Tie the strings around the venison, making sure that the foil and parchment enclose the meat but stay outside it. Fold in the ends and tie the other pieces of string tightly around the outside of the parcel to hold it in shape.
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7279764Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7279988Z     *   ### step 5
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7280230Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7281699Z         Put the venison on a baking sheet or in a roasting tin. Roast for 40-50 mins or until a meat thermometer pushed into the joint reaches 60C, for medium rare meat. Rest for 10 mins, then remove and discard the foil and parchment. Brush the venison all over with butter and return to the oven for a final 10 mins. Leave to rest for 10-15 mins before removing the string and slicing.
2026-04-24T09:06:32.7283264Z

2026-04-24T09:06:46.4042025Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4042191Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4042344Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4042509Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4044440Z         Soak the shallots in boiling water for 5 mins, drain, then peel – this makes it a lot easier to remove the skins. While they are soaking, slice the rest of the vegetables. Heat a large, wide flameproof casserole dish, then add the lardons. Fry for 5 mins or until the fat has run from the meat, then tip in the vegetables and cook for 10 mins until golden and starting to soften. Tip into a bowl.
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4045484Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4045648Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4045817Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4046718Z         Toss the rabbit meat with the flour, then tap off the excess. Heat half the butter and oil in the dish, then brown half the rabbit for 10 mins until golden all over. Add a good splash of water, scrape around the dish and tip the juices into a jug. Repeat with the second batch of rabbit, but use the wine to deglaze the pan this time, letting it reduce by about a third.
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4047649Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4047805Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4047971Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4049680Z         Add the rabbit and the vegetables to the wine, pour in the stock, stir in the herbs and bring to a simmer. Cover, leaving just a small gap for steam to escape, then simmer for 1½-2 hrs or until the meat falls away from the bones. If you like a thicker sauce to your stew, you can lift out the meat and vegetables and boil the sauce to thicken it a little. To finish, heat the knob of butter in a frying pan and cook the mushrooms with salt, pepper and a few thyme leaves over a high heat until browned. Spoon these over the stew, sprinkle with thyme leaves and bring to the table.
2026-04-24T09:06:46.4051196Z

2026-04-24T09:06:46.5139764Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5139929Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5140085Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5140273Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5140745Z         Ask your butcher to joint the rabbit, cutting the legs into two pieces, the saddle into three and the shoulders and front into two. Heat oven to 220C/200C fan/gas 7.
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5141239Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5141401Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5141573Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5142380Z         Put the rabbit in a large [roasting tin](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/review/test-five-best-roasting-tins), season and add 1 tbsp of the thyme leaves. Add the oil and toss until the rabbit is well coated. Roast for 20 mins.
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5143317Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5143479Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5143646Z         
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5144156Z         Add the onions or shallots and the garlic, then stir well and roast for another 15 mins, stirring again halfway through. Sprinkle over the remaining thyme before serving.
2026-04-24T09:06:46.5144856Z

2026-04-24T09:06:56.3410453Z     ## Why Grill Rib Chops?
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3410659Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3411535Z     If you’re still on the fence about lamb, try this recipe. I swear you’ll be a convert as soon as you finish your first chop. I like to choose a cut from the center (rib) section, which features long rib bones with meat at the end.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3412473Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3413913Z     Grilling cooks at high heat giving you that beautiful Maillard reaction and the smoky flavor beautifully complements the lamb’s gamey taste. Ribs may not be the meatiest of all chops, but they make a lovely visual presentation with a smooth, tender flavor – something you’d pay a hefty price for at a restaurant.

2026-04-24T09:06:56.3418665Z

2026-04-24T09:06:56.3420602Z

2026-04-24T09:06:56.3455791Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3456006Z     ## Tips and Tricks
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3456250Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3457306Z     *   Undercooked lamb chops are too chewy, and overcooked chops tend to be tough and dry. For rib chops, aim to cook them to an internal temperature of 130℉ (55℃). For loin chops, anywhere from 135-140℉ (57-60℃) is ideal.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3458854Z     *   Adjust your grilling time to your preferred doneness of the meat. And don’t forget to let the meat rest for 10 minutes before serving it so the juices can distribute evenly.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3460288Z     *   Rib chops are already tender, so your marinade is all for flavor. If you can, marinate your chops overnight, but an hour will work fine if you’re pressed for time.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3462374Z     *   Goat meat is popular in Africa and the Caribbean. Please don’t repeat my mistake by using lamb and goat interchangeably. The distinct flavor profiles mean that different spices work best for each. For example, there’s nothing like a good [goat curry](https://www.africanbites.com/instant-pot-curry-goat/).
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3463755Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3464000Z     ## Make-Ahead and Storage Instructions
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3464433Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3465673Z     Prepare your herbs and spices and marinate the lamb ahead of time (the day before), but grill when you’re ready to eat! It only takes a few minutes for these bad boys to reach grilled perfection. The sauce is a great make-ahead and reheats well the next day.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3466776Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3468867Z     Heat leftover lamb in the microwave, oven, or air fryer. Another great way to use leftovers is a clean-the-fridge stew or soup. Save all those veggies that need to be used today by chopping them, tossing them in a pot, adding broth and seasonings, and simmering until they’re tender. Chop up the leftover lamb, add it to the stew, heat it through, and enjoy. Save the bones for a delicious bone broth.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3470542Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3470776Z     ## What Pairs With Grilled Lamb Chops
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3471076Z     
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3472930Z     I love pairing lamb with fresh salads on the hearty side, such as a [three-bean salad](https://www.africanbites.com/three-bean-salad/) or a refreshing [Greek salad](https://www.africanbites.com/greek-salad/). I almost always pair them with a side of potatoes, too. [Fondant potatoes](https://www.africanbites.com/fondant-potatoes/) or [garlic mashed potatoes](https://www.africanbites.com/garlic-mashed-red-potatoes/) are family faves around here.
2026-04-24T09:06:56.3474974Z

2026-04-24T09:07:16.4783640Z     ## Method
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4783863Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4784084Z     *   ### step 1
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4784542Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4785828Z         Marinate the goat in a roasting tin the day before you cook it. Blitz all the marinade ingredients in a blender with 1 tsp salt, slather all over the goat, cover with cling film and put in the fridge.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4786720Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4786946Z     *   ### step 2
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4787184Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4788866Z         The next day, heat oven to 160C/ 140C fan/gas 3. Remove the goat from the fridge 30 mins before you cook it to bring it to room temperature. Unwrap, then cover the roasting tin tightly with foil and cook for 1 hr, then add the stock, return to the oven and cook for a further 4 hrs, occasionally basting with the juices, until the meat is coming away from the bones and is easily pulled apart with two forks.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4790452Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4790688Z     *   ### step 3
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4790933Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4792656Z         Pull the meat from the bones (which can be discarded) and set aside on a plate. Remove most of the fat, then pour a little boiling water in the bottom of the tin and use a whisk to scrape up any of the dark crust, incorporating the juices with the water. Return the meat to the [roasting tin](http://www.bbcgoodfood.com/review/test-five-best-roasting-tins) and cover with foil. Keep warm in a low oven until ready to use.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4794109Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4794474Z     *   ### step 4
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4794723Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4795671Z         To make the pickled pomegranate seeds, combine the cider vinegar with the sugar and a pinch of salt in a bowl, stirring until they dissolve. Add the pomegranate seeds, red onion and chilli, and stir to coat.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4796695Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4796937Z     *   ### step 5
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4797110Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4797642Z         To make the coriander dressing, combine all the ingredients in a food processor with some seasoning and blend until you have a runny green dressing.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4798127Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4798296Z     *   ### step 6
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4798470Z         
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4799120Z         Warm the flatbreads through on a skillet or [griddle](https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/review/best-griddle-pans) and serve topped with the cabbage and warm goat. Garnish with the pickled pomegranate mix, mint leaves and dressing.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.4799776Z

2026-04-24T09:07:16.6211352Z     **Asun** (spicy roast goat) is delectable and spicy, delivering bite-sized pieces with big, bold aromatic flavors from onions, habanero, garlic, and bell peppers. Smokey, sizzling, spicy! Definitely not for the faint-hearted. Native to the Yoruba-speaking Ondo people in Western Nigeria, it’s a must-try recipe.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6212241Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6212759Z     ![Serving up a platter of asun spicy roast goat for a special occasion with fried plantains.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-1.jpg)

2026-04-24T09:07:16.6214930Z     Goat meat isn’t as popular here in the U.S., but in most parts of the world, especially West Africa and the Caribbean, it’s always a crowd favorite during parties, special occasions, and Christmas. We just got a whole goat from a local farm in preparation for a family celebration. And guess what? I’ve been cooking up a storm.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6215818Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6217054Z     I look forward to seeing my loved ones licking their plates and sweating all over their faces. Oh yes, this dish is definitely not for the faint-hearted. The authentic Asun spicy roasted goat recipe calls for smoking a whole goat in an open pit with skin on. After slow roasting it to perfection, you cut it into smaller pieces and sautee with lots of onions and peppers to spice things up.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6218083Z

2026-04-24T09:07:16.6219434Z     ## How to Make Asun at Home
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6219702Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6220965Z     The smoking process isn’t so easy in a standard home kitchen, so I’ve created an easier version. If you can’t get hold of traditionally smoked goat meat, use what you can get. It tastes just as good as the smoked version, especially if you add smoked paprika!
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6222075Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6222690Z     To tone down the heat, replace some of the hot peppers with red and green bell peppers. A little sweetness from the red bell pepper goes way further. It’s a must-try!
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6223361Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6223948Z     Serve this aromatic, sizzling asun simply with oven-baked or fried plantains. You’ll be singing for joy! And if you’re doing this for the holidays, Merry Christmas!!!!
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6224574Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6224754Z     ## How to Make Asun Spicy Roast Goat
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6224984Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6225483Z     ![Boil the meat, then broil it for extra flavor. Reverse sears are coming back in style.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-1-4.jpg)
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6226007Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6226540Z     1.  **Boil** the smoked goat meat with salt, pepper, bouillon powder, onion, and as little water as possible in a medium pot until tender. (I used a pressure cooker to speed up the process.) _(Photos 1-3)_
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6227128Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6227595Z     ![Finish broiling, saute aromatics, add the meat back in, and add the spice.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-5-6.jpg)
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6228088Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6228626Z     1.  **Broil** – Remove the goat meat, place it on a lined baking tray, and broil it for about 10 minutes, rotating every few minutes until browned. _(Photos 4-5)_
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6229688Z     2.  **Sauté** the onions, garlic, and ginger in a bit of oil for about a minute. Then add the tomatoes, white pepper, paprika, green onions, curry powder, scotch bonnet pepper, and bouillon. Continue cooking for 5-7 minutes. _(Photo 6)_
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6230635Z     3.  **Add** roasted goat and bell pepper, stir for about 3 minutes, adjust the seasonings to your preferences, and serve hot. _(Photos 7-8)_
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6231293Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6231764Z     ![A skillet full of spicy smoked goat meat called asun in some parts of Africa.](https://www.africanbites.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Asun-Spicy-Roast-Goat-3.jpg)
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6232270Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6232436Z     ## Tips and Notes
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6232622Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6233041Z     *   If you can’t find smoked goat meat (which is usually frozen), you can make this with fresh, with or without the skin.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6233779Z     *   If you wish to grill the meat in an open fire, you’ll need to steam it first if it’s a female, since they tend to be tougher. There’s no need to steam a buck.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6234418Z     *   When broiling the meat, you may want to grease the tray lightly.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6234796Z     *   A food processor or blender saves time when chopping the spices into bits.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6235266Z     *   When adding the hot peppers to the meat, make sure that they only wilt a little to create authentic, fresh heat.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6235638Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6235935Z     ## Make-Ahead and Storage
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6236144Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6236576Z     You can make this the day before, keep it in the fridge, and reheat just before serving. You could also freeze it, then thaw it overnight in the refrigerator.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6237102Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6237815Z     While I don’t usually have leftovers, they do make me happy. Loading up the freezer in serving-size packages lets me create a special meal on a dull day.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.6238289Z

2026-04-24T09:07:16.6239146Z

This lamb recipe comes with meltingly delicious potatoes for the perfect meal in one pan, and if you’re looking for some color and crunch, add a big [Greek salad](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/traditional-greek-salad-recipe/) (more ideas below).

2026-04-24T09:07:16.8001946Z     ## **My go-to lamb roast**
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8002209Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8005162Z     For some, [beef tenderloin](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/beef-tenderloin-roast-recipe/), roast [turkey](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/roast-turkey-breast-recipe/), or even ham may be the celebratory roast of choice. But where I come from, nothing beats a tender, oven-roasted leg of lamb for all occasions, but particularly for [Easter](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/16-mediterranean-easter-recipes/). Not only is it delicious, but it makes a dramatic centerpiece for a special dinner, especially served over gold potatoes.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8007241Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8007999Z     If you have not cooked lamb leg in the oven before, it is easier than most people think! With a simple technique and my bold, garlicky lamb seasoning, you will have the perfect lamb roast every time.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8008806Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8009448Z     I’m excited to share my family’s go-to roast lamb recipe, but first some basics and no-fail tips for beginners.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8009967Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8010182Z     ## **What is leg of lamb?**
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8010445Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8011715Z     Bone-in lamb leg is a large and impressive cut that comes from the back haunches of the animal, and it usually weighs anywhere from 5 to 7 pounds (a larger leg will serve up to 12 people). A [boneless leg of lamb](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/greek-boneless-leg-of-lamb-slow-cooker/) will weigh about 2 pounds less.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8013175Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8015043Z     Lamb leg is usually sold without the shank. If you’d like to buy one with the shank attached (as I have in this recipe), ask for “shank-on leg” or “American leg.” A shank-on leg is beautiful for presentation, but that’s about it. So, if you’d rather have the shank removed, feel free to go with that! You will not compromise on flavor.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8016731Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8016992Z     ## **Expert tips for buying lamb** leg
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8017322Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8017794Z     Lamb leg is available in most grocery stores at the meat counter. Here are some tips for buying lamb:
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8018348Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8019027Z     *   **Buy from** **a reputable butcher**. This is not a cheap cut of meat necessarily, so buy from a place that you can trust, and ask all the questions you need to.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8020158Z     *   **Look for market weight.** Avoid buying baby lamb or mutton, which is older and tougher meat.  
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8021843Z     *   **For less “gamy” taste ask the butcher to trim the fat.** They will trim the thick outer layer of fat, which typically covers this cut of lamb and tends to make the meat taste strong and gamy. Keep in mind that a little bit of fat insulates the meat and keeps it nice and tender.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8023046Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8023819Z     ![Leg of lamb with potatoes](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Mediterranean-Style-Leg-of-Lamb-Recipe-with-Potatoes-19.jpg)
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8024839Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8025079Z     ## **Best way to cook lamb leg**
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8025367Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8027616Z     Whether [boneless](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/kleftiko-greek-lamb-in-parchment/) or bone-in, the perfect leg of lamb should be either oven-roasted until blushing pink on the inside (with an internal temp around 130°F) or roasted long and slow for several hours until the meat is very tender and falls apart to internal temp around 175°F (I often use the [slow cooker](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/greek-boneless-leg-of-lamb-slow-cooker/) for that).  
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8029641Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8030697Z     In this recipe, I go for perfectly pink meat. A good **meat thermomete**r will take the guesswork out of the equation and help you get perfectly pink lamb every time – no gray, rubbery meat here!
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8031597Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8033108Z     Remember that the roasted meat will rest for some time (at least 15 to 20 minutes or so) before serving. During this time, the meat will continue to cook, and its internal temperature will rise. That’s why it’s not a bad idea to take the lamb out of the oven just a tad before it reaches your desired doneness.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8034716Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8034965Z     ## Cooking time for leg of lamb
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8035279Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8037731Z     How long the lamb will take in the oven depends on how you like it. For a medium cooked lamb, which is perfectly pink on the inside, budget about 25 minutes per pound or until the internal temp registers somewhere around 130°F (about 1 1/2 to 2 hours in a 325 degrees F-heated oven). And for falling apart lamb, budget about 40 to 45 minutes per pound or until the meat’s internal temp registers 175°F (this can take 5 to 6 hours in in a 325 degrees F-heated oven).
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8039592Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8039869Z     ## Internal temp guide for cooked leg of lamb
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8040221Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8041974Z     Always keep in mind, as mentioned earlier, the meat will rest for a bit after you take it out of the oven, and its internal temperature will rise as it sits. To me lamb leg is best when cooked to perfectly pink on the inside (internal temp somewhere around 130°F) or when cooked long hours until falling apart tender (internal temp somewhere around 175°F). Here is a quick table:
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8043417Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8043991Z     *   **Rare:** About 15 minutes per pound or until internal temperature registers 125 degrees F.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8045388Z     *   **Medium-Rare:** About 20 minutes per pound or until internal temperature registers 130°F to 135°F. About 1 1/2 to 2 hours or so.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8046407Z     *   **Medium:** About 25 minutes per pound or until internal temperature registers 135°F to 140°F.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8047296Z     *   **Well-Done:** About 30 minutes per pound or until internal temperature registers 155°F to 165°F.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8048390Z     *   **Falling-Apart Tender Lamb:** About 40 minutes per pound or until the internal temp registers 175°F. This can take about 6 hours or so.
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8049024Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8049739Z     ![Leg of lamb with roasted potatoes](https://www.themediterraneandish.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Mediterranean-Style-Leg-of-Lamb-Recipe-with-Potatoes-18.jpg)
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8050525Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8050800Z     ## **Should I marinate lamb leg before cooking?**
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8051144Z     
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8052040Z     You do not need to marinate lamb leg for long hours if you don’t have the time. But first, make sure you season the lamb well with kosher salt and black pepper. And for best flavor, give the meat
2026-04-24T09:07:16.8052824Z

2026-04-24T09:15:21.3910908Z Method:
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3911188Z 1. Place a flat dish in the oven and preheat to 80°C.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3911527Z 2. Heat oil in a heavy pan, sear steaks hard on both sides. Transfer to the dish.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3912082Z 3. Sweat Salicornes, onion, and garlic in the pan until coloured. Spread on top of steaks. Steaks need approx. 30 min in the oven (medium/rare).

2026-04-24T09:15:21.3914880Z Method:

2026-04-24T09:15:21.3920553Z ### VENISON STEAK SOURCES
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3920677Z 
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3920846Z **Source A: BBC Good Food — Venison steak with Port sauce**
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3921190Z URL: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/venison-steak-port-sauce
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3921574Z Serves: 4 · Recipe from Good Food magazine, October 2010
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3922305Z Ingredients: 750g small potatoes (halved/quartered), 2 tbsp olive oil, 4 venison steaks, 1 tbsp cracked black pepper, chopped parsley to serve. For sauce: zest 1 orange (in strips) + its juice, 6 tbsp redcurrant jelly, 4 tbsp port, 1 cinnamon stick
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3922974Z Method:
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3923289Z 1. Make the gravy by simmering all sauce ingredients together until redcurrant jelly has melted. Keep warm.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3923815Z 2. Steam or simmer potatoes until just tender, about 8 mins, then drain and add a few drops of oil.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3925025Z 3. Lay venison on a board. Sprinkle black pepper and a little salt on each side, pressing pepper into the steaks. Heat remaining oil in a pan. When shimmering, add steaks 2 at a time. Cook 2 min per side for rare, 3 min for medium, 4 min for well done. Return all to pan, pour over sauce, warm 1 min, sprinkle with parsley. Serve with potatoes.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3925938Z 
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3926139Z **Source B: BBC Good Food — Pan-fried venison with blackberry sauce**
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3926528Z URL: https://www.bbcgoodfood.com/recipes/pan-fried-venison-blackberry-sauce
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3926936Z Serves: 2 · Recipe from Good Food magazine, November 2007
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3927636Z Ingredients: 1 tbsp olive oil, 2 thick venison steaks or 4 medallions, 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar, 150ml beef stock (made with 2 tsp Knorr Touch of Taste), 2 tbsp redcurrant jelly, 1 garlic clove (crushed), 85g fresh or frozen blackberries
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3928275Z Method:
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3929047Z 1. Heat oil in a frying pan, cook venison 5 min on first side, then turn and cook 3–5 min more (depending on how rare you like it; 5–6 min each side for well done). Lift meat from pan and set aside to rest.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3929946Z 2. Add balsamic vinegar to pan, pour in stock, redcurrant jelly and garlic. Stir over high heat to blend, then add blackberries and cook until they soften. Serve with the venison.

2026-04-24T09:15:21.3931761Z Subtitle: "Auf die einfache Art, ohne Backofen" (Simple way, without oven)
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3932217Z Ingredients: 1 venison steak (roe deer) approx. 1.5cm thick, 1 tbsp sunflower oil, 1 tbsp butter, salt and pepper
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3932594Z Method:
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3932887Z 1. Briefly rinse meat cold, pat dry, leave at room temperature for about 1 hour before frying.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3933731Z 2. In a pan, heat sunflower oil with butter until it stops foaming. Fry venison steak on each side for just under 2 minutes. Fold a large piece of aluminium foil double and wrap the steak inside. Rest on the counter for 5 minutes, then season with pepper and salt.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3934998Z 3. Optional: deglaze pan with a little red wine or bouillon and bind with ice-cold butter or Mondamin (starch). Note: "This method is safe and quick for any beginner who does not want to over-cook their venison steak."

2026-04-24T09:15:21.3976733Z Method:

2026-04-24T09:15:21.3978563Z 2. Toss rabbit with flour, tap off excess. Heat half butter+oil in dish, brown half the rabbit 10 min until golden all over. Add splash of water, scrape dish, tip juices into a jug. Repeat with second batch, deglazing with wine (reduce by about a third).
2026-04-24T09:15:21.3980049Z 3. Add rabbit and vegetables to wine, pour in stock, stir in herbs, bring to a simmer. Cover (leaving small gap for steam), simmer 1.5–2 hrs until meat falls from bones. 2026-04-24T09:15:21.4011757Z 6. Fry potato wedges in clarified butter, turning often. Halfway through, add remaining onion and sprinkle with rosemary.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4012271Z 7. Season everything lightly with salt and pepper. Plate in groups of three and serve.

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4029355Z Ingredients: 1.5kg mutton from the shoulder, 4 medium onions (quartered), 1 handful fresh thyme, 2 garlic cloves (crushed), 100ml olive oil, ¾ bottle semi-sweet red wine
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4029858Z Method:
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4030350Z 1. Cut meat into gulasch-sized pieces. Heat approx. 50ml olive oil in a large pot on high heat and fry meat all over. Meanwhile preheat oven to 200°C.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4031189Z 2. Add quartered onions, crushed garlic and thyme, reduce temperature slightly. Continue frying until juices have released (approx. 30 min).
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4032227Z 3. Transfer everything to a baking dish. Season with salt and pepper. Add remaining olive oil and pour over the wine. Seal dish tightly with aluminium foil, pierce 3–4 times with a sharp knife. Place in oven and braise for 2 hours.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4033359Z 4. After approx. 1.5 hrs, reduce temperature to 150°C and check occasionally. Serve the braising juices as sauce (or thicken with sauce binder). Add liquid if needed. Serve with sweet potato and green beans.

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4057368Z Path: /home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/bison/bison_steak-pan_sear.md

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4058204Z Sources: Sources 1, 2, 3 (3 Chefkoch bison recipes). 
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4059967Z Cut profile: Bison (Bison bison) entrecôte/ribeye is a lean, iron-rich muscle from the rib section. Bison carries ~40% less fat than beef, making it extremely easy to overcook. The 80°C oven method used by all three Chefkoch authors gives gentle, even heat after the initial sear, preventing the outer layers from drying before the centre reaches temperature. Because bison is so lean, probe placement must be central to avoid reading the hotter surface. Similar to wagyu beef in that it rewards a short, hard sear + gentle finish.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4061461Z Pull temp: 60°C (medium-rare), range 57–63°C.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4061997Z Notes for coding: doneness=medium_rare, pull_temp_c=60, pull_temp_range_c=[57,63], safety_level=safe_with_rest

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4062539Z ### FILE 2: venison/venison_steak-pan_sear.md
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4063176Z Path: /home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/venison/venison_steak-pan_sear.md
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4063953Z Tree: Category=game, folder=venison/, cut_slug=venison_steak, method=pan_sear
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4064727Z Sources: Source A (BBC GF port sauce), Source B (BBC GF blackberry sauce), Source C (Chefkoch Rehsteak rosa)
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4067958Z Cut profile: Venison steak most commonly refers to a haunch (leg) steak from roe deer (Reh) or red deer (Hirsch). Venison is exceptionally lean — leaner even than bison — with a deep crimson colour and a mineral, gamey flavour that is accentuated by overcooking. The optimal texture is achieved at medium-rare (55–57°C), where the centre is still pink but the connective tissue of the outer muscle has relaxed. The short cooking time (2–5 min per side) means probe placement is critical: insert parallel to the surface and lengthways into the thickest part to get a true centre reading. Unlike beef, venison does not benefit from extended aging past ~7 days; fresh venison should be treated quickly at high heat.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4070356Z Pull temp: 57°C (medium-rare), range 54–60°C.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4070744Z Notes: doneness=medium_rare, pull_temp_c=57, pull_temp_range_c=[54,60], safety_level=safe_with_rest
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4071027Z

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4077396Z ### FILE 4: venison/venison_loin-pan_sear.md
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4077847Z Path: /home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/venison/venison_loin-pan_sear.md
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4078364Z Tree: Category=game, folder=venison/, cut_slug=venison_loin, method=pan_sear
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4078819Z Sources: Same sources as venison_steak (A, B, C) but note that loin/backstrap/tenderloin is even more delicate.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4081206Z Cut profile: Venison loin refers to the backstrap (longissimus dorsi) running along the spine, or the tenderloin (psoas minor) which lies inside the pelvic cavity. Both are the most prized cuts on a deer carcass. The loin is ultra-lean, tender without connective tissue, and has virtually zero fat marbling. It cooks faster than a haunch steak of equivalent thickness — a 3cm loin medallion needs only 90 seconds per side on a very hot pan. The loin is particularly unforgiving: past 60°C it begins to toughen quickly. Probe insertion for a loin medallion: insert horizontally through the side, keeping the tip dead-centre. For a whole backstrap roll: insert lengthways from the thick end.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4083127Z Pull temp: 55°C (medium-rare), range 52–57°C (slightly lower than haunch steak due to delicacy).
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4083600Z Notes: doneness=medium_rare, pull_temp_c=55, pull_temp_range_c=[52,57], safety_level=safe_with_rest
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4084149Z 
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4084533Z ### FILE 5: venison/venison_roast-oven_roast.md
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4085247Z Path: /home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/venison/venison_roast-oven_roast.md

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4086583Z Sources: Source E (BBC GF stuffed saddle — pull 60°C for medium rare). Since only 1 source is available for a whole roast, note this.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4089043Z Cut profile: Venison roast — typically the saddle (both loins on the bone) or haunch (bone-in leg) — is the showpiece cut for formal game dinners. The saddle is extremely tender and lean; the haunch contains more connective tissue but both reward medium-rare service. Pancetta or bacon wrapping (as in the BBC GF recipe) provides basting fat that the lean venison lacks. The stuffing in the BBC recipe adds moisture and acts as insulation to slow cooking, meaning the probe temperature is the definitive doneness guide. Rest time is as important as cook time for a large roast: 10–15 min allows the outer crust to cool slightly and redistribution of juices. Probe must be inserted in the deepest part of the meat mass, away from bone and stuffing.
2026-04-24T09:15:21.4091717Z Pull temp: 60°C, range 57–63°C.

2026-04-24T09:15:21.4092930Z Path: /home/runner/work/meater-in-local-haos/meater-in-local-haos/docs/recipe_research/game/wild_boar/wild_boar_chop-grill.md
